tracks.desc: One of thee best songs made on Audiotool and the remixes for this song were absolutely phenomenal! This encouraged me to use more piano in my tracks. It just adds to the caliber of complexity. I can't get over it. It is the epitome of what great artists should be.

tracks.name: Surface
tracks.desc: Jake Tyler OH MY GOSH!!! His music is always amazing. It's full of feeling and emotion which is always key when producing music. This song specifically I listen to when I am angry or like when I need to get something done. He makes the most beautiful music, filled with passion which is clearly seen in all his work.

tracks.name: The Knot at the Rope's End
tracks.desc: I mean come on, it's Inavon. I like reflecting my styles off of his in general but this song is one of my all time favorites by him. This song reminded me of the 80's trance and I love that era of music. Me and my Dad listen to this a bunch when we drive.

tracks.name: Dirty Resurrection
tracks.desc: French synths, equal to angle voices. Quite Lovely. This group only has two songs but they are awesome and to me they are easy to listen to and definitely give off that french like tone.

tracks.name: Stare at the Sun
tracks.desc: Uprising is amazing and has always been so supportive and nice to me. He is a great person and an amazing musician. When I first started audiotool, I thought he was the coolest person and wanted to be as known as he was. This song is cool and full of layers which I love incorporating in my music.

tracks.name: At The End
tracks.track_artist: E I D Ø L O N
tracks.desc: This influenced me through it's genre. I love the presets and sound incorporated into this song. EIDØ LON makes awesome stuff and I wish they were still creating works because they are fantastic. Everything blends perfectly and always makes my day.

tracks.name: It's My Time To Shine (dedicated to 21st Century)
tracks.desc: Where do I start, this is full of dimension, different sounds that complete each other and blend in their own mysterious way. I keep this as a ringtone for my phone.

tracks.name: Control
tracks.desc: The pads and the pure innocence of this track and of all Gravidon's music makes me so happy. There is not one single track by them that I have ever disapproved of. I enjoy the mix of feelings provoked by his music.

tracks.name: I'm Not A Fan
tracks.desc: I like Zone, super cool and chill music. This music helped me realize that a song can have diversity and can be constructed with many different genres within it.

tracks.name: Audiotool Day 2014-CallyKay
tracks.desc: Favorite own track - a collab with Inavon for Audiotool Day 2014.

intro: He himself claims to have no experience in music production and not a trace of talent. But with his odd audio collages and a goodly portion of self-irony he has achieved the status of an Audiotool cult figure. From the earliest days of the website Andrea Karl Fontana, aka Fonti aka "King of Audiotool" has always left us wondering what he will come up with next. Hats off for this original personality.
about:
Fontana, Karl Andrea - aka Fonti
Place: Chur, Switzerland
Age: 61 (otherwise sweet 16)
Profession: Worker Teatcher

interview.a: PIZZA ... normally I (re)mix everything together, add some samples, a little bit of Tonematrix, some Rasselbock, Tube and Crusher and it ends on … PIZZA
Oh my gosh ... there is a lot. I listen to the radio and watch TV.
Next year (hopefully)
In 2007 I was looking for some Flash web sites and found Andre Michelle's lab with the Tonematrix. I made some Tonematrix live session tracks and I fell in love with audiotool. That's it.

Audiotool is a gift, a wonderful parcel. Free, without adverts, no time-limit, comprehensive, accessible for everyone. The fact that Audiotool works with this amount of data is initself a miracle for me. Ok, I can't upload complete audio tracks, of course the libary of other DAWs is larger and possibly, but only possibly, certain functions more elaborate. I like the tools, the modular system with the synth on whitenoise and more than anything the cabling and usability. And then we can publish our works in the community. And last but not least we can invite people to collaborate on a track, and learn from others. Where else is that possible?

The bit of spam is negligible. If it gets too much I can mute the troublemaker. But I only used this facility once: the community is just great.

If help is needed, you get it immediately. Or you can look at the video tutorials on Youtube.

For this and much more to the Aduiotool crew and the community a big

THANK YOU!

tracks.name: fontana - it socks (Farcio's Malandrone Mix)
tracks.desc: I like Cripta's (Farcio's) tracks from the very beginning. He is a brilliant musician, composer, very professional … Techno or not Techno ... with a bright sense of humor.

tracks.name: Testees (Original Mix)
tracks.desc: Awesome track - Astrum - what a talented Audiotool artist! Stunning.

tracks.name: Winter sky
tracks.desc: Beautiful music by sbronz. I close my eyes, hear your music, smell the wind and feel the sun … like at home. This music leaves me with a sense of empowerment: I miss sbronz.

tracks.name: Papa was an Audiotooler he had the Blues
tracks.desc: Funk, Jazz and Audiotool – That's South Bronx Den. His tracks are unique, every one of them. And all are recognizable as his very special personal style.

tracks.name: My Industrial
tracks.desc: Intense industrial track by White Grizzly. Fantastic and deep are his sounds. What a trip in White Grizzly's world.

tracks.name: summer base 1
tracks.desc: Japan + music + gaming = pot … He deserves much, much more support. Great artist, very talented and so underrated … shame on you!

tracks.name: minutes of your time
tracks.desc: audioarchitekt: 'lay down, put on your headphones, turn up to a suiting volume and let me take a few minutes of your time...' I do

tracks.name: amaterasu / wren
tracks.desc: It's time, soul and music, own samples and Audiotool … emotional tracks by dronealpha.

tracks.name: tijuana
tracks.desc: Excellent Chill track with great sounds of the southern-vibe of Tijuana.

tracks.name: A Clown and a Realist
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. You should check his music. He deserves much more attention.

tracks.name: OLA - OLA ... Audiotool
tracks.desc: My own favorite track.

intro: Bluedude stands for: powerful, funky EDM Sounds and has done so since the early days of Audiotool. All his tracks go like hotcakes and it's hardly surprising to find two of his productions among the top rated tracks of 2014. We are glad to have him back on board after a longish creative break.
about:
Jedediah Caro
Place: Oxnard, CA, USA
Age: 17
School: Adolfo Camarillo High School. Go Scorps!

interview.a: I like to cover any genre that passes up in front of me and one thing that's apparent is that I try to make danceable/close-to-mainstream tracks. My style was somehow called "Bluelectro" by some listeners. Also I take from Reversal's style of adding exciters to almost everything. Savant is one of my main outside inspirations because of the 8-bit melodies he makes. Also, anime OST's inspire me as well. In short, fast saw-wave melody + gritty bass + 4x4 drums + Blue = my style.

I learned drums when I lived in Indonesia in 2004-2006 (so when I was about 7 years old), but I forgot how to play them now. I learned piano in 2006, the year I immigrated to the USA. Since then, I listened to classical music until I had a piano teacher that taught me how to play piano for a year. I still play piano but I can't read notes anymore, so I learn and play by ear. I had no experience nor knew anything about electronically made music until I discovered Audiotool.

Fall of 2011....or 2012. I don't remember very well but I know it was a few weeks after my birthday and around the same time Uprising joined. By the end of 2010 I started listening to electronic music. Soon I wanted to make my own music and an anonymous user from some random website that I can't remember suggested Audiotool. Once I checked it out, there was no way I wouldn't make music. "Bluedude" was a joke name. I just chose it because I liked the color blue and I am a dude. Now the Audiotool people remember me by it. Bahahaha!

Audiotool is such a blessing. It's probably (if not) the BEST free DAW (Digital Audio Workstation) out there. Actually, it is the best DAW in my opinion because of the amount of great tracks able to be produced and the amazing community. Seriously. The community is a family...albeit a strangely lovable family. To my friends who heard some of my tracks, thanks for listening. For the growing Audiotoolers, you will get a lot better but it takes so much practice and patience. I'm sure you already know what I mean or you will know soon. Thanks Audiotool for featuring me as an artist! I never would have thought that I would get featured because a lot of people said that I would, but it never came. I learned that making music isn't about attention, it's about what YOU want to make when YOU want it. It's about passing time doing something you love. It's about bacon...ear bacon (because ear candy is too mainstream). Audiotool should be a hobby, not a job. Also, stay happy and stay fresh! Peace!

tracks.name: Hybrid
tracks.desc: This was probably the first track that inspired me the most on Audiotool. Back when I first started Audiotool, trance was my favorite genre and this has a magnificent melody! (Also Infyuthsion and Reversal make one of the best collabs)

tracks.name: Delete You
tracks.desc: Another collab track with a good melody. Through this, Astrum and Reversal showed me that hard-hitting tracks can be made on Audiotool. This helped me realize the music style I love the most, too!

tracks.name: Drawing In A Bar
tracks.desc: Frigolito is just insanely talented! His work leaves me in awe all the time. This particular track by him is one of my favorites because as fast paced as it is, it taught me that Audiotool isn't limited to sounds that sound like they are electronically made.

tracks.name: Jam and Toast
tracks.desc: Wow if you're feeling Saturday morning fun, this is a great track! I love the melodies in it and the blend of styles in it. You can also hear some 'Archetech' parts in it, which used to be an Audiotool genre that inspired me a lot.

tracks.name: Cadmium Hotel
tracks.desc: Definitely my favorite Techno on this site. It blends the Neurofunk styles of the wonderful CGman and the underrated technohead KaTaNa. The bass is so great!

tracks.name: kambuchka
tracks.desc: It was extremely hard to pick any one of noraus's tracks, because in my opinion, they are all worthy of notice. The fullness and rich sounds in this blows my mind away. I chose this because I imagine songs in movies and this would go great with a mystery or action movie in its more suspenseful parts.

tracks.name: Summertide
tracks.desc: Reversal has always been my favorite Audiotooler. I look up to him because he has a real life passion for music and medicine just like me. Plus his music is amazing!! This was the first track that I've ever downloaded on Audiotool.

tracks.name: Make Me Feel
tracks.desc: Phenom is so underrated that a colony of ants can go over his tracks with ease. I fell in love with this track when I first listened to it mainly because it's my favorite style of Nu-Disco. This track taught me that sampling on Audiotool can be a very beautiful thing.

tracks.name: Guiding Moonlight
tracks.desc: Inavon was my greatest trance inspiration. This track has two things that I absolutely LOVE to hear in Audiotool tracks: piano and formant filter choir pads. Choir pads!! Truly a gem.

tracks.name: Iwi Biwi Back
tracks.desc: Amazing depth and superb vocals!

tracks.name: Cosmos (Feat. Luxior)
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. Kavil is too underrated in my opinion, even at 350-400 followers right now. His style has developed so well into a stylish, pulsing, relaxing, and melodic tone. Since my favorite aspect of music is melody, he earns my spotlight for underrated artist that deserves more attention.

tracks.name: Audiotool Day 2014 - Bluedude
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. In my opinion, this is my best track at the moment because I'm striving to create more high quality tracks. Plus, this encapsulates my style pretty well.

intro: Khoi is obviously a very talented guy. Not only that he is a very good musician and constantly taking Audiotool to the next level, he also is a very good programmer. Having Khoi in the team would be a real enrichment for Audiotool. In fact sometimes we think he is already working with us. We hope some day, we can hire him officially. Keep it up, Khoi!
about:
Nguyễn Trần Minh Khôi
Place: Ho Chi Minh City / Saigon, Vietnam
Age: 16
School: Asian International School

interview.a: I'm not so sure. I like to experiment with many genres. I tend to make more progressive house than any other genre, I think. Chiptune is one of my favorites too, but I can never figure to finish a chiptune track :/

I started piano lessons when I was 4 and stopped several months ago.

I really hated learning piano at first, it was forced, and not something I was willing to learn (now it's different hehe). Currently, my mom is teaching me the traditional Zither. She played it when she was little but stopped and just has recently started playing it again.

Bonus fact: I would never have known about electronic music if there wasn't David Guetta :P
When I found out about Audiotool! No where earlier than that!
Vsauce on YouTube! One of his videos of D.O.N.G featured the Tone Matrix. After going around the links for a while, I ended up in Audiotool. I quit at first and came back after a long while.

You gotta make more; you'll never know when you got better! Seriously! When I first started making music, my sound design sucked! Thank you so much to people who've helped me so much and commenting on all my tracks! Thank you, bitch2.0, Kooscha, Getout, None, Theorist, humasigno, Marodeur, xavrockbeats, Luxior, Jambam, opaqity, yaffee! and so many more! (Thanks ΛΛRON for helping me with the CS:GO ;)

Cheers guys & gals!

tracks.name: Radiator
tracks.desc: One of my favorite grooves from Sandstrum! A great collab. I have left a comment every month, when I listened to that track.

tracks.name: Palladium Feast
tracks.desc: Nothing can beat the bass in this! CGMan and Flying Baby Seal made some bad-ass head-banging tunes!

tracks.name: Off to Tokyo
tracks.desc: VAPEROUS just does things. And they're done well. Now with this new name, I think VAPEROUS is moving onto a new direction. So exciting!

tracks.name: 17nm
tracks.desc: Noraus's sound design is irrefutably amazing. I can't say more! Hope he'll do more music :)

tracks.name: Megaman 3 (Bitstepper Mix)
tracks.desc: One of the first tracks I listened to on Audiotool! I also found out about looks (Luxior) through this track. Infyuthsion did an amazing remix on this one.

tracks.name: Office Bossa Nova
tracks.desc: I really like Frigolito's music. Lots of Jazz elements. This one is Bossa Nova - very relaxing!

tracks.name: Heat
tracks.desc: This is one of the best Hip Hop tracks on Audiotool! Always keeps me grooving.

tracks.name: feat. Fauko - Beautiful reality
tracks.desc: I just love this one from Sandburgen / Cripta and Fauko. Great lyrics and the music blends so well with the emotions.

tracks.name: Tagadà
tracks.desc: Cripta doesn't always make Synthpop, but when he does, it blows my mind!

tracks.name: In no Sight
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. His music is full of emotions, they're like written straight out from the heart. I hope I'll be able to produce music like his some day. His sound design is unique. He deserves much more attention than he's getting now.

tracks.name: 2 Whom I 1-2 4-GET (No Vocals)
tracks.desc: My best track in my opinion. This one has more chords and more time-taken than any other tracks I've made. I don't have a best track until I have a proper arrangement, but I hope you'll enjoy my music :)

intro: Since the primaeval days of Audiotool the name Cianghir has stood for high-karat Tech-House floor-fillers. He masters the tightrope walk between Mainstream and Undergroung brilliantly, producing one clean authentic Club Track after another, all of which should belong in the DJ's cases throughout the world.
about:
Cihangir Eryıldız
Place: Istanbul, Turkey
Age: 37
Profession: UX / UI Designer

interview.a: 4x4 on the floor with love :)

My Father forced me to play Bağlama - an ethnic string instrument - when I was 10 and I played it for 2 years. O_o I didn't really like note reading stuff but interacting with an instrument was great. I simply love the concept of giving a physical input to a machine and getting different sounds as an output. When Depeche Mode led me to electronic music in my early college years I also wanted to create the sounds I like and I ended up playing with Digital Audio Workstations.
In 1995, With my Pentium 166 MMX with dance e-day installed :)
4 years ago. I was looking for some music applications and bam! Audiotool was a dream come true for an amateur.
Love you all guys, keep producing what you like and deliver some great fresh music.

tracks.name: sentinel
tracks.desc: Beside the fact that I'm a big fan, I can't imagine an Audiotool selection without Tornsage :) He always finds ways to touch your heart. I can count many amazing Tornsage tracks but 'Sentinel' is special for me. Piano, drums, overall arrangement and amazing vocals fit together so perfectly and make you feel like you're floating and wandering between different emotions.

tracks.name: Ocean's Tides featuring Sumad
tracks.desc: Oh man, I love everything about this track. That great intro, entire progression, and every single element on this track is simply amazing. One of those tracks that give you goosebumps out of pure joy. Dark, deep and emotional!

tracks.name: A Walk In The Dark
tracks.desc: Kepz can create soundscapes that can take you places. Loved the Spaced Out atmosphere in this great track. I just can't get enough of his 'mostly boring spaced out shit' :) Great artist and Inspiring work!

tracks.name: Requiem
tracks.desc: I have been following Boyles since he was Nowai. Although he delivers awesomeness on multiple genres, I really love his House Tracks. This Deep House piece is just one of many inspiring tracks in his discothequé! Chill yet vibrant...

tracks.name: 7 chords
tracks.desc: My favorite track from the Italian Techno Beast! Chords, Bassline, and that awesome melodic twist after the break. Loving everything about it.

tracks.name: Ibiza Club (Electric Love)
tracks.desc: One of those amazing deep house tracks you listen to when chilling out after six hours of dancefloor marathon and bam! you feel recharged all of a sudden and start dancing again. Amazing Track!!

tracks.name: Technicolour
tracks.desc: I love Akimbo's Style! I wasn't familiar with the genre but Akimbo made me love liquid after I discovered this masterpiece. Banger!

tracks.name: Heis-T'!:)
tracks.desc: One of those tracks I keep coming back for. Over and over. Still I'm not sure why, lol, I mean Great bass and beat, amazing smooth atmosphere but probably just because of its fresh sound, which is above genres. Uber groovy, great dance track!

tracks.name: Steal You Away
tracks.desc: I'm closing with a gob-smackingly glorious House track from Teqtonic. Amazing Beats, Groove, Piano arrangements and a rolling bassline! What can I ask more from a House Tune? Never gets old for me.

tracks.name: Heh
tracks.desc: Artist Recommendation. Of course Zé! I simply love his work and style. Amazing Techno Sound crafted with love :) For me he is the rising star of the AT Community. Keep up the great work man! :)

tracks.name: Blue Days feat. Emma
tracks.desc: Favorite track of mine. This is the very first track I'm proud of. I was actually playing with some house elements and trying to beat dissect a sound I like. Bam, Emma's vocals brought it all together and gave it a melancholic, sad, moody atmosphere which I frequently find myself in. :)

intro: Solace moves confidently in the field of modern Bass-Music, but doesn't want to be pinned down to one particular style. With contagious enthusiasm he throws himself into the production of his unmistakable Solace tracks. Let yourself be infected by his exuberance and creativity.
about:
Solace Bowden
Place: I was born in Baltimore/Maryland but now I reside in New Paltz/New York (USA)
Age: 15
School: New Paltz High School

interview.a: I like to try to keep things weird and obscure, like Soly. I used to be this really jumpy guy that always made super cool drum and bass and super happy electro and tried to make bass-heavy stuff, but now I just like to keep things personal. Every song I make now has some sort of relation to my life in some way, and I like to cheesily use synths as metaphors for things. I'm inspired by mostly underground artists, and I think my style is just mostly some sort of spacey classic stuff. I try to make songs seem like really casual chill acid trips. I never tend to stick to one genre, however. "Relaxing" is what I'd call my style.

I actually don't have any musical background. I learned a bit of piano when I was, like, 7 or 8 (which is how I come up with chords for my songs nowadays) and I used to try to rap, but that's about it.

I started when I was 12. My first song was created with audiotool on january 16th, 2012. When I first started, I'd come out with songs, like, every 4 hours. They weren't the greatest, obviously, but I loved making them.
I spent, like, weeks trying to find some kind of cool but free DAW that I can easily make music with. One day I went on the Google shop thingy, and I found Audiotool on january 16th, 2012.
Don't worry about what other people think. Make what you wanna make... Oh, and I love all of you guys, as people.

tracks.name: Climax
tracks.desc: As if it wasn't obvious af. This is what inspired me to go through my huge DnB phase. My main goal is to make a song that achieves the feel and quality of this track. Best song on AT imo.

tracks.name: blend
tracks.desc: I dunno if kavil puts emotion into his songs. But it sure does sound like it. His stuff really relaxes me.

tracks.name: Night Run
tracks.desc: Long live luxior.

tracks.name: Audiotool Day 2014 - opaqity
tracks.desc: Opaqity is a genius with this track. I always try to make a super cool string synth, and this one is just executed perfectly. Best string synth on AT imo.

tracks.name: East Westwood
tracks.desc: Xavrockbeats is so badass. I can't fathom how beautiful this man is.

tracks.name: There once was
tracks.desc: I love how weird TopHat is.

tracks.name: Cerulean
tracks.desc: Second best song on AT if you ask me. I love his style of keeping things obscurity. He's also great when it comes to manipulating samples and things like that.

tracks.name: Vanilla
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. I think kavil is super underrated. He's a musical genius and I can't get over the way he executes his stuff. He has tons of potential. His stuff deserves to be on the popular list more often.

tracks.name: Girls With Stars For Eyes
tracks.desc: My favorite track that I've made. It is a perfect execution of what I want my new style to be for 2015, and it has a great mood.

intro: Triquent's tracks can largely be described as club music with a focus on Drum and Bass. What makes them unmistakable and unique in the community are the influences of Reggae and of the folklore of his home country Armenia. All that remains for us to wish for is that his studies leave him time for a comeback on Audiotool.
about:
Shahen Khandkaryan
Place: Yerevan (Armenia)
Age: 18
University: Yerevan Komitas State Conservatory (Composition/Compositor)

interview.a:
I haven't been active in Audiotool since last year. So some things may have changed, but not everything. Now I am working mainly on some Neurofunk and other "Neuro" things. Also I do Trip-Hop, Nu Jazz and of course Reggae and all kinds of Drum and Bass. I also like to do some experimental stuff. I like to do - work on drums and percussion as you may have noticed in my Audiotool projects. But now I like to work on everything: melodies, sound-design, synths, effects, drums, drums, drums and everything else. When I was doing my stuff in Audiotool my style was Experimentaliquidsteggaedrumbass. Now it's Experimentajazzsteggaeurofunkiquidrumbass.

So I can't tell you a concrete genre. You're the judge, you're the listener.
I have studied drums and percussion since I was 13. Now I'm a student at a conservatory. My subject is compositon and of course I play the piano. That's important.
I have been "making" music since 2010. But I have been making music since 2012. But I have been writing/making/working on music since 2013.
I discovered Audiotool I don't remember how. It was 2011-2012 and I remember when Hobnox became Audiotool. I think it was 2011.
Love you all guys, keep producing what you like and deliver some great fresh music.

tracks.name: kitsch
tracks.desc: One of the all-time most perfect tracks. I have this on my mobile and I still listen to it sometimes. P.S. Kepz did a great remix of it. Check it out.

tracks.name: Bounce
tracks.desc: Reminds me of my ex-ex-ex-girlfriend. Oh sorry. The track.. Great stuff Aaron, thanks for this piece of art. And I still listen to this.

tracks.name: EVERYDAY ANYONE
tracks.desc: This one is one of my favorites. Unique. Great dark atmosphere. By the way, I have started a remix of this, but I haven't finished because of job/university. I Hope I'll finish it some day.

tracks.name: Mellow State (Nominal Remix)
tracks.desc: Again Nominal. Yes he is one of the most unique artists. Love the dark atmospheres, percussions, pitch-shifted vocals.

tracks.name: The Core
tracks.desc: I know him as Plus+. One of the greatest Drum and Bass artists on Audiotool. This track is one of my favorites of his. Love the Reggae vibes, drums and everything else.

tracks.name: s.st.02
tracks.desc: I remember when I put this on my playlist at a party. Everyone danced to this. Great work.

tracks.name: Ubongo
tracks.desc: Don't forget about CGMAN. The greatest Neurofunk artist on Audiotool. Thanks for this masterpiece. Basses are killing. Drums are perfect and unique.

tracks.name: Spin'Ed
tracks.desc: Perfect use of vocals and all the glitch stuff and of course the drums.

tracks.name: 042/Ins Grüne gehen.
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. He needs more attention people. He has always made great Drum and Bass tracks. Now he does more of some Future Garage, Chill, IDM things. And he's awesome. Go and listen to him. You'll absolutely love his type of art.

tracks.name: The Nature
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. Because everyone needs nature.

tracks.name: Anna
tracks.track_artist: navor, Cuds, looks, tornsage, тяєкк, S.N.O.V.A., _______, The B-ST, Christian-Chrom, TwoSworded & swrlly
tracks.desc: Oh my gosh, I can't describe how great this song is. It just is. I remembered crying for quite awhile when I first saw this, oh goodness, my parents thought I was having a weird fit or something! Good job, guys, this is such a beautiful track!!

tracks.name: rabbit
tracks.desc: The piano within this song is so beautifully executed. The general feel of it gives a chill down my spine. I actually burned this song down to a CD and would occasionally listen to it while driving about to work or home! Definitely my favorite work from Tornsage!

tracks.name: Gamma
tracks.desc: This was the first ever song I've heard from Audiotool and Audial. Right away, I knew, this site is exactly what I want. This is such an underrated track, quite frankly!

tracks.name: Run
tracks.desc: This song was awesome. I can't really describe much of why I love it so much, but in my opinion, this is my most favorite work from Xav!

tracks.name: Empire
tracks.desc: Wohoahoa, this track is just pure badass. I loved the bass and everything about it makes me feel awesome. I can totally imagine this in a videogame or a movie, which I hope CGMan one day gets that upgrade!

tracks.name: bike ride
tracks.desc: The reason behind this song is beautiful. I love just about everything about this track from that synth to the general feel of it! Always gives me a mix emotion that I just absolutely cannot describe.

tracks.name: why did i fade away
tracks.desc: I love the creativity of this track! Opaqity has so many beautiful tracks, but this one I found most interesting due to that synth that really reminds me of old school Metal Gears Solid for whatever reason!

tracks.name: The Tower (unplugged)
tracks.desc: Without a doubt, Sandboogie is amazeballs! This track starts off with a very suspenseful hook, which I like because it makes me feel like I'm in a murder mystery. Not sure why I'd ever want to be in a murder mystery, but blame it all on Sandy if I ever do!

tracks.name: im sorry
tracks.desc: Last but not least, is this track! It is so strange, and unique due to the radio esque distortion! Very chilled and very cool!

tracks.name: G303T
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. 303 is the way of living. Jambam is amazing at what he does, and THE WORLD NEEDS MORE JAMBAM!!

intro: Vassrvögl aka Atomic Duckie brought her own vivacious vibes and turned the place upside down. She's not only a talented musician, with her slightly dubious sense of humour she also manages to keep the community on their toes. But today let's concentrate on the special Ducky style: a blend of brutal but melancholy melodies and hard Bass Music beats.
about:
Anna Pentaghast
Place: I came from Weimar, Germany, but moved to Clinton, New Jersey when I was 10
Age: 20
Profession: I am currently a waitress and am on my last year at North Hunterdon High School due to my poor grades and inability to speak English when I came to America

interview.a: My style of music is anything that I have in mind at the time. I don't really have a trademark other than the usage of the tonematrix in almost every single one of my tracks. Other than that, my songs are mostly melodic and slap dashed as heck!

Before there was Atomic Duckie, I used to play a whole lot of banjo and piano! My old artist name was Laxxy which seems extremely silly now that I think about it! People back in middle school used to call me German Johnny Cash, even though Cash never played banjo! I then later found this song by Zircon called, "Augmented," which really got me into complextro and dubstep. I started making music ever since I was 8 on the piano. Piano class was probably my most favorite at the time, since I just get to play whatever song I want to. I knew Für Elise by heart ever since because of that class!

I discovered audiotool mainly through browsing the Chrome Store after receiving my Chromebook for the first time which is back in 2013 or so. I never thought I'd actually get into using the app since I had no knowledge of music, but after a whole day of just looking at tutorials; I really got into it!

My final words will be, use Audiotool as something fun to fool around with. Like Play Doh! You can shape it into anything you like, but you can never expect to be the real deal next gen Audio Jesus. Never should you stress yourself over Play Doh or Audiotool. And also, SUCK IT CUDDLY, I GOT FEATURED!! WOOH!! MOVING UP IN LIFE!!

intro: ΘLE has undergone one of the most perceptible metamorphoses in the community. If at the beginning he won the listeners attention with robust Drum and Bass pieces without any trumpery, his focus now is on sensitive, atmospheric Future Garage and Chillout. Both of his specialties are definitely worth listening to.
about:
Ole Renfordt
Place: Iserlohn, Germany
Age: 18
School: Märkisches Gymnasium Iserlohn

interview.a: Somehow Futuristic/Garage/Chillout/
7 years of drums/ some experimenting with some analog synthesizers
I discovered audiotool after reading an article about it in the days of Hobnox. I was hooked.

'Got love for each other cause this world got none' - be thankful for what we have here! We are one loving community and I guess there is no other place on the Internet where you get so connected with your personal art as on Audiotool. I love each and every single one of you. Thanks to the Audiotool staff for giving us the opportunity to let our inspiration and creativity flow into our very own music FOR FREE! Have a nice day everyone.

tracks.name: U [Vocals by Julsy]
tracks.desc: What a wonderful track by my german friend Aaron. To start of this album I chose this track for a particular reason. I think this might be my biggest inspiration on Audiotool even today. The drums, the bass and that outstanding and beautiful vocalist Julsy everything goes together perfectly. 10/10 would listen.

tracks.name: 3- EVERYDAY ANYONE [2013 mix]
tracks.desc: This one will never get old! Nominal's track has become some sort of evergreen within the Audiotool community and for a good reason. Creating such a professional track is almost witchcraft. Everything goes together perfectly. The mixing is outstanding and the 4x4 beat gives the beat a futuristic touch even Martey Mcfly would be impressed.

tracks.name: Reckless
tracks.desc: It is almost kind of sad that I discovered Olaf and his superior Garage music only 2-3 months ago but ever since I'm in love! You can clearly see that Olaf is a huge inspiration for me now as I tried to create a futuristic garage Beat in my Track '2048' as he does all the time. Why I chose exactly this track? Cause it is undanceable.

tracks.name: Watashi Zone
tracks.desc: Zone's 'Watashi Zone' has touched my feelings since the day it was published. The melody really warms up my whole body from the inside. Do I have to say more?

tracks.name: Unrealize
tracks.desc: EVERYBODY!! I found Burials secret AT account! There is no other track here on Audiotool that is so well produced in every way. There isn't even any Future Garage track close to the quality of this one. Prove me wrong. Pro tip: You can't.

tracks.name: young
tracks.desc: My personal favorite by the great amoeba. you may disagree with my choice because there are much more popular tracks by him but the fact that 'young' was made completely without the reverb-tool makes it outstanding.

tracks.name: .deepthought
tracks.track_artist: • Λ И Θ Я M Λ Ƚ Ү T Ξ •
tracks.desc: This one is way underrated! The track shows the technical possibilities of the great Application called Audiotool. If you know another artist who can create such pro sounding sounds or uk-ish Garage music in general please let me know.

tracks.name: Midnight
tracks.desc: I could have taken every VLTRA song ever. He never disappoints me in any way. His music is definitely the most artistic on Audiotool. Props.

tracks.name: Jonjon - Breach of Trust (Akimbo and Jambams future Jungle Remix)
tracks.desc: Another friend of mine is Akimbo. Those of you who have known me a little longer might have noticed that I changed my style completely over the years. Back in the days of 'G. Lumberjack' I used to produce Drum and Bass music only and I still love listening to it. So this is a big shout out to all the great Drum and Bass artists like Akimbo, Jonjon, VAPEROUS, Triquend, Notoz and all the others not mentioned here ;)

tracks.name: Plane
tracks.desc: I hope you get famous one day boys. Best group on AT. Best song in this list.

tracks.name: SICK
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. Please stop deleting your tracks :( you're awesome as f..ried cheese! Deer is in my eyes one of the most underrated artists around. Just listen to this track. Has there ever been anything like this ever? Give him a follow!

tracks.name: Someone Said So
tracks.desc: Personal best. Last but not least a Track of my own which is the best I have got at the moment. If you mix everything mentioned above I guess It ends up in something like this. Thanks for listening, Ole out.

intro: The dark figure of Agrippa stands out a mile in our colourful mixture. Reminiscent of the Gothic Music of the late 1980s, his New Wave Sound is chock full of the spirit of that decade and would not be out of place in any contemporary record collection. His eminently experienced Synth work is rounded off by his own vocals, oozing with a goodly portion of Weltschmerz. A real must, and that not just for listeners in dark clothes.
about:
Richard
Place: Originally, from Liverpool, but for some time now I've lived just outside London, and I work in London.
Age: 42. It is the answer to the ultimate question of life, the universe and everything.
Profession: I studied art and design, but now I work at an advertising agency, heading the creative team, using my powers for evil.

interview.a:
I suppose it could be categorised as minimal synth, synth wave, DIY, new wave, vintage synth pop. Occasionally I touch on EDM, but more often I start off with melodies and vocal ideas now, rather than beats and basslines.

I'm really into vintage, analogue sounds. Software synths can often do a great interpretation of analogue sounds – the Pulverisateur being a fantastic example – I love that instrument.

However, over the past year, I've also started purchasing a few physical analogue instruments. I love them. I think a lot of it is down to the small inaccuracies, especially in pitch. And I love the feeling of sounds directly created by turning dials and moving sliders, manipulating voltages.

So, increasingly, physical devices feature in a lot of my tracks on Audiotool, especially over the past 9 months. But I keep coming back to the Audiotool staples, especially the Pulverisateur, which often remains an instrument of choice.

I suppose my period in the 90s as a post-punk and Goth obsessive has stayed with me to a degree. In the 00s I was more into Electro and House. However, more than anything, I think the earlier music of my childhood had a profound and lasting impact on me. OMD, Tubeway Army, The Human League and many others. I vividly remember first hearing their early records (and radio play) as a child in Liverpool – around the 1978-1982 period.

I had a college band in the early to mid 90s, very guitar driven and post-punk and new wave in style. Though electronic was part of it – I used a drum machine and lots of "synth" – actually just a cheap, Yamaha presets keyboard, fed through a chorus pedal and a reverb. Back then creating electronic music just wasn't anywhere near as accessible (or affordable) as it is now.

I largely stopped by the end of the 90s. Probably because my attention shifted from music and nights out towards building a career in design.

At the start of 2013 two things happened. First, I met a few musicians/performers, initially online, then later in person, who were creating fantastic electronic, avant-garde music and I started to feel inspired to create music again.

But the real spark was discovering Audiotool at the same time. I was at a sister-in-law's house, feeling extremely bored, so I was browsing the Android app store and I came across the Audiotool Sketch app. I loved it, and then very soon discovered Audiotool itself.

My very first track on Audiotool was called "Sieben". I shared it with one of those musicians, who was very encouraging, and it was even featured on a small compilation CD, that was handed out as part of an exhibition on DIY music and new independent labels.

A few months later, I had an instrumental album released, physical and digital, on a little indie label.

I also collaborated on a cover version of 'Nowhere to Run' with Ivan Antunovic. I supplied the backing track, entirely produced on Audiotool, and he provided the vocals. It remains part of his set.

In 2014 I was invited to perform at Fluc in Vienna and Abattoir in London. It was never my intention to do gigs (or to sing), but as I was asked, I thought I'd give it a go. I took a physical synth, but in large part I was singing to digital backing tracks, produced using Audiotool. I really enjoyed it – a real buzz. I don't plan to start touring ort anything, but a few gigs a year would be fun.

tracks.name: solaris
tracks.desc: It's called 'Solaris' and the image is from the 1972 Tarkovsky film. I'm sold.

tracks.name: Black
tracks.desc: Fantastic sounds, and I love the progressions, especially in the first third.

tracks.name: Infernal loop
tracks.desc: Great sounds on this track. It's long one and a simple arrangement, but I think it really holds the attention.

tracks.name: you don't know
tracks.desc: Another very accomplished track. The bluesy element of it kind of reminds me of Blade Runner a little bit.

tracks.name: Hemorrhage
tracks.desc: Play loud – one of those tracks that really shows what Audiotool is capable of. Plus I have to include an Inavon track, as I almost always use one of the kick samples he's shared.

tracks.name: Exordium
tracks.desc: A unique, inventive, self-indulgent track. Clearly a very exploratory piece. A great example of the variety present on Audiotool.

tracks.name: Die Ruhe nach dem Sturm
tracks.desc: Amazingly accomplished, in terms of mood, this really does evoke acts like Harmonia for me – early to mid-70s German electronic.

tracks.name: mega device multiplied amplified
tracks.desc: Really cool one. Simple but really driving. I love the droning bass.

tracks.name: Blood Runs Cold
tracks.desc: Favorite track of my own. This is a fairly recent track. It's an example of combining physical analogue synths with Audiotool's effects and sequencing – with the most overtly analogue sound of all – the bassline – being supplemented by the Pulverisateur.

intro: Talking about Audiotool's Ambient and Chillout music one of the first names that spring to mind is that of the hugely talented amœba. With a seriousness unusual at his young age he enchants the listener and takes him on a journey through his personal universe of sound. Find out for yourselves how well this artist's name suits his music.
about:
Andrew Edwards
Place: Fullerton, CA (USA)
Age: 16
School: Troy High School

interview.a: My electronic style of music is geared toward Ambient/Atmospheric/Lo-Fi sound mixed with a lot of other stuff... just basically anything I make I always try to take the listener to another place.
I've been playing piano since age 6
Started scribbling notes around 6 as well but didn't start writing actual music till about a year or so after that.
My brother saw Audiotool on the Chrome app store or something like 3 years ago and thought it looked cool, I looked at it and I thought it was pretty cool.

I think Audiotool is the perfect place to have fun and let your creativity run wild, and I've found there are so many things you can do with electronic music that you can't with the classical approach to writing music for instruments and stuff. When you're writing electronic music I would advise you to listen to lots of different electronic artists and also listen to classical music. Listening to electronic music really helps me find inspiration for different sounds I could make, different textures and stuff, and the classical music helps me with the actual compositional technique; I try to get into the composers head and wonder what they were thinking, why they are doing what they are doing with the music. Just listen and learn from what is out there, keep doing it until you get better and above all have fun. :)

tracks.name: Lost
tracks.desc: This track is very unique in that it goes so many different places, explores so many different sounds from so many different angles in just like 2:45, and I haven't heard alot of that kind of dynamic change in electronic music. And it sounds amazing too.

tracks.name: The stars dance for me (sketch)
tracks.desc: Really nice, I love how it sorta builds up and then goes back down and then the climax hits you.

tracks.name: morning tadpoles
tracks.desc: This one is outstanding first just because of the synthesis. Try opening it up, it's just crazy how he got those sounds. I wish it ended nicer but everything else is perfect.

tracks.name: woes
tracks.desc: I really wish enjoyyourshoes would keep making stuff like this, very beautiful and unique.

tracks.name: dapper
tracks.desc: I couldn't find my favorite track by him cuz he has like 500 so I just put this. It's still perfect, it's cool how he's able to make so much awesome music while still using so much repetition.

tracks.name: Northern comfort
tracks.desc: This one is just awesome, it's repetitive but keeps the listener engaged. And I especially love the sound design and the detail of it. It's really unique and sorta lo-fi. and the movement of it all is great too.

tracks.name: WE TIRE [RIVER EP]
tracks.desc: An amazing experience, probably one of the most creative things I've heard in general.

tracks.name: We Fall
tracks.desc: Very beautiful track and awesome use of vocal samples.

tracks.name: Tides
tracks.desc: This track I really love. Just amazing.

tracks.name: ノスタルジア
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. Very good Hip Hop producer, very musical and unique, and it isn't just all sample manipulation. I love this, it's so tranquil but what's cool is that the sample is actually Clair de Lune and he was able to transform it into something completely different and equally beautiful.

tracks.name: high places
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. At first I didn't like this, sounded too pop-like/EDM-ish but actually it's grown on me. The percussion could be fixed a bit but other than that I like it. It's pretty accessible and sounds pretty good too.

intro:
It's about four years ago that a kid calling himself 'The Crazy Druminator' made his first tentative steps on our app. One name-change and 138 tracks (resulting in 11205 likes) later, Astrum has reached the peak of Audiotool popularity and long since stretched out his feelers towards the big stage.
At the audiotool.con we had the opportunity to experience the energy of his music in a breathtaking live performance which convinced us all: this guy is going places
about:
Jackson Hale
Place: Albuquerque, New Mexico
Age: I am 20 years old at the moment
University: I am studying Recording Arts at the University of Colorado in Denver

interview.a: My style is all over the place, never sticking to a distinct genre. Nowadays it lies somewhere between EDM and IDM and takes on whatever form my mood currently is.

I started playing piano when I was 5 years old, and moved onto drums when I was 9 or so. Since then I've lost nearly all of my piano skills. I played drums in a lot of jazz bands when I was in high school and put in a good deal of time in symphonic type bands as well as an instrumental metal band my friends and I had.

I began making music electronically when I was 15. I stumbled upon Andre Michelle's website which showed me the (in)famous Tonematrix. From there, I followed a link to the Hobnox site, found Audiotool, and was instantly hooked on the concept of electronic music production.
Do, or at least try, anything and everything you can think to do. The more absurd, the better.

tracks.name: sol solo
tracks.desc: Noraus has always been an innovator within the community. He's created some of the richest sounds I've ever heard on the application, and I've learned a lot from checking out the guts of his tracks. Sol Solo has stuck with me especially over the years.

tracks.name: Airglow
tracks.desc: Kepz is able to achieve a depth with his music that most aren't capable of. Airglow is a dynamically lush and airy journey from beginning to end that takes you out of the chair in which you're probably sitting.

tracks.name: Family
tracks.desc: This is about as good as it gets when it comes to collaborations on Audiotool. Jace (J. Fields) supplies a flowering backdrop of pads and light percussion samples while Julsy saturates the scene with her gorgeous drifting voice and thoughtful lyrics.

tracks.name: Line ∞
tracks.desc: Perspective displays his wonderful composition abilities in this one. The two-step styled drums fit perfectly with the melody and crushed bass elements. This one's gentle, but it's bound to get stuck in your head.

tracks.name: Spite
tracks.desc: The feeling this song invokes is so unique; a blend of nostalgia and anguish. It also makes you question the timing and tempo as it switches through drum grooves of differing paces.

tracks.name: Climax
tracks.desc: Top tier DnB. The music speaks for itself really. A thank you to Vaperous is in order for the distinct memories I have tied to this song.

tracks.name: AFTERGLOW ((Jonjon Mix) + deep1tone)
tracks.desc: While we're on the subject of uplifting Drum and Bass, this track will really have you flying. 3 great minds put energy into this thing and it's clear.

tracks.name: teeth.
tracks.desc: Not only is he great fun to be around, he has created some of the most monstrous combinations of tools and cables the audiotool app has ever seen, with a sound to match. rnzr creates music with an extremely high level of mindfulness and creativity.

tracks.name: Cheerful tunes for the end of summer
tracks.desc: I saw Mr. Standfast and immediately knew what I had to do. I love this song, and thats all I really need to say. It never ceases to put a smile on my face.

tracks.name: omni
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. I've always been curious about pot's music. He's created a lot of 8-bit styled compositions through the years. He should go back in time and write soundtracks to the first racing video games.

tracks.name: I Think You're Pretty Great
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. I was inspired by 'Teach Me How to Dougie' but ended up channeling some pretty potent teenage-romance feelings over the beat. I think y'all are pretty great.

intro: The Seahorse's first publication about a year ago, was more like a Machinist-based finger exercises than a complete track. But it was cleary the work of an experienced rhythm man. In this short space of time he has managed to enhance his skills so much that he can now create music which any movie director would give his eye teeth for.
about:
Mark Wells
Place: Denver, Colorado, USA
Age: Almost six (in dog years)
Profession: Recent Environmental Science grad seeking bartending position

interview.a: I don't really know how to describe my style or what genre it is. Sort of a mix between Ambient, Techno, Psychedelic, Acid, World, Big Beat, and an assortment of different live music. Is that a genre? Probably. ;)
I've spent most of my life playing drums in everything from marching band to Punk and Metal Bands with a little bit of Jazz, Funk, and Latin thrown in here or there.
In the 3rd grade with the saxophone and quickly decided that I wanted to play drums instead. My parents were thrilled with that decision.

I discovered Audiotool through the Chrome store. I downloaded the app and promptly forgot about it. I rediscovered it a few months later and was immediately hooked! The Machiniste was the coolest thing I had ever seen ... Then I discovered The Pulv!!!! I had absolutely no idea how to use it and no idea how subtractive synthesis worked, so I became obsessed and read everything I could about the subject and understood nothing. That led to tons of experimentation and relentless studying of other AT artists tracks and I finally began to learn! Yeehaw!!

This thing we call Audiotool is amazing! Not only is it a powerful DAW, It comes with an international community of open minded and generous folks who are more than willing to let you look at how they make their music, and give you advice and feedback. It also comes with people who will listen to your tracks and an almost unlimited catalog of tunes for your own enjoyment. Besides, how many of your friends can tell you they just finished writing a song with a dude from Macedonia. :)

tracks.name: blimps
tracks.desc: My favorite song on AT, by my favorite artist on AT. Listening to a Sndbrgn track is like watching the pieces of a puzzle fall into place, every sound is exactly where it's supposed to be. There's almost a feeling of confidence about his music.

tracks.name: Yeah I know...
tracks.desc: Brainwalker has the amazing skill of making his tracks sound truly like 'real' music. He also has the ability to make you smile when you hear his tracks. If I didn't know it was digital I would have never believed it.

tracks.name: The Hope
tracks.desc: When I think of White Grizzly I picture a man living deep in an eternally snow filled mountain range, a man who snowshoes forty miles uphill just to find an internet signal with which to publish his apocalyptically beautiful soundscapes.

tracks.name: Low Pressure
tracks.desc: Syntax was one of the first artists I got into on AT. The way she writes her music just really makes a lot of sense to me. I basically learned how to use the pulv by opening her tracks and studying them.

tracks.name: Haedra
tracks.desc: The first name in AT techno. His skills are definitely not limited to one genre though. This tech house track is my favorite by him. Great one for dancing!

tracks.name: Untitled
tracks.desc: Mikke is a genius. His sound design is some of the best and most inspiring I've heard on AT. Not to mention, he's an incredibly nice and humble guy.

tracks.name: ground control
tracks.desc: Tornsage: Broken Philosopher, selfless promoter of other artists, and eternal supporter of new ATers. One never knows exactly what to expect from a Tornsage track, but you always know it's going to be GREAT!!!

tracks.name: Oriental Hypnowlsis
tracks.desc: Sonic Particles was only with us for a couple of months last year, but he made a huge impact on many ATers, myself included. I've searched many times for new material from him, but have never found anything.

tracks.name: Highway 169
tracks.desc: I stumbled across 1moreastronaut about six months ago very much by accident. Very glad that I did! Apparently he quit publishing not too long before I started, which is a great loss for the AT community.

tracks.name: All night chime
tracks.desc: Artist deserving more recognition. One of my favorite artists on AT. His distinctive brand of percussion based techno, as well as his awesome synth made percs never cease to blow me away.

tracks.name: Tarantula Hawk
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. This probably isn't my best track, and it's not my favorite, but it's newer and it has less work needed to fix it soooooo......;)

intro: For about three years now MCT has worked his way through a range of widely differing Electro styles. From House via Trance to Ambient and - inspired by his great Audiotool idol VLTRA - even New Garage. His repertoire expands from track to track. If you remind yourself that he got to know Audiotool as a musical greenhorn, his progress has been considerable so that he has made his way into the community's heart.
about:
Tenji Tembo
Place: Baltimore, Maryland, USA
Age: 21
Profession: Student/ UX Developer @ Must Win LLC

interview.a: My style can best be described as a story: When I first started fiddling with Audiotool, I was heavy into the dubstep scene, so I focused on creating intense tracks with crazy bass-lines, under the name DJ-240. Then suddenly, everyone I knew was doing dubstep, so I sought to re-invent myself. I went from DJ-240 to MCTembo, and with that, a change of style. MCTembo focused on Ambient and Future Garage (influenced by artists such as VLTRA, Olafd, and enjoyyourshoes), which lead to the release of Simplicity EP. From there MCTembo ventured deeper into pure ambience, with the release of Isolation and Depths EP. After my foray with Ambience and Future Garage, I began to experiment again. It was around the time trap was up and coming, as well as my discovery of Deep House (thanks to Cihangir, and Mikel Boyles aka Nowaii) ending with the release of Future House EP as MCT. Who knows what the future holds?
I have a fairly light musical background, stemming mainly from piano lessons as a young child. I did about 3-4 years of lessons, as well as a year experimenting with other various instruments.

I really got into making music when I discovered Audiotool, as they provided the platform and the tools to make whatever ran through my imagination, and translate my inspirations into melodies others can enjoy.
Remember Burn Studios? I discovered Audiotool during their Sasha Remix Competition. #Throwback. I wonder what happened to them lol

You guys are amazing. I've never seen such a vibrant and diverse community such as Audiotool. The ability to make, share, learn, and grow as musicians via the internet is a blessing to be had. The feeling of elation you get when you discover the next best thing (like that time I found Intracktion and Claine) and want to shout it out to the rest of the world, it's moments like those that make me excited to use the site. Keep making music. Keep inspiring me. And maybe, just maybe, I'll do one more EP :p

tracks.name: Serenity (progressive house edit)
tracks.desc: It's so hard to find good progressive house. Serenity is a breath of fresh air, because it stems from innocence, and injects the right amount of fun. A stellar classic.

tracks.name: Allure
tracks.desc: The definition of a lazy summer afternoon. The perfect kickback song when it's 3pm on a Sunday. Just you, the homies, some beer, and the sun shining down.

tracks.name: ｗｈｙ ｄｉｄ ｔｈｅ ｌｉｇｈｔ ｆａｄｅ
tracks.desc: Raw Emotion. Raw, Schizophrenic Emotion. It takes a mad scientist, to construct the story this song tells. Ignore the technical marvels, and lose yourself to the emotion this song engulfs you with.

tracks.name: Iwi Biwi Back
tracks.desc: If I heard the makings of a cinematic score, this is it. From the story hidden inside the note, to the amazing pads and melodies constructed. I remember when I first stumbled upon Intracktion, because this was the song that struck me the most.

tracks.name: the last thing
tracks.desc: Witch House is a unique thing, and there are very few that can understand and showcase it's brilliance. Nominal is Witch House in my opinion. The infusion of different styles really sells this as one of his best tracks.

tracks.name: the littlest things [Audio Scribble]
tracks.desc: What is a Remix? This song sounds like it overdosed on LSD and puked rainbows and thunderbolts. Genius.

tracks.name: Night Train
tracks.desc: I have to thank Olaf for introducing me into ambience. The incredible percussion, the atmosphere it presented, the story it told. It's what helped create my first EP, Simplicity. A brilliant Classic.

tracks.name: [Phresh.]
tracks.desc: I'm fairly certain OBN is signed. That theme song you have when you walk down the street, and you know everybody gives a crap about you? This is it.
Experimentation drives innovation. Devoid ¾ was my first touch into Future Garage, and my first exposure to the legend we all know as VLTRA. Plus, Natalie Portman :p

tracks.name: Choosin'
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. Super underrated, a hidden gem in the scene. Choosin' is one of my favorites by him, a real testament to his natural and unadulterated style.

tracks.name: Isolation
tracks.desc: My best track. Do it at night or in a dark place. Grab your best pair of headphones and lie on your bed or floor. I want your heart to beat in unison. Fall deep into the depths of the darkness, and isolate yourself from the rest of the world.

intro: Together with Frigolito, Abstract is probably the user with most Swing characteristics on Audiotool. Expertly he mixes basslines reminiscent of Jazz with elements of Dub-Step, Electro House and Drum and Bass, and that with great success: with almost 500 Favourites, his track 'Illuminati Lumberjacks' is one of the all-time chart-breakers of our website. A must on any playlist
about:
Nathan Glaser
Place: Tulsa, Oklahoma (USA)
Age: 16
School: Booker T. Washington High School

interview.a: Well, since my musical development has primarily happened through jazz, almost all tracks that I write (and my style in general) is heavily jazz-influenced. As an EDM producer, I was first majorly inspired by Savant, and as result a lot of my tracks feature 8 bit type solos and such. Mord Fustang inspired me to write big electro chord-based songs. Once I discovered glitch hop, it fit perfectly with my jazzy style, and I like to write a lot of glitch hop. Overall though, I try to become good at as many genres as possible so that I can be a versatile artist, and now I write songs depending on what I'm liking at that moment.

Both my parents are professional musicians, and I grew up singing in various choirs, as well as playing piano for a year when I was fairly young (somewhere from 6-8 years old), which I then hated but now admittedly wished I had followed through on. From 6th grade to present time I've played trumpet at school, and from 9th to present I've played in my high school's Jazz band.

Sometime from January to March in 2012, one of my friends found out about Audiotool and decided that we should try messing around on it. We both started out as total production noobs and learned from each other for a little bit. I started looking around the site and naturally found Infyuthsion (since he is and probably always will be the most popular artist of Audiotool), as well as Pilgrim. I got super inspired by, well, simply how awesome their music was. Ever since then, I've been having fun on Audiotool and trying to constantly get better.

There's always going to be someone out there that makes amazing music that you can be inspired by. Inspiration is key in getting ideas to flow. Learning from each other is very important to the continuing development of practically anyone as a musical artist :)

tracks.desc: Probably my favorite and most listened to track on Audiotool. The track gives off that barren and muted feel, like a person walking through an icy wasteland... then the bass kicks in and the true deep and dark style of VLTRA comes to light.

tracks.name: tranquility
tracks.desc: Don't let the title fool you; this is one of the most hardcore and eclectic neuro-hop tracks I've ever heard. Luxior did an outstanding job here.

tracks.name: AFTERGLOW (Original Mix)
tracks.desc: An older track that still stands out as a progressive house masterpiece. Epic and inspiring are two words that describe this track well.

tracks.name: Nerd
tracks.desc: A nice little fun jazzy dnb piece reminds me of a spy chase scene. The production quality is sublime, with unique and sophisticated chords and synthwork. Hats off to Gno4166.

tracks.name: wait
tracks.desc: A track that invokes pure and raw emotion. The pad and bass combination is really just outstanding. A prime example of the amazing future garage that Audiotool has to offer.

tracks.name: vitamine DnB
tracks.desc: THRONE (or PLUS+) has crafted some of the most professional-sounding liquid dnb on Audiotool. The moving bassline is so creative, and the typical PLUS+ plucks are meaty as ever.

tracks.name: Survival
tracks.desc: CGMan is the Neurofunk king of Audiotool. This is probably one of my favorite tracks by him, simply for the amazing groove. A shining example of the sound design capabilities of Audiotool.

tracks.name: Space Clouds
tracks.desc: Now this track is simply brain food. When I just want to lay on my bed and let my mind wander, this is the perfect soundtrack. An incredibly clean and chill trance track with a great minimalistic feel.

tracks.name: Firefly (Ft. Luxior)
tracks.desc: I liked this chord progression so much that I learned to play it on piano. The suspension at the end of the progression is just so dang tasteful.

tracks.name: Daddy's Home
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. He makes some groundbreaking stuff. For his high quality trap bangers, he gets only about 20-30 faves, when he should be getting 100+.

tracks.name: Vivacious Vagabonds
tracks.desc: Personal favorite. I really tried to pour my heart and soul into the sololines, especially in the second drop.

intro: SouthBronxDen has been an Audiotool elemental force since its earliest days. His consistently individualistic version of Funk and Jazz in all its scope is his main distinguishing feature and over the years has become his very own style. He skillfully manoeuvers around musical norms and comes up with a genre-mix which can perhaps best be described as Den-Fusion.
about:
Dennis Davis
Place: South Bronx, New York (USA)
Age: I am to old to be alive (62) but I still love to party
Profession: My profession I really don't have one but I do a lot of different things, like working for a hospice, for an airline company, for a shipping company et al. I have sold some paintings I have done. I guess the place I learned and gained the most knowledge from is Audiotool.

interview.a: My musical style is anything goes from classical to Funk, Funk is my fave.
My musical background is listening to all types of music and loving them.

I started making music on Hobnox and followed Audiotool to it's new platform.

I purchased some Fake Books and some on theory and started to use them in my attempts to make music. I am still learning.
Like I mentioned before I started with Audiotool from the Hobnox days when an e-friend ask me to check out his tracks, I have been hooked ever since.

tracks.name: Hot T'!:)
tracks.desc: Hot T by yafee? has greatness in every note with a touch of mystery.

tracks.name: The Restaurant at the End of the Universe
tracks.desc: The Restaurant at the End of the Universe by enjoyyourshoes is a very successful experimental track.

tracks.name: Papa is an Audiotooler
tracks.desc: Papa is an Audiotooler (remix) by Fontana showcases why he is known as the 'King'. Great work.

tracks.name: escape
tracks.desc: Escape by Invisible Green Spider aka DroneAlpha combines thought provoking music and takes the listener to a place of his soul he didn't know he had.

tracks.name: Summit
tracks.desc: Summit takes you to the top, and the bass work keeps your feet rooted firmly to the dance floor.

tracks.name: Can't Get Enough of Your Love
tracks.desc: A cover I did after listening to the recording about a million times, it taught me so much, enjoy.

tracks.name: Alive Comeback
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. An artist who doesn't get enough attention in my opinion. His soundscapes are amazing, they really show how versatile Audiotool really is.

tracks.name: In A Trance (the day my son was born)
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. Kind of a mix of techno and funk. This one has sentimental value.

intro: To call Tornsage an Audiotool legend is really not exaggerating: every one of his tracks raises the standard in the Ambient field a notch higher, showing us again and again how much magically destroyed atmosphere can be conjured out of old familiar piano samples. Beside his enormous talent he is remarkable for his friendly presence in the community: he gives new arrivals a warm welcome and makes them feel at home in Audiotool. Check out the man with the rabbit head!
about:
John Ertsgard
Place: I was born in SanJose California but moved east as a teen to Massachusetts where I have lived all my life since.
Age: I have been around the sun 55 times but feel like I just got started.
Profession: I am an Electronics Engineer and currently work at Bose Corporation in the division creating automotive sound system designs.

interview.a: After I finished my stint in the USMC, I started a band with some fellow musicians named "Johnny Nervous and the Shapeless Pygmies" (rhythm guitar/vocals) playing covers and originals in local clubs. Following that I jammed extensively with my son Nick (drummer, keyboardist) and now I work on my piano and audiotool exclusively.
I started playing guitar as a kid and sang songs like "Yellow Submarine" in a 5th grade band early on.

At the time I was jamming regularly with my son he turned me on to the audiotool DAW. After some time and a couple first tries I was absolutely hooked and have not looked back since. The site replaced oodles of equipment that I had lost, given away or thrown out over the years thus presenting an amazing opportunity to rejoin an earlier effort to create music without having to compromise.

My message is thank you. Thank you all for being so kind, helpful and inspiring over the years. Thank you for the vast array of music and for opening my eyes to new genres that I had never considered. The community at Audiotool is diverse and talented and I will always be grateful to have such an amazing place to work on music and share ideas with others as well as a gifted group of SW professionals who designed and run the site. I truly think that Audiotool is set for greatness and will heartily compete with alternate DAWs in the future.

tracks.name: Heron
tracks.desc: What can I say more than what has been said about Olaf – a massive inspiration!

tracks.name: When Stars Collide
tracks.desc: RNZR's best in my opinion.

tracks.name: Shut up and Fuck
tracks.desc: Elia Trix – a masterful producer who I wish would come back and continue.

tracks.name: The Snow
tracks.desc: I had to include the beautiful work of vile – always an amazing mix and a great influence.

tracks.name: Watashi Zone
tracks.desc: Zone!

tracks.name: please
tracks.desc: Amoeba – what more can one say than what has already been said – a true talent and a beautiful piece of music.

tracks.name: The Hunt Is On (Hunter X Hunter Bump)
tracks.desc: I included this because I love everything offbeat does – this was an especially influential bump probably because it was some of the first work I heard from him. One of the best artists on audiotool.

tracks.name: The sound of the time
tracks.desc: The grizz is a deeply enigmatic artist whose music has no parallel and who has influenced me with everything he produces.

tracks.name: Monoscope
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. Dzool stands out in my estimate of an excellent artist who is very underrated although there are many more I would like to list.

tracks.name: ravenshead
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. This is a track I collaborated with invisible green spider on and to this day remains one of my favs – I think it has a lot to do with the story or the idea behind the track.

intro: Interview unfortunately not available.

tracks.name: Digital Turbulence
tracks.desc: When I barely could use the 909, 808Chunk released bombs like this one, and I was like 'How the hell does he do that?!' He's one of my strongest inspirations and absolutely a nice guy.

tracks.name: bobby
tracks.desc: I have been hooked to this track since I listened to it for the first time. This one is like reading a book, it tells a story. Has some of the best bassline, leads, pads and percussions I've ever heard.

tracks.name: KTN-34
tracks.desc: I'd have liked to include all of KaTaNa production in this edition, but I'm only limited to ten :) His tracks are just technically perfect. He helped me a lot in understanding why and how to use filters, EQs, etc.

tracks.name: techno breakfast
tracks.desc: Christian-Chrom is the bomb! You can feel the energy punching you in the face while his tracks are playing. He got currently 82 tracks in less than a year!

tracks.name: Cold Summer (Marodeur's Bleakness Mix)
tracks.desc: Marodeur is one of the AT techno bosses. His tracks are an unstoppable flow of Techno joy. Great percs and basslines will make you jump while hypnotic FXs and leads build tension.

tracks.name: Synthetik
tracks.desc: in5omniac is another AT Techno hero. His clean and powerful sounds build up a gritty minimal Techno whose rhythm will shake your bones.

tracks.name: Vellicator
tracks.desc: I love how Alan Ross took some noises and arranged them in such a beauty! I think this track was made to introduce the Rasselbock if I remember correctly.

tracks.name: Bogota Funk ft. Thick Dick
tracks.desc: Cihangir knows how to make you dance. All of his tracks are greatly produced, but also build an ambient and intimacy rarely found. Definitely one of the best Deep House tracks.

tracks.name: Pan-Pot - Charly (Farcio's Cripta Mix)
tracks.desc: Cripta took a Pan-Pot song and made it better than the original. Period.

tracks.name: Chord
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. Check this dude out, he's got huge potential! I especially love his dub techno tracks.

tracks.name: Industrial
tracks.desc: My fave track of mine. In this one I tried to tell a story, or at least make it evolving and consistent.

intro: Navor is one of a group of highly talented young American EDM producers on Audiotool and initiator of the very successful and heartwarming mega collaboration for a sick member of the community. In the wide range of Electronic Dance Music he doesn't want to nail himself down to one particular style. So better expect the unexpected.
about:
Orion Hendershot
Place: Currently living in Ft. Myers, Florida
Age: 16
School: Sophomore at South Fort Myers High School

interview.a: Honestly, I don't even know myself. I go from Dubstep to Ambient to Trap to Future Bass, I'm really kind of all over the place. I just enjoy producing everything trying to actually define a style for myself, so I go all over genres trying to find something that I can fall in love with.

I played violin from 4th to 8th grade, but that never really went anywhere. I recently have been trying to teach myself to play piano, so there's that! Before I was listening to electronic music I was listening to Metal a lot, which was what introduced me to electronic music when one of my favorite bands mentioned Deadmau5 in an interview or something. I listened to Deadmau5 a lot when I first got into electronic music, and just branched out from there.

I started making music when I was 12, so four years is coming up within the next year. I started with Looplabs if anyone remembers that monstrosity, then I found Audiotool and let loose. I've been using other DAWs recently such as Ableton and FL Studio, as well.

I discovered Audiotool when I was 12, but not really sure when, but I think it was somewhere in the middle of summer so guessing June or something? I found Audiotool by browsing Google for "Free online music maker". I thought Audiotool sounded like a cool name so I signed up and started messing around. I've had a few accounts before my current one, but those are lost in the past (thankfully).

Just keep your head up. Music is something that should come from within, so always try your hardest to better yourself and others around you. Critique yourself and always try to expand your horizons. Audiotool has a ton of stuff to offer, and even more with the coming updates. Always interact with the community, making friends on Audiotool is one of the best things to ever happen in my life. I've made so many close friends and even met one in real life, which is incredible! The community is one of the best things that Audiotool has over other DAWs and music sharing sites, so my final advice is to take advantage of it!

tracks.name: Silent Anomalies
tracks.desc: Wow. Best word to describe this work of art. Until I heard this track I never really understood the extent Audiotool could reach out to. The basses CGMan makes and the atmosphere he captures is ridiculous.

tracks.name: Nor
tracks.desc: This track is craaaaaazy good. The amount of detail put into the background is astonishing. Not to mention the bass is incredibly good, one of my favorite collaborations on Audiotool.

tracks.name: The Final Scene
tracks.desc: I've loved this track ever since the day it came out. I listen to this track so much you'd think it'd be the most annoying thing on earth to me, but no.

tracks.name: Last Chance
tracks.desc: One of the most professionally produced songs on Audiotool. The setup, organization, sound design, chords, melodies, etc. Everything about this just does it for me.

tracks.name: Still.
tracks.desc: So chill. Melodies in this track are so uplifting. Near, if not, perfect. If only it were longer.

tracks.name: Most Popular Song Of The Hour
tracks.desc: This track is sooooo goooooood. The melody during the drop gets me literally every time I listen. Crazy professional.

tracks.name: two hundred / Audiotool Day 2014 - cluless
tracks.desc: Really one of the best Future Bass songs on Audiotool. Pads and chords sit so well with the bass and melodies going on throughout the track.

tracks.name: Serenity (progressive house edit)
tracks.desc: There needs to be more of this on audiotool. Uplifting house that's not super generic. This track hits me right in the feels so much.

tracks.name: Anna (Notoz Remix)
tracks.desc: One of the most beautiful tracks I've heard on audiotool. Great spin on the original.

tracks.name: t h e w o r l d
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. This guy is a huge friend of mine and I really love his work. He's crazy underrated and talented.

intro: To describe Pathfnder's style only as Retro would not do justice to his wild and always dynamic mixture. But what in fact is ever present is the character of the Roller Discos and Arcades of the late seventies and early eighties, enhanced with a trace of the Modern. And that at the highest level of production techniques. So pick up your joysticks and follow his path on the way to the Highscore.
about:
Metin Filiz
Place: St.Gallen/Swizerland
Age: 32
Profession: Dispatcher / Technical Support engineer

interview.a: I think most producers are influenced by the music they hear and grow up with. Maybe that's why my tracks are a mix of Funk and 80s Synth wave. I love this pushing feeling with strong beats and the characteristic bass and clear synth design.
I learned to play the drums and keyboard for a few years.

My first contact with something like a DAW was in 1994 with a program called Magix Music Maker. Back then it was more of a loop puzzle game with a small library. Producing my own stuff began in 2007 when I discovered Ableton and the possibility to record and work with instruments

I discovered Auditool some years ago at a time where I was bored with pre-designed synth patches but had no idea how to create my own. I thought that my mix would be this blurred because of the bad patches. It was on YouTube that I found a tutorial for the pulv - I think it was by Einhard Luther.

It is really awesome how many talented and creative artists there are in this community! Keep listening, commenting and learning from each other. This is one of the strongest parts of Audiotool I think. Publishing songs on Soundcloud is not half as satisfying as it is on Audiotool, because of the real feedback of the community. Don't underestimate Audiotool and invest a lot of money in "professional" music workstations early on. A painter does not automatically produce a "better" picture with more expensive brushes without knowledge about colors and proportions.

tracks.name: Sole Survivor
tracks.desc: This is what happens when Hans Zimmer decides to produce his next blockbuster movie theme on Audiotool. This track is astoundingly engaging and deep. I can literally feel the drama that is happening.

tracks.name: Airwaves
tracks.desc: This track is so powerful and pushing! This is the soundtrack I would choose if I had to run from helicopters shooting with laser beams.

tracks.name: Airwaves (Teqtoniq Remix)
tracks.desc: This is just brutal! Loved the original and this gave me the rest. Love the buildup so much and then the drop with the vocals.

tracks.name: Highway Cruisin'
tracks.desc: This track could be directly out of my Spotify track list. Melodically and funky Synth Pop with an overwhelming professionalism. I'm in love with the synth design.

tracks.name: Coming Back To You
tracks.desc: Jordi Moragues... what an impressive artist! The emotionality in his work and his talent for playing with the expectations of the audience is unique.

tracks.name: white lies
