tracks.desc: It is absolutely mind-blowing how genius Luxior works with his groove and various puzzle pieces. Every second you hear an unexpected noise that fits perfectly into the shuffled rhythm structure.

tracks.name: Ghetto Blaster
tracks.desc: Retro music at its best! Wonderful job with the synths and percussion. I love this retro Synth Wave style and he captured it with perfection.

tracks.name: Bottleneck
tracks.desc: I could listen to this track for hours, it has a great groove and some of the cleanest and best-mixed synths on Audiotool. The creative note pattern never loses the attention of the listener.

tracks.name: why did i fade away
tracks.desc: This song has everything. I love the dreamy atmosphere and the on-point percussion. Opaqity has the talent to create something that is more than the sum of its parts.

tracks.name: solace
tracks.desc: Solace by Tornsage is one of those tracks that make you forget time and the environment around you. It's like reading a book of emotions. Sad and full of hope at the same time.

tracks.name: Harmonisateur
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. Jordi is a very nice person and a versatile artist, his sound design skills and his feeling for atmosphere are top notch!

tracks.name: Choices
tracks.desc: Favorite song of my own. I've tried to catch the typical 80s feel and power and give it a bit of background with the George Carlin speak.

intro: Since the early days Daftwill has been a well liked member of the community and producer of suspense-packed dark Electro and Techno tracks. Among the sources of his input were a number of Audiotool greats of those days, as can be seen from the number of collaborations he has been involved in. With sixteen collaborations to date he must be the record holder on Audiotool.
about:
William Lachoua
Place: I'm from France. I'm have been staying in New Caledonia for a year but I actually live in Paris.
Age: 20
Profession: I have been working in a gas station for eight months. In October I will start studying at an audiovisual school and attempt to become a sound engineer.

interview.a: I don't really know how to define my style: I try to vary it for each new track I make, but some guys say that my tracks have some kind of dark, trippy and psych sounds.

I never played or composed music before Audiotool, but I like listening to music all day long: from Beethoven to Neu!, through Dave Brubeck, Thomas Newman, Pink bFloyd and Fluke. I like to discover some old and new stuff from any style every day.

I discovered Audiotool in a french High-Tech magazine more than 5 ago, when it was still named Hobnox-Audiotool. At that time, I didn't publish tracks. I just played around with the Tonematrix and the Bassline.

Audiotool is really a powerful website, not just because you have total access to unlimited music production tools. Only your personal creativity sets your limits. But especially because the community is really nice: attentive and helpful for helping you improve. From amateur users to master producers, there will always be people to listen and comment on your pieces.

tracks.name: Space Odyssey
tracks.desc: My favorite track on Audiotool. Sumad and his fellow pall077 did an amazing work on this piece. It's so intense, so trippy. It gives you the feelings of traveling through galaxies!

tracks.name: trip 17
tracks.desc: Each time RNZR publishes a track, he makes everybody agree: each of his pieces is awesome. Very spacey sounds, with only a kick as percussion, it transports you far above the clouds.

tracks.name: Train Of Fear
tracks.desc: For me Frigolito is the best composer on Audiotool. The man who can make synths sing! It's simply a drop of acid in your ears.

tracks.name: Karma Ex
tracks.desc: For a long time my favorite by him was 'crocodile', but he has recently produced that one, and I fell in love with it the moment I pushed the 'play' button. A kind of dark hypnotic melody.

tracks.name: el machinista v.1
tracks.desc: A little 'bomba' as he likes to say. This one is my favorite of his. I love those hypnotic pads, those jumping drums and that clear and terrific bass! No Pulv... but party there is my friend!

tracks.name: FUKT
tracks.desc: Together with Jonjon, the best DnB producer on Audiotool. This track expresses so much violence. A real horror movie soundtrack!

tracks.name: Broken Projector
tracks.desc: I have spent so much time, listening to it while watching sunsets, stars, landscapes, just thinking. I love the kind of Trance Kepz produces!

tracks.name: Shifted
tracks.desc: What a bomb! CGMan made me discover the Neurofunk with his banging tracks! How he makes his synth roar on this track... Unbelievable.

tracks.name: The Nightmare
tracks.desc: If I could, I would choose every track of his, he is simply a genius! That bass! He manages the sound and the specialization so well!

tracks.name: Fluid
tracks.desc: A huge track too! Another Audiotool genius! I love Ascent and his banging Techno tracks! Its sound design is for me one of the best on Audiotool.

tracks.name: little devil
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. Sumad is my favorite Audiotool artist. My favorite Techno track on Audiotool! This is simply perfection.

tracks.name: Head into space
tracks.desc: My own favorite track. The only one I could make sound almost as I wanted it to. But big credits to Noraus.

intro: If you hear driving Minimal beats reinforcing mechanical chirps and swishes coming from all directions you have come across a track by Slopo. Even his first steps on Audiotool suggested that here is somebody who is in command of the Minimal materia. Now two years later the technical know-how is in place, so that flawless Techno tracks are guaranteed. Not only a must for Techno-Heads
about:
Goran Spirkoski
Place: Skopje/Macedonia
Age: 31
Profession: Cisco Certified Network Associate

interview.a: Uff... :) somewhere between Minimal, Techno, Acid and Psy influences. Some people call it Minimal Mechanical Techno, others call it Tribal Psy Techno. I just love to mess around with the music that I grew up with :)
Audiotool:)
I have been working on Audiotool for around 2 years now.
I read about Audiotool in a music magazine about 2 years ago. Immediately searched for it and here I'm :)
Just keep on rocking!

tracks.name: Times 2015 edit
tracks.desc: Trip-Hop-ish 'timeless' stuff from Sharkyyo that leaves you speechless...

tracks.name: an ending
tracks.desc: I love to mess around with the oldies from some of the greatest artists on Audiotool... this is what I found one year ago. And if he says that he always ends up on this one, then yeah it's good enough :)

tracks.name: 200th
tracks.desc: Ok ladies and gentlemen 200+ and still counting... Thanks for your support and critics on my weak basses :) We are waiting for your 228th...

tracks.name: schnibbel it
tracks.desc: Talking about Techno? Olondro is the real guy... He always brings fantastic powerful sound. I still remember when he faved my track for the first time, I knew I was doing the right thing :)

tracks.name: GRIK6:)!
tracks.desc: Who can stay indifferent to this beat? Right now while I'm writing, I'm dancing on the grik6. Fantastic groove, probably my all time favorite.

tracks.name: Preacher man
tracks.desc: Ok this is getting serious :) Great collaboration, so much power, just great.

tracks.name: Cold Summer (Marodeur's Bleakness Mix)
tracks.desc: This one could jump directly to the Top 100 on Beatport. Fantastic vibe, great Techno, great message to the world...

tracks.name: sloroochrom
tracks.desc: Hope you are not angry with me because I added this track where I participate, but this is a pure example of Christian's producing ability. I'm still waiting for his comeback on Audiotool :)

tracks.name: That's What They Want You to Think
tracks.desc: When I met The Seahorse he was doing some big beat stuff and I liked it. In the meantime he has developed his previous style with some new influences and now he is one of the greatest artists on Audiotool.

tracks.name: Kill the noise...
tracks.desc: Brain-walker is one of the few that work on psychedelic. He always brings great basslines on his tracks. This one is my favorites...

tracks.name: We Stopped Dreaming
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. I don't think he is underrated, just he is not so active in the community. However one of the greatest Techno artists on Audiotool.

tracks.name: Pagan
tracks.desc: Favorite track of mine. Pure Audiotool track without samples and without beatboxes. Enjoy :)

intro:
CGMan's Drum and Bass and Neurofunk tracks are legendary on Audiotool. It's just all there, in the right place. It feels as if a soul has been breathed into a huge army of dystopian mega-machines. Always somber, mechanical, yet funky and always on the edge of mixing perfection, everyone of his tracks is an institution in its field.

From our point of view a well deserved top vote.
about:
Robin B.
Place: Altusried, Bavaria, Germany
Age: 20
Profession: Kempten University of Applied Sciences

interview.a:
Of course this changes depending on my mood, but it's usually fast, bassy, experimental, tense, a bit empty, sometimes uncanny. I love the Dead Space game series and sometimes I try to transport these eerie feelings in my music, usually unconsciously. Maybe you felt like there's something missing in my tracks. This is often intended. The reason is difficult to describe. I like this feeling of something strange happening. The absence of the expected consequence of something. A good example is "Silent Anomalies". In case it shows itself, it can still be strange. It's there, you can see it, but it's not normal. You don't want to touch it by any means. Like rotten food. Haha I feel insane now as I write it, but you probably know what I mean. These feelings are so interesting to me as they always make my flesh crawl. It's pretty much the opposite of a love song.

I love subtle melodies in tracks mixed with "breathed" sounds and short samples, so that's what I often want. Some tracks by other artists have short pieces repeated over and over, they can sound equally great.

I tried to play contrabass once but stopped. I never wanted to commit myself to one specific instrument. Sometimes I grab my keyboard and try to play songs I remember from movies or games, or just for fun. I can't play chords and melody at the same time, though. Middle eastern and Chinese instruments are very interesting to me as they look and sound different to what I'm used to. I'm really fascinated by the idea of sounds one never heard before, not melodies. Yes, melodies help, but for me these are "just" 12 different notes in different octaves in different orders, with little freedom. You'll probably notice that when listening to my tracks. Some of them need more melodic elements. If you listen to tracks like "Non Human" by Phace & Misanthrop you'll notice that there is a subtle melody in it. That's one of my goals.
I started with a demo of the "MAGIX Music Maker" at my aunt's computer. They all went to a party and I had to stay home. It wasn't that bad. I was 11 or 12 years old.

Yes, Google. I can't remember the search string exactly but it was something like "make music online". I knew it theoretically was possible. Audiotool says it was 4 years ago, but for me it was at least 5. I did not expect a whole working studio :)

Don't be afraid of messing your track up while editing it. I am afraid and it does not help. It's probably better to just export parts as samples and use these. You'll treat them differently. You won't see them as a huge and complicated network of devices anymore, but a simple sample. I don't do that so you can see how I did it. I don't like the idea of a huge flat sample base either. You can go even further and work in iterations, but this is experimental and not tested yet. I'll try it out.
Thank you for this amazing and completely free application and website. There is no better way to show people what is possible with music and the web and what it's about.

tracks.name: Take and strike v2
tracks.desc: As this was the first track with fast beats I heard on Audiotool. This incited me a lot.

tracks.name: steampunked ted
tracks.desc: I already said it in the comments: I have great feelings when I hear it. Dabrig is a master of little details, everyone should listen to him more often. Do it now!

tracks.name: Living With Stones
tracks.desc: Never forget this track!

tracks.name: .ChillFactah
tracks.desc: If you ever need an endless list of great beats to listen to, you'll find them on his account.

tracks.name: The Truth
tracks.desc: Hey, Goa is a great genre!

tracks.name: Rotom
tracks.desc: Again too few favs. It's a good Breakbeat track with a nice second part. Listen to his other tracks as well!

tracks.name: Empire
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. It feels epic, powerful and interesting to me every time. It has melody, bass, drums and a great vibe. The idea was to create a track based on a pad in the background.

real_name: Joa D. Ebert. The D is for Diego
intro: Joa Ebert is not only a very talented programmer and musician, he is also Audiotool developer of the first hour and father of the first track released on Audiotool. We still consider Joa as a part of the Audiotool team even if he has now already founded his own company with Tim Richter. Defrac is the foundation for the HTML 5 version of Audiotool to be released later this year. Thanks for all your hard work. Without you, Audiotool would never had been possible.
about:
Joa D. Ebert
Place: Originally from Bielefeld, Germany. You could say it is the modern Atlantis, the town that is nowhere to be found. But nowadays I am living in Cologne, Germany
Age: 28

interview.q: What is your job at Audiotool?
interview.a: I founded Audiotool and worked on it for about five years but our ways parted – no bridges burned though. My main job at Audiotool was the core foundation of the application. I worked mostly on algorithms and data structures. But I did basically everything from drawing little monsters, building and shipping the browser app to developing the website and backend. I also wrote some of the "cool" parts – the desktop and cable rendering for instance. I also developed my personal favorite: the Rasselbock. It went through many iterations: from a first completely autonomous cellular automata, playing stutter effects to what we have now (André also applied his Midas touch to the DSP implementation). I left Audiotool to start a new company called defrac. My co-founder Tim Richter and I are developing a platform to convert compiled Java code to different target platforms like the web, iOS or Android. This is quite technical but really fun stuff! I heard through the grapevine that Audiotool Next is built with defrac

interview.q: How would you define your work?
interview.a: As a developer you're in the fantastic position of imagining something up and then being able to create it immediately. It is like Lego on steroids. There are no limits and once you hit a wall you have to be creative to find a solution. It's basically never boring and creating a massive Flash application like Audiotool was a real challenge. If you like solving puzzles, you can probably become a really good developer.

interview.q: What is your educational background for the job you are doing?
interview.a: Blood and sweat. I was a small child when I started programming and went the path of the autodidact. I started to study cognitive computer science once but dropped out which I kind of regret. When I find the time, I seriously want to get that computer science degree.

interview.q: When and how did you start programming?
interview.a: I think I was about eight years old. Our first computer had no case and the only way to reset the machine was by putting a screwdriver on the jump. We also had two games. GORILLA.BAS and NIBBLES.BAS. Those were written by Microsoft to demo QBasic at that time. The only way to start the game was by starting QBasic first. You always saw the source-code. The love of a lifetime emerged.

interview.q: How would you define your musical style?
interview.a: Short-circuits, buzzes and noises. Honestly I feel a dwarfed by all the talent on Audiotool and I wish I had the skills to produce the music I have in my head. I really enjoy pads and melodic synthesizers with a hint of glitch. The music I create is the music I want to listen to myself. When there are 100 listens on one of my tracks, you can be sure that I count for 96 of them.

My mother did a real good job and I am really thankful for her efforts. I went to early music education in elementary school and started playing classical piano around the age of ten. Puberty made me ditch the classical education and I went to a different music school with a focus on improvisation. I also started playing guitar there and I have a bass lying around somewhere. We did a lot of great live gigs with that school and it influenced me a lot. One person suggested a song, then someone who played the drums joined, someone who sings and so on until you have a band for that song. You meet for one or two evenings and then you play on stage. It really showed me that random people can get together and make some music. Hello live collaboration!

interview.q: When did you start making music?
interview.a: I think in 1996 or 1997 my brother and I received a fantastic christmas present. It was a copy of ReBirth RB-338. Later I started using FruityLoops and got into chiptunes for some reason.

Audiotool has the best community ever. Stay exactly like you are. Creating music makes you vulnerable. You share your songs and emotions with complete strangers. I believe that Audiotool is one of the few places on the internet where people aren't talking you down because your technique isn't perfect. Ergo the only thing I can say is that you are simply amazing and that I am proud to be one of the people who made all this possible.

PS.: Some people continue to ask why my profile is so weird. I guess one late evening there was so much work that my head was exploding and I kind of expressed it by turning my profile upside-down. When Martin joined Audiotool and took over he kindly kept this tradition!

tracks.name: The acid threw my suit
tracks.desc: This is simply a great track and I totally love it. It reminds me of a bar where crazy music is played and people dance funny. More of this please! Also: more funny dancing please!

tracks.name: Carsberg
tracks.desc: All time favorite of mine. Techno94 created amazing tracks when Audiotool was still very young and there was no Heisenberg. It's a shame he is no longer active.

tracks.name: Recluse
tracks.desc: This was the hardest to pick for me since Olaf has released so many great tracks. I think Recluse is an absolute Audiotool masterpiece. One of those songs I listen to while working late at night.

tracks.name: nightlight
tracks.desc: Opaqity is my personal favorite Audiotool user actively releasing tracks. I love his music with all the subtle details.

tracks.name: teeth.
tracks.desc: There is simply no top ten without Rnzr. As an Audiotool developer I must say that Teeth is my emotional favorite. It was a track that blew all of us away when it got released. You can't believe how happy we were listening to that song.

tracks.name: Lost (ascent Remix)
tracks.desc: The original by Tyburn is already beautifully itchy. I also love to see when someone like Ascent takes a great track and makes an even better remix. It is what Audiotool is all about.

tracks.name: Guitar
tracks.desc: One of those early classics which used the - at that time brand-new - sample player. It shows once more that you don't need expensive equipment to create great tracks.

tracks.name: Spite
tracks.desc: I can't decide which one to pick. There is Spite and there is Submerge. Spite is definitely more whorled or 'quirlig' as we Germans say.

tracks.name: morning tadpoles
tracks.desc: The synthesizer connoisseur at his best. We're all grateful for the fantastic Heisenberg presets created by Noraus. My personal happy place.

tracks.name: Machine Autopsy
tracks.desc: I want to end this list with a lovely experimental track. I also want to encourage you to try to create your own unique sounds and to continue to push the limits of Audiotool!

tracks.name: Travel
tracks.desc: Underrated artist. Jeremy has lots of potential and will continue to perfect his skills to create mesmerizing Chillout tracks. The track has a great balance and is very relaxing.

tracks.name: ATLAS
tracks.desc: My favorite track. I was the lucky one who released the first track ever on Audiotool: On/Off. ATLAS is the track I like the most because of the way it sounds and what it reminds me of.

intro: Crazy about You's music drifts between melancholy and relaxation. Never intrusive, rarely melodramatic. There is hardly an Audiotool artist who connects his inner live so much with the music he is making as this talented young guy from Spain. An up and coming Ambient artist, who belongs on everyone's list.
about:
Manuel Francisco de Casas Sánchez
Place: Melilla (Spain)
Age: 18
Profession: I'm studying at university to become a teacher in the future. And if possible for sport. I really like kids and I like to teach them. Exactly, I'm studying school teaching and sports.

interview.a: As I said, I prefer to focus my music on my experiences or how I feel at that moment. So the style which I feel more connected with is Ambient/Chill. I also like Jazz, Hip Hop and Trap but my style is Ambient with soundscapes. Although if you follow my music you will see an influence of the poetry that I try to combine the music with. I really like to write with an influence from my life.

I don't have any musical background but I'm really focused on listening to music all day long. Anyway my father loves Jazz music most, so when I was a child I always listened a lot of musicians from Blues to Jazz and Rock and things like that. But I don't know anything about composition or pianos I just like to hear music.

I think that my musical development started a few months ago because I didn't have a ''style'' and I was very bad when I began but I started with my first tracks on AT like 2 years ago… actually I'm learning a lot.

It's a long story. Well one of my best friends ''Mr. Delfe'' told me that he was making music on a website, so I decided to take a look at it When he showed me his first tracks I really liked it so I started making some fun songs there.

You always need to have an inspiration, guys. If you don't have one it is better just not to waste your time. For me the most important thing is to live life to make music. You guys have to live and try to express how you can feel. Never be scared of that.

tracks.name: stay
tracks.desc: This track was the song that moved me to make Ambient and to make the sound that I crafted. I have a lot of memories with this song like a night walk in Roma with someone special to me... you know it's too great.

tracks.name: ◤ dont leave me ◥
tracks.desc: I can listen to this song and not tire of it... the depth of the synth is so calm... the composition of this is simple but it makes it lovely.

tracks.name: Amsterdam
tracks.desc: I can pick any song by TopHat because he always moves something in my feelings. I remember walking to school everyday with this song. It's so peaceful.

tracks.name: Missed
tracks.desc: Everything about this guy is too amazing... I have no words to explain his music. It's a style too personal and very beautiful.

tracks.name: Reaching
tracks.desc: When I discovered this song I thought that I was in a sea of calm. I love every voice, every single synth that makes the melody. My mind escapes with this.

tracks.name: Frigid.
tracks.desc: I really love the chill stuff because it means everything to me and I feel it so deeply. Beautiful melodies with some trap beats. It's a blessing.

tracks.name: She ft Crazy bout You
tracks.track_artist: ·· A w a k e n s ·· & Crazy about You
tracks.desc: I haven't words for this guy. My friend. This track is so charming... I fell in love when he showed me the guitar. The guitar is so lovely and it comes from the heart.

tracks.name: You Found Me
tracks.desc: It's the perfect piece mixing styles. I found it pretty chill and pretty electronic and when the beat comes in I dance alone.

tracks.name: About time
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. A track with full of tempi, little synths sounds, a bass, a pad, and the last part is how to travel in time... I worked hard on it and I really love to make sounds like this.

tracks.name: BGN {OVR VIP)
tracks.desc: I think there is at least one thing everybody can learn from Nominal's music. I could listen to this track a hundred more times and still evoke new emotion from it. One of the most influential pieces on Audiotool.

tracks.name: MCT-03-808 (Vltra Remix)
tracks.desc: Given such a unique style combining Ambient, Future Garage and Experimental elements, there are about 13 VLTRA tunes I want to place here, but this one in particular seems so timeless.

tracks.name: Pour Baby
tracks.desc: A beautiful example of sample based beats, probably my favorite on Audiotool. One of few tracks that you can jam out and chill out to at the same time.

tracks.name: [Adult Swim] Believe In Me
tracks.desc: I remember spending a year making a hip-hop mix just so I could end it with this track. [offbeatninja] is one of my biggest inspirations.

tracks.name: VVV
tracks.desc: xavrockbeats' music never ceases to amaze me. I listen to VVV whenever I have tests coming up. I swear he is actually secretly producing major label Techno music in Japan.

tracks.name: Sequence III
tracks.desc: If Audiotool were one big family, Syntax would be my mother. I always come to her tracks for learning and inspiration. Because of this her older tracks have a high nostalgia factor.

tracks.name: Allure
tracks.desc: This is an absolutely perfect instrumental hip hop beat. One of my first real loves on Audiotool.

tracks.name: Endless
tracks.desc: I meditate to this gorgeous masterpiece; I really hope more people will see it here and listen to it. This track can be interpreted in so many ways.

tracks.name: I'd Rather Be Happy Than Right
tracks.desc: This was my first favorited track on Audiotool. I have never heard another track like it, and I think it is the definition of 'underrated'. It is a true gem.

tracks.name: 真夜中
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. There are some days where I go to U3N's Audiotool page and just play through all of his music. His unique touch is a bit under appreciated.

intro: Talking about Audiotool chilled Hip Hop Beats Sweattz is one of the artists who immediately come to mind. Whereby he does not restrict himself to the common practice of stringing together drum- and music loops. He skillfully combines his tracks with original syntheszier sounds and -lines giving his music an organic substance.
about:
Lance Alexander Sweatt
Place: Greenville, South Carolina
Age: 20
Profession: Majoring in Electrical Engineering at Clemson University. About to study abroad at Tohoku University in Sendai, Japan for a year.

interview.a: Sample based, Hyphy Hip Hop

My father and uncle were arguably the best rhythm section in the state, and so they made sure that from a very young age I was well versed in American Classic and Progressive Rock artists. However, once I discovered anime and video games, my interests started shifting towards more video game soundtrack music, more Japanese Pop, and less Rock & Roll. Even today, my biggest Hip Hop influences are from the Silent Hill and Samurai Champloo soundtracks. Interestingly enough, I almost exclusively listen to super poppy electro house from Japan nowadays, especially in the middle of making a chilled out Hip Hop beat on Audiotool.

My uncle gave me my first bass guitar for my 16th birthday, and then I started a band with two of my close friends. All we had were drums, bass, and keyboards, but we managed to make some interesting progressive jams.

In between band meetings, Jeff (the guy on keyboards) would show me instrumental hip hop beats from the Samurai Champloo soundtrack, as well as artists related to it. That music forever changed me, and from there on I had a deep-rooted interest in chilled out instrumental Hip Hop. Pretty soon after that, Jeff and I were sending each other beats that we found on YouTube. That was when we discovered [offbeatninja123], and through him, Audiotool.

I want to say thank you to everyone who is putting their hearts into making music here on Audiotool. Let's hope that in 10 years the community will be much bigger, and that there will be famous producers who can say they started in Audiotool!

intro: Tottenhauser is one of the Drum and Bass bedrocks of Audiotool's first hour with a huge output of dark, minimal and always driving tracks. His enthusiasm has always been infectious but focused and effective: like his initiating the first international AT get-together, 2012 in Gent. People like him are a great asset to the community being a motor for its progress and inspiration - apart from creating awesome music!
about:
Thomas Valcke
Place: Belgium
Age: 35
Profession: Product Designer

interview.a: Dark & loud atmospheric music with an emphasis on drums
None
Am I making music?

A few years ago. Back in the days when I was 18 one of my mates discovered a Rebirth demo. It was a warm welcoming from playing GTA1 all day. The demo had a time limit so in 20min you had to make all your drums and melodies and then find enough time to make a very rudimentary track. We taped all these little experiments. And listened to it until the tape was beyond salvation. From thereon I realized that everyone could make music without having to buy expensive equipment. (Instead you had to buy expensive software). So because there where better things to spend my money on, it never really got serious.

Many years, already a workingman, and probably a bit frustrated in life and work. I was in dire need of a creative outlet and thought back to those moments we had with the Rebirth demo. So I decided to go and look on the Internet for free music software. And then I stumbled upon Audiotool. I couldn't believe my eyes. It had all the features Rebirth had and a lot more! It ran in your browser and it was free! I was instantly hooked. And the first 2-years the addiction went quite far. Just ask my wife.

Nowadays I can control the addiction much better. But from time to time the urgency comes and then I know exactly where my medicine is.

Have fun, respect the community, be eternally grateful for the makers? Don't make music to be popular or rich. Don't follow trends. Don't care about followers, listens or favourites. Just be you and make your music because you need to and enjoy doing it.

tracks.name: Amen Junglist
tracks.desc: One of the first tracks I heard on AT, and definitely the one that showed me that old-school Drum and Bass/Jungle was feasible on AT. Ascent has made many masterpieces but if I had to pick one in the end it would be this one.

tracks.name: Poymi-Menya (a.b.demo)
tracks.desc: Another less-known classic. I fell in love with this track the minute I heard it. The eerie chantey like background. The percs... And then the sub. And to top it all: the harp-like sound. Everything fits so well together. Anthony is a genius.

tracks.name: Impact
tracks.desc: I will always know him as plus+. Another liquid Drum and Bass producer I'll gladly include in my list. The first time I heard a Reese-like sub in an Audiotool track. I studied the crap out of it.

tracks.name: ...and then all this crazy-ass robots appeared
tracks.desc: What can I say? That bass, the percs... the crazy-ass-robots! Just brilliant! Mindboggling and a huge game-changer. Noraus always leads the way. We mere mortals can only follow.

tracks.name: R
tracks.desc: Surely Oedipax had to be on the list. She always makes the darkest Drum and Bass. The layered drums. The eerie background sounds. The Trance-like synths. A massive uplifting break. She truly is the queen of Drum and Bass.

tracks.name: Limitless
tracks.desc: The Amen breaks, the synths, the pacing... the energy of this track... every time I listen to it I get goose bumps. Probably not the best-known track from Notoz but to me it is his best by far.

tracks.name: A Completely Normal Day
tracks.desc: The guy is crazy we all know that. Jambam has a great talent for combining sounds and find melodies in the chaos he creates. His glitch work on this track is probably the best on Audiotool.

tracks.name: REsurrection
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. This track is the closest I have yet come to creating the dark Drum and Bass/Jungle I love. There is still a very long way to go. But this track was a really important milestone for me.

intro: Fauko aka Pedestrian is one of the few Audiotool one man bands. His compositions are influenced mainly by the Synthpop of the 1980s whereby the pleasant timbre of his voice makes its own contribution to their authenticity and evokes memories of bands like 'Depeche Mode' and 'Camouflage'. Not only fans of the early electronic Pop music will get their money's worth.
about:
Tom Davis
Place: Currently living in the Washington, DC metro area
Age: 42
Profession: sysadmin

interview.a: Whatever comes naturally. My last couple of published tracks (last one from fauko and first one from pedestrian) could be considered Synthpop.

I sang in church as a child and throughout my youth. I also took piano lessons briefly as a child (wish I had stuck with that!) and played trumpet in school bands from age 10-18. I also played with brass ensembles at parties and events as an older teen. Oh yes, I was in a rock band for a brief period of time after graduating high school. I never studied much in terms of theory – making music was just a fun hobby for me.

In terms of attempting original composition, as a young teen when I got a Casio MT 205 keyboard as a gift. It wasn't much more than a child's toy, but this was circa 1987 and about all my parents were willing to shell out for. The best thing about it was it had an output that I was able to utilize with a jack on a boombox on which I would record to an audio cassette. I would record what amounted to accompaniment and then attempt to add a solo melody with my trumpet. That lasted until I was about 19. I stopped making music completely until discovering Audiotool many years later.
In late 2011, I was exploring the Chrome app store and ran across Audiotool. It was and remains far beyond anything I expected to find for making music online.

To the Audiotool team: Thank you for the site/DAW/community you have created and continue to develop. AT is a true gift to anyone interested making electronic music and I believe it provides value to users of any experience, from novice to pro.

To the community: It is amazing to experience the music you create and share. I have been introduced to and influenced by styles of music I never would have considered listening to before joining Audiotool. I appreciate the inspiration, encouragement guidance and friendship that I have received from you.

tracks.name: Fabrique
tracks.desc: One of Shakey's early tracks that helped put him on the map – it directly inspired one of my own. I have always felt a connection to Shakey and his tracks.

tracks.name: system of a system
tracks.desc: I love the concept of this track. AudioArchitect is one of the best artists I have come across when it comes to articulately expressing his state of mind through his music.

tracks.name: evolutionary humanism
tracks.desc: This track has some wonderful old school elements in it. The bass in this track is hypnotic to me. Whenever I hear this track, it generally takes about 24 hours to completely work its way through my brain.

tracks.name: almost (transmutation)
tracks.desc: Dronealpha is one of the unique artists on AT in that he uses it as tool to combine samples generated from recording live instruments. He is one of the artists that inspired me to begin uploading my own samples.

tracks.name: Auf Kurs (2015)
tracks.desc: A recent remake by this prolific and legendary AT artist, this track showcases signature Sndbrgn sound that can be found in his work, regardless of genre; intricate percussion with mysterious synth work delicately layered on top.

tracks.name: Waiting Door
tracks.desc: Another AT legend typically known for dark Techno tracks, this track shows that Cripta is a versatile artist with outstanding synthpop sensibilities.

tracks.name: Set Phazers to..... Everything
tracks.desc: I realize this is Jambam's most popular track and everyone reading this has probably heard it before, but it's a track I can't stay away from for too long without experiencing withdrawal symptoms.

tracks.name: Trance´n dance
tracks.desc: When Intracktion showed up on AT, it was evident he was an accomplished artist. His experience and talent allow him to show how powerful a DAW AT truly is.

tracks.name: leck am reaktor
tracks.desc: The synth work in this track still haunts me (in a good way) to this day. Where the heck did this guy go?!

tracks.name: dark floor
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. I came across this artist after seeing some faves of his tracks from Shakey and was instantly drawn to his stylings. Loads of potential here.

tracks.name: Electronic Soul
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. I feel like this track has a decent amount of polish to it. When I first started on AT, I never would have dreamed of trying something like this!

intro:
Since the early Audiotool days Mikel Boyles aka Nowai has been a guarantor of a sound which - whatever style he is trying his hand at - shows his unfailing feeling for the origins of that musical culture. Whether Detroit -Techno, Chicago-House or Oldschool-Jungle: his tracks could without exception have had their place on the vinyls of that era.

His portfolio is reminiscent of a visit in a records store in the 1990s.
about:
Mikel Arrid-Thomas Boyles
Place: Born in Pensacola, Florida, USA but spent most of my years in Fresno, California, USA.
Age: 26
Profession: Professional Dad

interview.a: Fluid. I constantly shift between different genres and styles of music. Repetitive Techno one day, grooving House the next, Ambient and D&B on another.

As far as playing actual instruments I didn't really start until I was a sophomore in University and that was guitar. Nowadays, aside from making tracks on Audiotool, I play guitar when the feeling strikes.
I started making really bad mashup Hip Hop tracks back in 2007. I'd literally take an instrumental track, an acapella track, and smash them together using Fruity Loops (now called FL Studio).

I discovered Hobnox Audiotool in the winter of 2009. I made a plethora of pretty basic Techno tracks and haven't looked back since. I watched it go from a pretty basic app to something that can be used to produce very impressive tracks if you have the vision and skill and a powerful enough computer!

To the community: Just have fun making music. Seriously. Don't get caught up in trying to make the next big club hit, trying to recruit everyone on the site to be your follower, or making tons of money off of your music. If you make good tracks people will find them.

To the Audiotool Devs: Thank you so much for your hard work. Because of you lot I've been able to hear some incredible music, meet some awesome people, and find a way to make the music I always wished I could! Thanks for the spotlight!

tracks.name: Blue Days feat. Emma
tracks.desc: Cihangir was an interesting artist to me. I first heard 'Back 2 Corporate Slavery' and thought he was decent. When I caught him again he had released 'Blue Days' and I was hooked.

tracks.name: allday long dub
tracks.desc: Noraus is a wizard when it comes to utilizing the tools that Audiotool makes available to us and he is a big reason why I try to experiment as often as I can.

tracks.name: invaders
tracks.desc: Christian-Chrom is a more recent artist I stumbled upon. He likes to have fun and experiment with different synths and tools much like noraus.

tracks.name: Subsonic Collision
tracks.desc: Choosing my favorite 808chunk track is like choosing which of your children you love most. 808chunk has always been a great ambassador of Oldskool music and very tight sound design.

tracks.name: Kiçini Bikini
tracks.desc: I don't know much about Austek other than that he is Turkish, makes incredible tracks, and loves to move between styles of music with almost every release. His tracks always sound polished.

tracks.name: Guitar
tracks.desc: 5 years ago I was absolutely floored by what yas had created. This one really inspired me to try chopping samples more. yas was very influential in my musical development.

tracks.name: Levitation
tracks.desc: The first of my two favorite Audiotool junglists. She has a way of using samples, manipulating them, and fitting them seamlessly into a track. This track ended up on the 'Oldschool Jungle' Wikipedia page. Class!

tracks.name: Dirra
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. He is an old friend from school who wanted to know how I made music. This is my favorite track of his since it was his first attempt using the site.

tracks.name: Iluntasun
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. One of my first proper and well produced Jungle tracks. It highlights my love of the Rasselbock to create different sounds and beats and my gratuitous use of the Machiniste.

intro: In his past four years on Audiotool Teqtoniq has made some kind of a fairytale career. From his beginnings as a comparative novice in production to one of the most successful and inspiring artists on the website. Hardly a week goes by without one of his tracks at higher places in the charts. Whereby he plays on the claviature of contemporary electronic music as if he never did anything else before.
about:
Arjun Sharma
Place: Toronto, Ontario
Age: 18
Profession: I'm studying Biochemistry at McMaster University.

interview.a: One of the things I'm most proud of is that I feel like I'm able to work on music from a wide variety of styles and genres. I love to play around with hybrid genres, and I enjoy pulling influences from all over the place - jazz, EDM, classical, movie OSTs, whatever.

I've been classically trained in piano since I was about eight or nine, in addition to studying music theory. I've also played viola for 7 years in both small chamber groups and larger orchestras. And sometimes I play around with a guitar.

I've been listening to electronic music all my life. I guess it was only natural for me to start playing around with the idea of trying to make it myself, so I started looking at free DAW options around 2008-2010, when I fooled around on a site called Splice Music (where I first used the trance10 username).

When the original Splice Music went down around 2011, I started looking for alternatives, and the visual layout of the Audiotool app at the time was pretty similar to Splice Music's layout. I decided to try a few things out. At first I just played around with loops, but then I started to play with the Pulv and it kind of snowballed from there. I would go over to my grandparents' house and just sit upstairs for hours working on music on crappy speakers - but it just captivated me.

tracks.name: ./dream
tracks.desc: Just a really gorgeous soundscape. There's also a nice balance between having energy in the percussion and just creating a relaxed feel overall, which I love.

tracks.name: please
tracks.desc: I can't express enough how emotional this track is. It's gotten me through hard times in the past, to be completely honest.

tracks.name: On The Roads
tracks.desc: I love Jazz. This song inspired me to continue to try and incorporate different genres and influences into whatever I write. No nuance is too subtle to impact a song.

tracks.name: No Future (ft. CGMan)
tracks.desc: Just one of those tracks that leaves you in awe. I've included it purely on its merits in sound design. Seriously a pleasure to listen to.

tracks.name: BABY LET ME
tracks.desc: One of the tracks that I really fell in love with earlier in my Audiotool journey. It really showed me the beauty in percussion.

tracks.name: River
tracks.desc: Really just amazing atmospheric work. Everything is done so perfectly; I'm still listening to it from time to time.

tracks.name: Proxima
tracks.desc: This brings back so many nostalgic memories of the SEGA Genesis, I can't even explain how happy this track makes me. There'll always be a place for Sonic the Hedgehog's music in my heart.

tracks.name: Opus 1: 'Dubstep Sonata' Mvt. 1 in F# minor
tracks.desc: This is such a cool track. The production was awesome for the time, and the composition was pretty inspiring; it really shows you that harder sounds can fit right in with more melodic ones.

tracks.name: Nocturne
tracks.desc: The power of a well-executed bassline, and a perfect atmosphere.

tracks.name: Rebel.
tracks.desc: Underrated artist. I've had the pleasure of working with Kryptic on a few occasions, and what amazes me the most is probably how far he's able to see ahead when he works.

tracks.name: Coriolis
tracks.desc: Favourite own track. This one went through a lot - the draft was broken and then recovered by AT staff - and it's probably one of my favourite tracks, both technically and musically. I'm still in love with that guitar in the breakdown!

intro: Our final Featured Artist of the 2014/-15 season is Bracolli from Brasil. His style is orientated toward the classic electronic Techno and Acid artists of the early years and the original Dub Techno music of the Berlin based project 'Basic Channel'. Be sure to check out this talented young guy it's definitely worth it.
about:
Gabriel Evangelista Adão
Place: Francisco Morato (São Paulo, Brasil)
Age: 17
Profession: Etec de Francisco Morato (school), Northern Sub Records (Label)

interview.a: Experimental Old School Music
None :) Computer Music?

In 2010 I started getting interested in electronic music, so I tried out an online application which was just clipping samples. Between 2010 and 2013 I listened to several different styles of music. I was so interested that I started listening to Deep House and researching the history of Chicago House on several sites. It was there that I came across Detroit Techno which was quite different from Techno tracks I had ever heard. Most of these were not Trance but Techno with the industrial sound of Belgium. At that time I started to feel like producing music. Until one day I listened to an 808Chunk which inspired me to finally get on with it.

In 2012 I discovered Audiotool on a website for applications and game downloads. I even tried to do a song but the Adobe Flash Player on my computer was outdated. So I asked my dad to update it because my Linux computer could not support other music applications. He was so busy that it took a long time to help me upgrade the Adobe Flash Player although he is good with computers.

Then in 2013 Flash was updated and I started making music with Audiotool. The first tracks were horrible. Audiotool is a great App! But it is very difficult for those who know nothing about music production.
Thanks for your comments on all my tracks! If you are new to Audiotool and do not know anything about music ... I have to talk to you: it's not easy but do not give up!

tracks.name: Detroit is Calling
tracks.desc: Superb! Besides being a great producer, richyrich makes a great chord stab, inspired by the power of the soul of Detroit Techno.

tracks.name: Extatic Flashback
tracks.desc: The master... (I do not need to describe the track any more, just listen) Thanks to 808chunk, I decided that I was going to make music on Audiotool.

tracks.name: Scarlett
tracks.desc: Mikel Boyles produces many beautiful and deep tracks, that make you go into a trance.

tracks.name: Bicycle Day
tracks.desc: I love Acid! I remember the tracks of the 90's. ShivaCult is one of the best acid producers on Audiotool.

tracks.name: Neptune
tracks.desc: Zé is a great friend... those toms, hi hats, synths, drums... The futurism of this track brings me a feeling that I'm in a Sci-Fi movie.
I have been impressed by ascent's ability since I heard 'Corner Shop' which he made with only two Heisenbergs, three Tubes and one Compressor. #05 is everything I like in minimalist tracks.

tracks.name: clock
tracks.desc: 'Super Neons On Vegas Airwaves' very great name isn't it? I could not forget it. It was him who heard my first track. He was my first follower.

tracks.name: Awsome Swells
tracks.desc: I really like the track and heliotrope's Old School style :)

tracks.name: Interactive Whiteboard | Techno track
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. There are very underrated producers on Audiotool, so it is difficult to recommend only one artist... I recommend my good friend Sousad: he is very creative.

tracks.name: four-dimensional space
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. It was an attempt to produce Dub Techno turned out to be more like some futuristic Detroit Techno. Special like 'Strings Of Life' by Derrick May, 'Pacific' by 808 State, 'Good life' by Kevin Saunderson.

tracks.name: Bein
tracks.desc: First track I ever heard on Audiotool.
To be fair, all of ascent's work is amazing, but this is my personal techno favourite.

tracks.name: In my Shadow
tracks.desc: Marodeur really knows what to do in techno and this is a perfect example to justify that.

tracks.name: REtaliation
tracks.desc: This is serious drum and bass. Serious, dark and in a way futuristic in the fx.

tracks.name: Faster and Faster
tracks.desc: I had to return the favour to xav. This track is like a proper soundtrack to a film with its amazing bass and general epic atmosphere.

tracks.name: spirit c
tracks.desc: Olondro never fails to impress - spirit c has some great progression, and somehow in its minimal structure it seems really interesting.

tracks.name: Industrial Sabotage
tracks.desc: I think I've said many times that this is one of my all time favourites, probably even stretching outside of audiotool. This is the real dark stuff.

tracks.name: peach, seven.
tracks.desc: Opaqity is quickly becoming one of my favourite audiotoolers - his melodies are always so complex and so professional.

tracks.name: extreme Party (re)
tracks.desc: A fine, perfectly crafted and heavy track... I had no idea sumad was so great at producing dnb...

Artist recommendation. In5omniac creates some of the cleanest stuff in the community, ranging from minimal techno to experimental soundscapes. I'm baffled when seeing how his stuff doesn't usually get 30+ favs.

intro: Another young talent of ours is Daniel Burgoyne, also 14, from London. We well remember how at the age of 11 he delivered such stringently produced Minimal-Techno Tracks that our jaws dropped when we heard how young he was. Today he is well established in his sector and again and again he proves his talent with successful experiments in other styles. Judge for yourselves
about:
Daniel Burgoyne
Place: London, England
Age: 14
School: Christ's College (Finchley)

interview.a: Don't know really, just anything outside of EDM
3 years playing the drums
Around 2011
I literally searched "free music production" and AT appeared

I'd like to thank all of the people who have supported my music, even when I seem a bit more unknown in the community and even when I am not slightly confident in a track. Thanks to the developers of Audiotool for creating this incredible environment (even if there's still a lot more to come) and not making me pay for it. I've learned a lot.

intro:
Infyuthsion is not only by far the most successful producer on Audiotool. From Hardtrance to 80s inspired Electro beats he has a near perfect command. But his involvement reaches further than mere music making. His video tutorials have cult status and many a beginner has gained from them valuable insights into the use of Audiotool and production techniques in general.

Thank you Michael for your support and the truly great contribution you have made over the years.
about:
Michael Hleck
Place: Winnipeg, Manitoba, Canada
Age: 28
Profession: Following my heart :P

interview.a: I like to use lots saw waveforms and play the same 4 chords over and over again. This is mostly because I have very little understanding of music theory. But that's okay. What I can tell you, is that if this was like, some kind of music mag interview. I'd fake a european accent so I sound all inspired and underground, and say some rubbish stock line like: "With Compu'ers, it's like the possibilities are endless."

I've never really played any traditional instruments, and in fact I had no interest in music at all until I was 15. I mean, I guess you could call a computer an instrument! So, in that case I'm a real professional at pressing/clicking buttons that make sounds. So Pro, brah.

Somewhere in the early 2000's, I fumbled my way through trial and error and learned how to make tracks in early versions of Fruity Loops. Which was funny, because there were so many really bad glitches. Like, really bad glitches no one explained to me. I mostly ignored normal social interactions like meeting girls and going to parties, to invest my time into pressing a key, hearing a stupid bass sample, laughing, and repeating. It's been about 10 or so years and now I have friends to come over, press a key, hear a stupid bass sample, laugh, and repeat.

"Back in the Day" when the site was still under "The Company That Shall Not Be Named" (AT seems to hate Hobnox or something, it's basically the Voldemort of Audiotool), My friend Tyler told me about Audiotool.

I used it to mess around while I was in class, instead of doing my work. Coincidentally, I also got top marks in that class as a network engineer. Also coincidentally, no one really seems to want to hire network engineers, and so I went on to work doing security. This is where I spent the majority of my time using Audiotool, as the cities highest paid musician.

tracks.name: allday long dub
tracks.desc: Noraus is just so godly good at creating wonderful atmospheres in Audiotool, and I don't think there is anyone on Audiotool who doesn't like this track.

tracks.name: AFTERGLOW (Original Mix)
tracks.desc: This was my favorite remix contest, the original track is just great and has a good flow. Plus there is some good memories of nostalgia.

tracks.name: Trapped Inside the Box
tracks.desc: An insanely gorgeous track that tickles my ears in all the right ways. It's just so, so good in every way.

tracks.name: Office Bossa Nova
tracks.desc: All of Frigolitos tracks are astronomically miles above what everyone else on Audiotool can make. Coincidentally, I find this track rather amusing.

tracks.name: Beyond Reality
tracks.desc: A really talented guy with obviously a lot of experience. Audiotool needs more psytrance. Everything here is pure excellence and shows what audiotool can do.

tracks.name: Too Far
tracks.desc: Love the rhodes in this track. Yeah Astrum has a lot of dance music, but I like his chill stuff.

tracks.name: Chasing Happiness
tracks.desc: Underrated artist. He's been around for a bit now, but if you haven't checked him out his tracks are really cool!

tracks.name: Topaz
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. There is just something about the simplicity and beauty of this track that reminds me of when I first started making music. I love how the synths just intertwine throughout the track. :)

intro: Whenever the signature n_n crosses your path UniverseCosmic is not far away. His fresh Disco House style is oriented to the origins from Chicago just like the French House of the late nineties. Get your bell-bottoms out and let the music play.
about:
Uriel Zaldivar González
Place: Chalco De Díaz Covarrubias, México
Age: Coming soon 22 years! (Day after tomorrow)
Profession: Employee at a Recycling Plant

interview.a:
My style... hmm. Well I like make easy beats in general which I experiment with. But I have two main styles:

The first: Stupid Melodic House Music. Which are cheesy melodies on easy drum beats are catchy sounds.

The second is my Disco Drugs. Which are danceable tracks between 120 and 140 bpm with the classic four on the floor beats with a lot of filtered sounds and in particular cases the 303.

Also I try to make Drum 'n' Bass and other stuff... n_n

I have been listening to Disco Music since I was 10 years old and I lhave listened to a lot of different music genres. I tried to play the guitar, keyboard, drums and some percussions but my experience is practically null. I only know the basics of music production. The scale, some chords and patterns, drums ... Currently I'm trying to study a little more... n_n

Well ... when I was a kid my dad had some old instruments. I had fun over the years creating sounds which I liked. In 2008 I tested a lot of programs for making electronic music and I made some tracks for fun. Oriented in tribal beats, percussions and drums. But I stopped until I found AT. n_n

One day, searching for musical games, I found the emulator RT 909 by André Michelle and I liked a lot.

Later in 2011 I came across Audiotool and I made some tracks. But I left it again to sort out my musical idea and returned in 2012 as Universe Cosmic. n_n

Audiotool: Thanks for existing:

To the new users: make and listen to the music you like and accept the good or bad critiques in order to improve. Enjoy this great opportunity to express what you think and feel to the Audiotool community and be grateful to the people who made Audiotool possible n_n

tracks.name: Menara Petronas
tracks.desc: Great sounds. I sense the feeling between two great artists. Maybe it's not their technically best track but it is a beautiful memory from those days. n_n

tracks.name: disco shark
tracks.desc: Awesome, I love the percussion work. The synths are addictive. I wanna dance in the sea... n_n

tracks.name: Suzlukla
tracks.desc: My first reaction was OH! That sound is really danceable, stupendous rhythm and crazy synths, love it... n_n

tracks.name: clubbing
tracks.desc: The synths are cool, love the subtle phaser effect. I keep my trance and feel a small sense of dance in me... n_n

tracks.name: Funk experiment
tracks.desc: Brings me old memories. Funk style. The sound of the bass and piano are pleasant but it's the synths that catch me... n_n

tracks.name: Lavish life (Filter house)
tracks.desc: Memorable track, essence of Old School... n_n

tracks.name: LoveBall
tracks.desc: Glorious sound, excellent management of the samples. Nice creative work in the bass, really feel something with this. It's right: No Pulve No Party... n_n

tracks.name: Great Escape
tracks.desc: Feel the energy flowing. This sound is really relaxing for me. n_n

tracks.name: Palm Trees
tracks.desc: I love a lot the Drum 'n' Bass, but this style is impressive. He is a genius with the synths. It makes me feel good and without a doubt I would call it brilliant... n_n

tracks.name: sister
tracks.desc: 8 bit nostalgic feel. Continuous rhythm, awesome synths. I love the wood block, it makes me shudder. Maybe it is a bit short but I love it! n_n

tracks.name: Stoici Urqhestrva (UC & n_n)
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. I am proud of this track. No samples, no presets, just Audiotool! n_n

intro: Another highly talented young Audiotool personality. The Trap Beats, which he mostly produces himself, give his tracks the independent character that is missing in the work of many others in this genre. Keep your eyes and ears open: there's something to discover here.
about:
Nathaniel Green
Place: Florida
Age: 15
School: Sophomore in High School

interview.a: I'd describe my style as instrumental Alternative Hip Hop and sometimes I'll make trap music.

I taught myself how to play piano in fourth grade, and I've been a percussionist since the 6th grade. I'm currently playing quints in my High school marching band. (Playing Quints builds some serious chops. My wrists are a lot stronger now.)

I discovered Audiotool while looking up ways to make music for free. At first I wanted to be an animator but I found out quickly that I'm really bad at drawing and that animating is really difficult. So I found this website called Soundation and it was cool, but I had a feeling that I could find something better. I did a Google search, found Audiotool, and the rest is history.

My advice to the Audiotool community is to always be yourself, always experiment, never be intimidated by people better than you, and for the love of all that is good DON'T DELETE YOUR TRACKS. THERE WILL ALWAYS BE SOMEONE DISAPPOINTED WHEN YOU DO THAT. That's all. Stay classy Audiotool.

tracks.name: Odyssey.
tracks.desc: Lewis is a huge inspiration. We're about the same age, but his music is on a completely different level than mine. This song in particular is very cinematic and the choir like main synth is simply magical.

tracks.name: Faded
tracks.desc: I've got to thank $Ramoney$ for helping me get interested in trap music. Songs like this one make me believe that trap music isn't just repetitive hi hats, 808's, and air horns. This song is a gem.

tracks.name: Teal//Water
tracks.desc: Definite proof that some truly beautiful tracks on this program. [Hybrid] unfortunately deleted most of his tracks a while ago. Luckily, he kept this gem. When I listened to this song I was inspired to make more professional sounding tracks.

tracks.name: Play That + [Hybrid]
tracks.desc: This collab has two artists who I love very much. It's probably one of the most original Hip Hop songs I've ever heard. I love the chill synths and the fact that this song is in 3/4.

tracks.name: Doctrina de Lumine
tracks.desc: I was feeling kinda down and I'm not sure how, but it kinda matched how I was feeling. I loved the percussion. The attack on the pad was genius. I just really like the vibe this song gives off.

tracks.name: ixcanxfly.
tracks.desc: Possibly one of the best Lo-Fi Hip Hop songs I've ever heard. Everything about this song is excellent to me. The sampling is great. The distortion is superb. This is a legit [AS] bump track.

tracks.name: Home
tracks.desc: This was one of the first songs I'd heard on audiotool when I first started. This song sounded so professional. To this day I wonder how DR U M was able to make this masterpiece. This song helped me get interested in making music.

tracks.name: [AS] Magic Lamp
tracks.desc: OBN helped get me interested in experimental hip hop early on. After I heard this song I started discovering artists similar to him. OBN has made hundreds of other beauties but this one will always have a special place in my mind.

tracks.name: I'll never forgive you
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. This guy is very underrated. He's just producing hot black fire track after track and I don't feel like he gets the attention he deserves. He's very unique, and he's very skilled.

tracks.name: Sippin
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. It's short, it's simple, and I can bump it with my friends when we're hanging out. It sounds pretty nice on my speakers. I've made better tracks but this is my favorite.

tracks.name: Illuminati Lumberjacks
tracks.desc: I love the melodies in this track. I like how they all blend perfectly when they overlap in the drop, it sounds very nice. Also the chords are reaaaaaally good.

tracks.name: [as] Make U Mine (But How?)
tracks.desc: The theme of this bump resonates with me a lot, when I think about past crushes and stuff like that.

tracks.name: Falls
tracks.desc: The beat and the piano sample go really well together. I like the rough sidechaining on it, and the melody.

tracks.name: young
tracks.desc: I challenged amoeba to make this track a while back. It's a track without any reverb at all. He still managed to produce an amazing ambient track just like I expected.

tracks.name: A2 - HWE.1
tracks.desc: This track just takes me to another world. VLTRA has managed to create such an ethereal atmosphere and I'm at a loss for words to describe it.

tracks.name: ｗｈｙ ｄｉｄ ｔｈｅ ｌｉｇｈｔ ｆａｄｅ
tracks.desc: This one is very unique. I've never heard any song like this before. It's interesting because it was made of familiar sounds, but it was presented in an unfamiliar way.

tracks.name: Roll Through [Scribble]
tracks.desc: The melody is so catchy, I love it. The vocals are catchy even though I don't know what they're saying.

tracks.name: Twinkle, Twinkle - Synth Voice
tracks.desc: This is just next level. Frigolito is known for being extremely creative and he always does things with the app that no one else even tries. I admire this track because it shows the undiscovered capability of AT.

tracks.name: 2 - SLEEP DEP
tracks.desc: My favorite part of this track is the intro, it just mesmerises me. Whenever I listen to it... I feel sentimental.

tracks.name: Paradise, Honest
tracks.track_artist: Future Sound Collabs, trrrrillionare, Tyburn, Nominal & Post Myth
tracks.desc: One of my favorite tracks of all time. Even outside of AT. This song is just so beautiful. All the parts of this track just flow perfectly and blend really well, and I love it.

tracks.name: FROGs and RABBITs
tracks.desc: Underrated artist. MUDA is probably the most underrated audiotool user I have ever seen. The way he uses presets is amazing, his/her music is so professional.

intro:
Proxima's abstractly minimal Dubstep approach leaves a hardboiled impression that suggests his productions are the work of an old hand. We were all the more baffled to find that the artist behind the fantastic album 'hope EP' was at that time 13 years old.

Today, a year older and no longer quite so reticent but no less tricky, Proxima is playing at the forefront in the league of complex contemporary Bass Music. Definitely worth a visit.
about:
David Thompson
Place: London, England.
Age: 14
School: Student at Dwight School London

interview.a: Dubstep music. I like making Dubstep music because there's a lot of freedom within the genre. (There are many different styles of Dubstep.) I really enjoy making melodies, with triplets and swing notes. I like it when a few good melodies overlap in the drop, it creates a really "full" sound. I never learn't music theory.. I kinda just go with what sounds good. I think it's working for me so far. :)

I play piano. I stopped having lessons about 2 years ago, now I teach myself. I used to learn guitar but it hurt my fingers too much, so I stopped. Now, my computer is my instrument. But whenever I write melodies..I always go to my piano to play it, and make chords and stuff.

2011 - 2012 i think..It was a few years ago. I first got into EDM music when I was in the car with my dad and a cool song came on the radio. It was Superbad by Doctor P and Flux Pavilion. I was like whoaaaaa. Before this time, I didn't really listen to music much. It was this track that first got me interested in the whole EDM scene. I found all kinds of music. I unearthed a passion for music within myself that I didn't even know was there. I loved music so much I wanted to see if i could create my own. So i looked on the internet for any free online music production software. And after some extensive searching i found a yahoo answers post where someone recommended audiotool.com. This is when I found Audiotool.

2011-2012. I started out making sample tracks (like most people.) I just put some samples into the timeline and published them under the alias "Lumax". Then for about half a year I just stopped. I think i got bored of that. Then one day when I was visiting a friend, he told me that he wanted to become a music producer. I told him about Audiotool, and produced a track in his house. This was my first actual track ever. "Alone" (I've deleted it now). I got back into Audiotool. I began to make Dubstep, since I was really into it. Then I made some experimental/ambient type tracks, and now I'm back into Dubstep. :)

THANK YOUUUUUU

I want to thank everyone. First of all the Devs, thanks for creating such an amazing and versatile software

I'd also like to thank all of my followers. I never imagined that I would get anywhere close to 600 followers, let alone being this week's featured artist :O. I don't always like my music that much but i'm glad you guys do.

To all the new/aspiring producers out there: DON'T GIVE UP! I didn't get to where I am by giving up. Just make what you enjoy, and become a part of the wonderful community we have here. The only way to improve with your music is to keep making it, and fine tuning your style.

Thanks for listening, guys. :)

intro: The brand name Mikke stands for versatility at the highest level. Whatever style he adopts it's always the characteristic hypnotic synth melodies that are at the forefront and unfailingly hit the mark. The most recent sensation was the quantitatively and qualitatively very successful remix contest of his track 'Ghosts'.
about:
Mikael Quantacquapende
Place: I was born in Rome, Italy on the famous Tiber Island right in the middle of the city. The non Italian name is due to the fact that I'm half Swedish. My mother is the blonde one and my father is the Italian one. Now I'm still living here in Rome but maybe I will leave Italy after school. I was just thinking about a colder place... oh yes I'm half Swedish.
Age: Almost 18
Profession: I'm in the last class of senior high school.

interview.a: This is the hardest question that Audiotool put to me. I think I'll define my tracks as a meditation but the truth is that I don't have one particular style. These are the ones I prefer and to which I'm getting closer: Cosmic Music, Ambient and Funky music.

So when I went to secondary school I played the flute... and that is pretty much my musical experience before Audiotool eheh. Not so much, really but I listened to a lot of music. When I was small my father made me listen to Peter Gabriel from whom I learned never to love only one single style.

I started making music from zero experience (oh yes the flute...) with Audiotool three years ago. Making music by my own was never in my plans but when a friend showed me his tracks on this mysterious app I was inspired. After this I decided to open an account and to publish some tracks.

Thank you to all my supporters for these three amazing years especially jinx, farcio and whitegrizzly for introducing me to the community and for teaching me a lot of things :). Audiotool is incredible, it completely changed me regarding music. Also when I listen to music I now distinguish all the sounds and understand more things. So thank you for creating this fantastic app!

Cheers Mikke.

tracks.name: sol solo
tracks.desc: This true artist never fails to surprise me and when this track came out I couldn't believe it was actually made with Audiotool. The first moment I heard this masterpiece it inspired me so much that I decided to start making this type of music.

tracks.name: Dream Tape
tracks.desc: This is the best Trance track I have found on Audiotool. I immediately fell in love with this amazing masterpiece because of the pad shots he uses which create a really cool ambience. At 2:51 my brain starts 'travelling by itself'.

tracks.name: 4 AM
tracks.desc: Astrum really chose the perfect title for his beautiful track. Every time I listen to it I feel that I'm actually alone at my desk at 4 a.m. producing in front of a window from which I can see all the city lights.

tracks.name: Resonance
tracks.desc: This track of my good friend LowFeel brings me so many memories. It was actually him that advised me to listen to the great noraus. I hope you will be back some day.

tracks.name: Airglow
tracks.desc: Another trancey bomb from Kepz. His basses, his pads, his melody and his reverb work are incredible. This track makes me feel as if I'm actually driving a car alone in the night, wondering.

tracks.name: Find a place right here
tracks.desc: Amazing collaboration of Cripta and Sandburgen! The melody is fantastic, the vocals are perfect and all the effects get me immediately. Great Masterpiece.

tracks.name: Shine in the Night
tracks.desc: Great collab of Lowfeel and uncle Cripta! Super sounds and melody which develops perfectly. I just hope that the four horsemen appear on Audiotool again.

tracks.name: Beyond Reality
tracks.desc: This track is just BIG! It's a Psytrance masterpiece which immediately penetrates your mind. At minute 3:12 BOOM, everything explodes and the same happened with my mind.

tracks.name: there's always tomorrow
tracks.desc: This great artist has amazed everyone with his beautiful tracks and this beautiful piece of art particularly inspired me. The melody is great and the sounds are as clean as they can be.

tracks.name: morning tadpoles
tracks.desc: I think this is the cleanest set of pads that I ever heard on Audiotool. All the sounds/effects are very professional. This is a track which you hardly believe that it came out of Audiotool.

tracks.name: Spine
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. Xtract - because I simply like all the tracks I have heard from him, because he is very underrated for the work he does and because he doesn't focus on just one style.

tracks.name: Lost Here
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. I consider this track to be my best one because it turned out as really wanted and expected. I'm usually not so sure when I publish a track but this time it was different.

intro: Hardly any other artist on Audiotool has such an unmistakably original musical profile as Mr. Standfast. Every one of his almost five hundred tracks is a small work of art, each with its own special cosmos. His work with drums, melodies and atmosphere is unique and for the inquisitive definitely worth studying.
about:
Jack
Place: Virginia, United States
Age: 33
Profession: Voight-Kampff specialist at Tyrell Corp

interview.a: Music for driving up to Grand Marais in the autumn. My stuff is inspired by a mixture of Presbyterian theology and boreal forests. An essential part of what it means to be made in the image of God the creator is, I reckon, to have a creative capacity. And the ability to, more or less, create and design the sound of your instrument every time you work with a synthesizer is part of what makes synthesis so amazing. I dig that.

At the same time I was listening to Grieg, Beethoven, and Brahms I was playing piano in a jazz band. We used to go into the practice rooms in my old high school and listen to really off the wall stuff during study hall. The drummer in our band lent me a copy of the Richard D. James Album, and from there I spiraled into Autechre, Bochum Welt, Photek, Global Goon and have since then only fallen more deeply in love with what a synthesizer can do.

I've been composing music as long as I've been playing instruments. I played piano from the age of 6, and pretty early on was interested in improvisational jazz, but also orchestral music and big cinematic and romantic musical landscapes.

A friend of mine (who is a brilliant musician, and analog genius) put me onto audiotool back in 2010. Since then, I've made around 471 tracks...which...if you do the math...is a track every 4 days. I don't have a lot of free time, and tend to just jot down melodies as they pop into my head. But this year I've been in grad school and only published a track every few months or so.

It should blow our minds that Audiotool is possible, and that it is free. in 2014 audiotool sent Kepz, Frigolito, RNZR and me to Moogfest, and I am not exaggerating to say it was a life changing experience. I think the culture of synthesizers is an incredibly generous and beautiful one. We are all people who have a deep respect for the people who make our instruments--and have to confess that much of what we do would not be possible without the artists behind the instrument. At Moogfest last year, Tom Oberheim spoke a bit about his role in the invention of MIDI, and he paused for a moment to play music written and performed by artists. He was moved to tears at hearing what other people had made out of his instruments. Clark did something similar during his set, pausing to express amazement at what he was able to get from his machines. I think that expresses a perfect summary of the community of electronic music--it's a place where technician and artist coexist peacefully, and get to make some pretty amazing sounds together. That's why I love the fact that Audiotool is full of old and young, remix and original, collaboration and solo act. Thanks to Audiotool for creating the instruments and the place, and thanks to all you Audiotools for making the place amazing.

tracks.name: A TURN
tracks.desc: This is just so impossibly brilliant I don't even know what to say. It showcases the talents of each artist (and they are formidable).

tracks.name: sol solo
tracks.desc: Max's work is its own universe of meticulous sound. He's got an incredible way of balancing massive bass and carefully balanced sound with delicate warbling melodies.

tracks.name: Auf Kurs (2015)
tracks.desc: There's a reason around 2000 people follow Sndbrgn - he's arguably the greatest pound for pound artist on Audiotool. Like Plastikman with a sense of groove. Just a top notch, brilliant artist.

tracks.name: Small Intermezzo
tracks.desc: Brilliant electro orchestral stuff here.

tracks.name: tears
tracks.desc: Amoeba is just always incredible. This stuff is demanding of the listener, complex and unpredictable. You can hear a sense of classical harmony in just about every piece.

tracks.name: Ipyfo
tracks.desc: Hanging out with Kepz and Moogfest last year was one of the highpoints of the year. He's (maybe more importantly) one of the most brilliantly natural musicians on Audiotool. Kepz belongs in any sensible top 5 list of greatest Audiotool artists.

tracks.name: Lonesome
tracks.desc: Thousands of Audiotools DEMAND to know what evil genius is behind this. The smart money seems to be on frigolito... but it's quickly becoming one of the great unsolved mysteries of Audiotool.

tracks.name: the passers by
tracks.desc: This song zaps whatever part of my brain can't stop listening to Global Goon. Opaqity always has something unusual and wondrous going on, this one's just offbeat and weird and excellent.

tracks.name: twin pines (Tott RMX)
tracks.desc: It's a bit embarrassing to include a remix of one of mine on here. But I love the remix culture on audiotool, and this song is one example of how Tottenhauser's mad genius can take one of my little tunes and make it something more memorable.

tracks.name: fma 2 fjfj
tracks.desc: Underrated artist. His last track was published three years ago, but I wonder if there's some way to bring this hidden genius back?

tracks.name: bactor
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. I wrote bactor just after something pretty excellent happened... so it's probably a more fitting choice for edition Audiotool.

intro: [Hybrid] has been an insider tip for a considerable time. About three years ago his deep, atmospheric track 'Please Keep Breathing' had not only made it on the second a+ compilation 'Amorfx', it also topped the a-records charts on Beatport for more than a year. His mixture of abstract beats and intense sound formations is definitely worth a listen.
about:
Kahliel Jones
Place: San Francisco, California, USA
Age: 20

interview.a: My style is a combination of samples (used as textures) that are tweaked using the pitch knob on the Machiniste, coupled with many different variations of leads and pads (primary created with the Heisenberg; occasionally the Pulv) and a wide variation of Drums and percussion, typically created through the Machiniste and Beatbox 8.
