tracks.desc: Kryptic started to reach out and find his potential in music last summer. This track was a good example of music progression on AT.

tracks.name: Underground Love (Ft. DubLion)
tracks.desc: This collab was the start of something special. Xav wanted to see what would happen when two styles of music production come together. I'm glad this collaboration happened.

tracks.name: Metropolis
tracks.desc: This song has inspired me from the start. Nostalgia is what is brought back for Ketram's old songs. Ketram has always been an Audiotool legend to me.

tracks.name: Stream
tracks.desc: This song takes a darker turn to any mix. He always delivers the best of Neuro Drum and Bass when are in need of it.

tracks.name: alone
tracks.desc: His tracks inspired everyone on audiotool. The chords on this track remind me of Hybrid minds but overall show what Audiotool is capable of.

tracks.name: Reborn - [1/5]
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. Kyba creates some wonders of Audiotool. He designs the most perfect atmospheres and definitive drums which makes me love the unique style he brings to Audiotool.

tracks.name: Story of a Lifetime
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. This song is the definitive of summer 2015. This brings memories back from this amazing time. I hope to release a 2016 summer album this year which will go beyond boundaries.

intro: Notoz' atmospheric Breakz with their reference to the original approach of Drum and Bass stand out among the many young Audiotool artists. Unobtrusive, beautiful pads and sounds accompanied by easygoing Break Beats leave the listener nodding with a faraway smile.
about:
Joao De La Cruz
Place: New York City
Age: 18
School: I'm currently in my final year of high school

interview.a: Drum'n'Bass!
I never had any musical background except Audiotool

I started making music around 3-4 years ago in another account where I would only use samples. I got fed up because what I was making was nowhere near what others could create, that's when I decided to start over with Notoz.

I once found an amateur music producer on YouTube, after listening to his music I was really inspired and wanted to learn how to make music like he did. It never came to my mind that these artists made music through a software before coming across this artist, it just never came across my mind. I then decided to search up "making music online" and found Audiotool.

Audiotool is really something great. I think we can all agree. Many great artists are being practically raised here. I can't explain how incredible Audiotool is, and how grateful I am that Audiotool is even a thing. Just don't give up. Let inspiration move you to greater lengths. Great Artists were once new. We improve every single day and with every single track we make strive to learn something new from it.

tracks.name: Resurface
tracks.desc: This track deserves so much attention. Really just a general favorite. This almost didn't sound like it was made in Audiotool. It's just so well done in every aspect.

tracks.name: spiral
tracks.desc: So many memories with this one. The melody, the bass, the atmosphere. Really just an overall beautiful track that I often come back to. Amoeba has many other amazing tracks, but this one hits the feels.

tracks.name: PLANO [Extended]
tracks.desc: Definitely an under-rated artist, very talented individual with so much potential and really shows it with this simple track.

tracks.name: Everlasting Autumn
tracks.desc: My original Drum and Bass inspiration here at Audiotool. Onyx has so many great tracks, however this one brings back so many memories and it was the reason why I moved towards Drum and Bass.

tracks.name: Into the atmosphere
tracks.desc: Another artist who inspired me to move towards Drum and Bass. Sota has many other great tracks so I suggest those who haven't checked him out should really do so.

tracks.name: Day Dream
tracks.desc: Dublion is without a doubt a talented artist. I've been working with him for some time now and I'm pretty impressed with the amount of motivation this guy has. This is personally my favorite track from him.

tracks.name: Waves In The Sunset
tracks.desc: Syntax' pads are incredible and in this track really shows it. A really great track made by Syntax, absolutely love the simplicity of this track.

tracks.name: Broken Projector
tracks.desc: Kepz is a master at his art, whether it be music or painting (yes I saw that Pulv painting of yours). This track by Kepz is my personal favorite, like a lot of the tracks on here, it brings back memories.

tracks.name: Comedown
tracks.desc: This along with Heis'n'b brings back so many memories. I remember this was my first track I ever downloaded at Audiotool around my freshman year of High school. A great classic by Astrum.

tracks.name: I Can't Explain
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. Previously known as 'Mourn' or 'Another Terrestrial'. This guy is incredible and his tracks are pretty unique. Definitely under-rated. I just really love the atmosphere he puts out in his tracks.

tracks.name: Kodama(Ft. Mourn)
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. I was pretty proud of the outcome of this track, also thanks to Mourn it was fleshed out. I think it kind of puts me into a nice step into the type of DnB I want to create.

intro: Ollie Jarvis - known to many of us as Oscar Ollie - has unmistakably made quite a development on Audiotool since he's been active. If his early tracks sound a bit dry and wooden, today he releases atmospheric Dub- and Chill-Step tracks with an obvious love for details and sound-design which should find their place in the top league of Audiotool producers.
about:
Oliver Jarvis
Place: Bromley, just outside South London (originally). Currently living on the Isle of Wight next to a beach!
Age: 20
Job: I left college a couple years ago as a trained chef/waiter, as for now I'm taking a break, but may potentially start at a music college later this year.

interview.a: More than anything, Dubstep, but if I can keep it 'fluid', then I'll try anything.

My background is pretty eclectic. I've been a member of 4 different choirs. I used to be able to play guitar, got to grade 4 on trumpet, and I can jingle fairly proficiently on the piano/keyboard. But my main passion (as hopefully conveyed by my music) is drumming or anything percussive/rhythmic. As many of my friends would attest to, I'm almost always beatboxing or tapping something out with my feet or hands.

I came across Audiotool through the lil Tonematrix app on the chrome store around 2011/12 and I haven't looked back since. It wasn't until I started looking around the community on AT and listening to all this incredible music that these like-minded people were putting out that I realized 'Wow I wanna be as good as these guys one day'. So I knuckled down and started trying to learn about it. It's not often I truly stick at something. I'm one of those guys that gives up pretty easily if I can't do a certain 'thing', but I was determined to be someone that people wanted to listen to. Around the time I started, I was getting little to no sleep at night so my days were literally 'go to school, come back, go on Audiotool, go back to school'. It was basically my alternative free therapy.

Without including cliché after cliché in my message, I just want to start off by thanking the whole team for letting Audiotool be a real platform and creating a totally unique community. It truly is something great here. All I can say (as someone that isn't committal in the slightest), keep trying to make music. There are literally no wrong answers when it comes to music. If you and your ears like something, then stick with it. And please please don't conform to the charts, or bandwagon onto the newest genre just to get favorites. You keep doing what you're doing and your audience will find you. Oh and just because I've seen it posted so many times; Yes, for any kind of creative person, whether its music, writing, drawing or what have you, having on and off periods is totally okay, it happens to everyone. Just don't decide to 'quit music' because just temporarily you don't like your own sound. If it helps, when I'm at a 'block', I listen to all kinds of genres for ideas. Inspiration can be found everywhere.

tracks.name: Cash Stacks
tracks.desc: When I heard this it blew my mind, the production was/is incredible. This was the first time I had heard somebody use Audiotool for that fast-paced, bright and complex sounding kinda style, which plenty of other artists then try/tried to emulate later on.

tracks.name: Re-boot the system
tracks.desc: I've always been a fan of Tyburn, he has musical golden thumbs, and this proved it. I wouldn't even know what to classify this as, but it's chaotic and it works so well.

tracks.name: Teal//Water
tracks.desc: The vibe in this track caught me immediately, the solemn nature of the pad/lead, and the reverb-ey percussion just sets the perfect mood, but it's the progression almost following a 'two steps forward, three steps back' format which kept me listening.

tracks.name: Soar
tracks.desc: I still maintain this is my personal favorite ambient track on AT. I don't know how he made that big gliding lead and I don't want to know, just to keep the magic. This has been one of my 'comfort tracks' over the last few years.

tracks.name: Chirp
tracks.desc: Xav is one of my favorite artists, but the sheer uniqueness of this track makes it memorable. It's all calm, birds chirping, some water fx, then bam, one of the fattest Neuro/Glitch basses I've ever heard.

tracks.name: #FDR002 Hearts
tracks.desc: This is one of those tracks that from the second I pressed play, I just sat motionless in my chair and listened, my mind playing out like a forlorn black and white film. A thinking track, but still a massive favorite.

tracks.name: there's always tomorrow
tracks.desc: I would just put Opaq's artist page here, he is literally golden, but this track is the standout of mine. He definitely influenced me to start looking for alternative percussion.

tracks.name: cars passin
tracks.desc: Not sure if it's just the key this track is in, or the work with that piano, but something about this track just puts me in a good place.

tracks.name: Malice
tracks.desc: Feel a bit bad for putting the same artist in here twice, but c'mon it's Tyburn. This was the first time I'd heard AT being used for UK-style Dubstep. The vibe Tyburn creates in this is still something I'm trying to emulate.

tracks.name: Technicolour
tracks.desc: Akimbo and I started out on AT at pretty similar times, and I like to think both of our sounds have matured and evolved during that time. This hands down is my fave of his though. The first time I listened my first thought was 'Yup, he's defo going places'.

tracks.name: Nervous
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. Always been a solid presence ever since I stepped into AT. It's always a gamble as to what genre they put out, but it's always a charm to listen.

tracks.name: I Really Liked You
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. It was made pretty shortly after a breakup, hence the name, but sentiment aside it's the first track I've made that I think really sounds like a 'live' organic track.

intro: Alexander Obst aka Sumad is a real technical genius. His methods are as unique as they are unmatched on Audiotool. From the very beginning his technically and musically elaborate, claustrophobic Minimal tracks convinced users and team members alike. So it was not surprising that with 'Sumersum' he released the first a-records album ever in 2011. The sound design plays a special role in his production. By the use of bouncing and other tricky recording techniques he creates this special organic sound for which he is known and admired.
about:
Alexander Obst
Place: Rudolstadt near the Thuringian Forest
Age: 34
Job: I trained as a Media Designer and I am working in a church Institution for the homeless

interview.a: I don't really know if I have a style. At the moment it's pretty dub-chordish. For some time now I have been tuning the synths to 432hz instead of the usual 440hz as I feel this makes it more harmonious. Besides which I sample the things I do in Audiotool using a microphone or some other analogue machine to avoid sterility. This method often leads to an atmospheric noise which I like to keep: I really love it cause the tracks gain more depth. Most of my tracks are build on one basic tone, I rarely work with melodies. In addition to this I use only a minimum of elements.

I started playing the guitar and Keyboards at the age of 14 mainly with Punk and Hardcore. My father gave me a lot of support by constructing me an amplifier and speakers and giving me a room in the cellar for my hobby.
In Autumn 2010 I discovered Audiotool which is now my main program.

A big thumbs-up for the developers. A very easy-to-use programm with practically everything you need. I would only wish for a sampler to load pianos or other stuff to play in the app and a delay which you can adjust to milliseconds. And otherwise: Don't watch TV it makes you stupid, make more music instead!

intro: From his earliest Audiotool days Cal Lycus aka The Marquis was not satisfied with just making his own music and continuously improving it – which he did, without a doubt. His involvement in Community matters manifested itself in the projects and ideas (some of them really fun) which he has brought in over the years. At the moment he is, as co-initiator, working on the ATU-Podcast series, which he also moderates in a fresh way. A valuable member of the Crowd, whose music deserves more attention than it has been getting.
about:
Calum Price
Place: near Cardiff, Wales (UK)
Age: 22
Job: Freelance Outdoor Instructor/Coach/Guide. (Rafting, Canoeing, Kayaking, Gorge Walking, Flowrider etc.)

interview.a: I guess at the moment Drum and Bass or Halftime. I like making Techno and atmospheric stuff too. There will be more Atmospheric/Ambient in the future.
Piano lessons from age 8 - 11. Drums from 12 - 16.
January 27th 2012 according to Audiotool.

I was writing lyrics and poetry around that time (January 2012) and decided I wanted to put something behind the lyrics, so I could find a singer and record it and see how it sounded. I searched for a free way to make music using the Google app store and found Audiotool. I struggled to get what I wanted from it but found the actual website and after listening to a few tracks from Astrum, Yas and - I think - Syntax I decided I would have more fun making electronic music. Previous exposure to electronic stuff - aside from anything made with synthesised drums or a synth here and there - would have been The Prodigy or Pendulum. Techno, Hardstyle, House (though later realised I had heard house and Old-Skool Garage before), Dubstep etc. were all very new to me. After a fair few terrible tracks made from horribly mashed samples, I started playing with the drum machines and synths, little bits of feedback from the likes of Jambam, Olondro, Shakey et. al and suddenly I started to improve.

Make your music, your way, but be willing to take on the advice given to you. That's important. I enjoy making music, I enjoy using Audiotool but lately I have dropped out of the community because it is just too intense and too childish. Chill out. This is a great tool and a great site filled with great people, so don't spoil it with "AT Drama" and enjoy the music.

To the staff - past, present and future: You guys are doing awesome things, all of you. So thank you.

ATU Podcast: http://www.podcastchart.com/podcasts/audiotool-underground or http://kh01.me/atu/

tracks.name: disco shark
tracks.desc: Boom. What can I say? Such a driving dance track. Great bassline and an awesome melody/percussive element. Vividly remember dancing to this one at both the AT:Con after party and the Tooltime party.

tracks.name: Inner Mask Disorder
tracks.desc: Schranz at its best. So many variations masterfully transitioned, how can anyone not be inspired to dabble in this genre? Must turn my hand to it again.

tracks.name: Blown (mind is gone)
tracks.desc: Couldn't tell you the genre, I just love the track. For me, though the bass is awesome, it's the vocals that make this a killer. Quite like the Hip-Hop influences in this too.

tracks.name: simply sound DUB
tracks.desc: Other than that sweet low bass, I really think the grime-vibe sounds in there are sweet. Really eerie track. I feel this is how melodic Dubstep should sound. Way cooler sound.

tracks.name: Survival
tracks.desc: This was the first CGMan track I opened up and subsequently copied the bass design. I have since gone on to use a set-up similar to these in pretty much all Neuro style tracks I do. I have now made a template track of the basic set-up for this...such a time-saver.

tracks.name: Yesterday
tracks.desc: Couldn't resist. In fairness, I do like almost anything this guy makes. This was the track he came back with after a break, best comeback ever. Nailed it.

tracks.name: Genera (Original Mix)
tracks.desc: Such a fun, up-beat track. Pretty cool, if simple, sound design. Great percussion work too. Listened to this kind of thing loads before finally turning to dnb. Never could make it though. We started a collab outside of AT a long time ago..I really should go back and work on it again.

tracks.name: Flake
tracks.desc: There is no one track that stands out from Olaf, I like his Garage, his tech and his Ambient. But this one is something I find quite inspiring. The composition is sound, the sounds haunting and the journey is sublime. When thinking ambient, this is where I turn.

tracks.name: The Eyes
tracks.desc: Nothing about this I don't like. Maybe one day I will remix it and turn it darker. Maybe.

tracks.name: miss with me RMX (WIP)
tracks.desc: I always liked Tott's (and Oedi's) darker Drum and Bass stuff. This remix is lighter than his usual Drum and Bass, but dark and filthy. I could probably attribute my first inclination to produce Drum and Bass to him and Oedi, with a nod to Jonjon too. I hope he comes back to Drum and Bass one day.

tracks.name: Sword Of Angels
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. This guy is seriously unknown, still learning but his ideas are sound. Recently featured one of his tracks on the ATU Podcast too, hopefully he gets the recognition he deserves. Truly funky tracks.

tracks.name: Babylon Shall Fall
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. Usually my latest is my greatest, at the moment that would be this one. So, like many people, I am not a fan of my own works. The things I am proudest of are generally things that are technically tricky.

real_name: Prinz Emil Wilhelm Otto Karl Von Schyren Von Bayern
intro: Mystical bombast coupled with contemporary Glitch and Dubstep elements - that's EMIL SHYREN our primeval rock. Hardly an Audiotooler can look back on such an eventful musical past as HSH Prinz Emil Wilhelm Otto Karl Von Schyren Von Bayern himself. So it's hardly surprising that his tracks resemble anything but the work of a novice: they are polished to the last detail and professional sounding. Bottoms up EMIL!
about:
Prinz Emil Wilhelm Otto Karl Von Schyren Von Bayern
Place: London
Age: A very mature 18
Profession: Multi Media Artist, Fellow of the Royal Society of Arts

interview.a: Glitched Orchestrals http://glitchedorchestrals.com/
Every kind of instrument that I could get hold of and experiment with.
Piano lessons at 6
In 2011, by accident on the Google App store, thought it was just some teen gimmick at first, how wrong I was.

Audiotool changed my life and my musical direction, every musician whether experienced or a newbie will benefit hugely by experimenting with it, and for the newcomer, the AT community will help support and guide your work, make you a lot of new friends and broaden your musical tastes.

tracks.name: time traveler
tracks.desc: A beautifully chilled track from another A.T. samplemeister, with a Bjork like haunting vocal.

tracks.name: R2-DTune
tracks.desc: Only a Mad Scientist can make music like this.

tracks.name: sunrays

tracks.name: That's What They Want You to Think
tracks.desc: Fab percs and cool vocals on this Top Toon, Feel da Beat.

tracks.name: A Place for Us
tracks.desc: Really just too cool for words... Just plug in and fly!

tracks.name: Little Box of Joy
tracks.desc: The Trancemeister at his best. Boogie on down to Frisco and Dance all night to this California Boy.

tracks.name: The eclipse....
tracks.desc: Dark Ambient Brooding Atmosfear... Run!

tracks.name: Engineer
tracks.desc: Glitch-hop at its very best by the Master Engineer himself.

tracks.name: Morning Walk on the Moon.
tracks.desc: Freaky mix of sounds on this Acid Psy-Fi soundtrack.

tracks.name: Tsunami
tracks.desc: One of the most exciting tracks on A.T ever. Just sends me crazy Man... Love it.

tracks.name: Clara's Eyes
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. This Glitched Orchestral of mine tipyfies my style and uses samples from my StonehengeS tone Guitar. Artist recommendation: DONATELLO - his use of samples reminds me of me when I started here on AT. His tracks would work well in a gaming environment, lots of potential, knows how to compose.

intro: Christian Chrom definitely belongs to the Techno elite of the website. His tracks are without exception authentic, driving and produced straight to the point. That this does not necessarily require samples is proved by some of his setups which do without them completely without giving you the feeling that something is missing. Christian Chrom doesn't only sound like a big name, for us he is one!
about:
Christian Biedermann
Place: Austria near the Italian border
Age: 35
Job: At the moment I am not working but I have mostly jobbed in kitchens cause I like cooking a lot.

interview.a: Hmm hard question but its Electronic music :) Influenced by Techno, Acid and Trance and sometimes I just do experimental stuff.
I have no real musical background but I have been interested in synths and djing since my youth.

I started making music with Magix Music Studios when I was 16 but gave it up because I was more interested in djing at that time. Later I came back and worked with Rebirth-Reason 1-5, FL, Ableton and hardware synths/sequencers and found my real passion.

It's about 1 1/2 years ago I searched the net for a Tenorion software emulator and I found the Tonematrix. It was not exactly what I was looking for but I gave it a try and while playing I saw a link to Audiotool and guess what :D I was hooked and started to explore AT and really liked the workflow and the simplicity of the DAW. After playing on AT for a while I realized that I was able to publish my work directly on Audiotool. It was mind-blowing that it became so easy to get my tracks public and get feedback on them from people who also make music. The moment I realized all this was one of the best moments in my time making music.

My life was an up and down like a sinewave and I did many things I regret but music always kept me on course. Don't force yourself to make tracks or aim at a genre. Try to make music out of your emotions and people will feel that the track is real. Enjoy what you do and have fun in your life.

tracks.name: Jingle Bells - Synth Barbershop
tracks.desc: All is possible even singing synths :) The first time I listened to this track I couldn't believe it and I was sitting at my pc with my mouth wide open. The skills of Frigolito are unmatched and this track is by far the best track on AT in my opinion.

tracks.name: Lonesome
tracks.desc: ONE 303 and an absurd amount of fx was needed to make this beautiful track. I spend a few hours in the draft but still haven't a clue how this is possible :) For sure one of AT's best tracks. I am sure many people are still waiting for another track from Someone.

tracks.name: smoked
tracks.desc: Tracks 1 and 2 were not influenced by my personal music taste so you could basically count this as nr1. I really like Drumcode rec. a lot and this track could easily be on that label. Olondro, in my opinion you are a Scandinavian, at least in your music style.

tracks.name: GRIK6:)!
tracks.desc: N1m8:)! Awesome track yafee? we want more!!!

tracks.name: Psychose
tracks.desc: Real dark and mysterious track and you should definitely check SnaKy out.

tracks.name: Underbred
tracks.desc: Known for his real good Neuro-Drum and Bass skills E-trim also has also made a few Techno tracks and I really like what he does.

tracks.name: Synthetik
tracks.desc: A perfect example that not much stuff is needed to make a real good track. Percs are on spot and I really like the deep and dark atmosphere. great one in5omniac.

tracks.name: The God Particle ( Christian-Chrom and The Seahorse )
tracks.desc: Shame on me to put in a track I worked on but it's still a favorite and stylewise something new. I am really proud of this track and The Seahorse did such a good job on it.

tracks.name: Future Cities
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. In my opinion Ayearty really deserves more attention because he is amazing. Great synth-design and atmosphere in his tracks. Check him out!

tracks.name: dark rite
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. I selected this track as my favorite of my own. There are a few others that I like but this one is the closest to my personal music taste.

intro: AUDIOARCHITECT belongs to the big, colorful family of first hour Audiotool artists. With his mixture of experimental Ambient and Trance influenced Techno tracks he delivered high quality again and again. Meanwhile it has become a bit quieter around the architect. No reason for us to lose sight of him: From time to time he looks in on us and leaves a musical reminder.

Martijn Jocker
Place: Belgium
Age: 43
Job: Sales manager

interview.a: Techno, Experimental, Electronic
Drummer in a band
When I was three with knives and forks on pans
More than three years ago, just found it when I was searching for this type of site

Audiotool is genius, I have been a happy user for more than 3 years, remember the good old times, with Tottenhauser, cript, jambam, yaffee?, oedipax, WG ...

Nights and nights I was chatting under tracks like in a chat-room, making great music-friends and making great music!! thnx!

tracks.name: yemen

tracks.name: damage
tracks.desc: Tornsage, very very good music from this great man. Very beautifull music. A must listen!

tracks.name: ecole
tracks.desc: Mr.Standfast, don't know him that well but love his music. Keep it up bro!

intro: Fresh and unspoiled QVIN who appeared on the Audiotool scene only a few months ago, presents us with a good mixture of contemporary styles. We are glad that the flow of new talents seems unending and that Audiotool can contribute to the development of young artists. And so we are proud to congratulate QVIN on his appearance on the online radio station 'Pandora'.
about:
Quinn Lemon
Place: New Orleans, Louisiana
Age: 19
Job/School: I go to the University of New Orleans and am studying education to become a teacher.

interview.a: If I had to use one word, I would say Trap and anything under it. I love the drum patterns of trap and dance-ability of it, so it influences a lot of my work.
I taught myself how to play piano, and taught myself music theory. And am now finally going to actual classes in college to help me even more.

I started making music 6 years ago, when Eminem's "Recovery" came out. I decided that I wanted to rap, too. So I began making awful beats for about 4 years. Then I heard "Blue" by Au5 & Fractal and fell in love. So I began to teach myself music theory and how to play the piano; I watched countless hours of videos on youtube. Then I looked up free producing software. And began to look at Audiotool, then I watched many hours of videos on how to use Audiotool. And then, 6 months ago, I released my first electronic song.
I discovered Audiotool about a year ago, by searching up free production software on Google.
I just want to say thank you to all those who have listened to my music and continue to listen to my music. Yall's support means the world to me. And I hope each and every one of you makes it big.

tracks.name: 1K [X___________X]
tracks.desc: Trap is my favorite genre, and I love vocal chopping. When I heard this, I lost my mind. I do drop this when doing sets at parties and the crowd loves it, too. By far one of the best trap tracks on Audiotool.

tracks.name: The Awakening
tracks.desc: One of the few Melbourne bounce songs I've heard on Audiotool, and one of the best. Very well produced and the lead synth was on point. The whole time I couldn't stop myself from bobbing my head.

tracks.name: Audiotool Day 2015 - n0maD$
tracks.desc: This has to be one of the more interesting tracks that I've heard, but I love it. The rhythms he uses are very interesting and kept me intrigued. And His percussion work is A1.

tracks.name: Audiotool Day 2015 - MyNameIsChuckles
tracks.desc: A personal favorite of mine when I need something to chill out to. I've many times fallen asleep to this song. Very atmospheric and great percussion work, as well.

tracks.name: Tsunami
tracks.desc: The track just goes hard and I just love it. I usually play it when I do shows.

tracks.name: Cluster I
tracks.desc: Another track I usually relax to. I love that smooth, sliding synth and those space-esque chords. Again, great percussion work.

tracks.name: first_work (edit/extension)
tracks.desc: I really do not know what to say about this except the fact that I love it oh so much. The original is great, but I love all the additions that Dove added.

tracks.name: Aarons Rush
tracks.desc: Ok, this song really hits me emotionally. I was going through all of Darklite's tracks and stumbled upon this one. Though the mastering could be better, throughout the whole song I could feel the emotion that Darklite must've been feeling. Absolutely great piece.

tracks.name: You Found Me
tracks.desc: This song is my all time favorite song on Audiotool. It is in my music library and I listen to it nearly everyday. This song puts me in a state of mind that I can't explain, and brings me somewhere else. Amazing.

tracks.name: what if they take your head off and turn you into a cop for there dirty work?
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. I honestly think this guy should get more attention as he is really making tracks that can only be called his style. I have never seen any producer like him. His rhythms are very odd and his sampling is exactly the same: odd.

tracks.name: Yearning VIP
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. This song has just really been my proudest just because of the work I put into it. I loved the melody and progression from the very start. And I'm very proud of those growls.

intro: John Cavanaugh aka Approaching Human is not only a really nice guy and much appreciated member of the community. His music has grown over the years from darkish sound experiments to deep Ambient pieces with a touch of Trap and playful Dance tracks. If you don't know him already, go and give it a try.
about:
John Cavanaugh
Place: Cincinnati, Ohio, USA
Age: 22
Job/School: Double Major - Biological Sciences and Spanish

interview.a: Pent up and lost emotions channeled to sound while looking at the stars naked.
Piano lessons for 6 years from a sweet grandma. Beatboxing at age 12. Taught myself acoustic guitar when I was 16. Still can't read music well. Found Audiotool at 15 (Hobnox days!)
First song in my head came when I was 6. First recorded track: 15 (on AT…but deleted) First live song performance when 19.
My friend shared it with me… he found it on StumbleUpon and I thank him every time I see him!

You all saved my life with the things I was going through. As one of my closest friends committed suicide, another died in front of me and one passed away all within a couple years amongst other traumatic events I was depressed but I found support through the AT community. Not only was the music a therapeutic process, but being able to interact with the community was an amazing honor and therapeutic. Thank you.

The process of the music is what matters, not the final product. If the music helped you get through something, it was worth it. You don't have to be an amazing producer; you don't have to get the attention you've been hoping for. Do it because you believe in your creativity and thoughts and actions. And if you do that, success becomes extremely relative. To approach human is to approach God according to some philosophies. We are forever becoming ourselves by creating what's inside ourselves… and that is God-like.

Lastly, if you need anybody to listen to you because you're going through something I am here for any of you. And I mean it. Any time.

May the moonlight guide you and dusk grant you courage.

tracks.name: Look Up To The Heavens
tracks.desc: This song was very controversial when it came out when someone hacked ClickZ's account claimed that he had committed suicide. We were all distraught, but relieved it was not true. I am in love with the progression of this track and perc choices. Beautiful.

tracks.name: Arrival
tracks.desc: Was an honor to be the first person to favorite this track by an artist unknown to the community and then see it as a featured track a few days later. An amazing song that I keep going back to over and over again. And an AT classic.

tracks.name: All Of My Friends (Julsy Remix)
tracks.desc: Another guitar-heavy track, both Julsy and Sky Bound Zoo on the same track is not an uncommon thing, but this remix is so beautiful it keeps me coming back, percs are a fav!

tracks.name: Underwater Trees
tracks.desc: Probably my favorite AT track. Two powerhouses of skill and perfect sound design, Zone and Minotaur team up and make a track that makes me feel so inspired to make something half as good. This song reminds me of a car ride where you reflect on all the trials that you've gone through to get where you stand today.

tracks.name: Elm Street
tracks.desc: A cinematic and gorgeous work by Uprising who gives so much to his music, the transitions are beautiful and the atmosphere is so cultivating from the needle-like high hats to the plucks.

tracks.name: Tidal
tracks.desc: Tyburn has been the person who I've learned from the most. I am constantly opening his tracks to get insight on how to make sound with such clarity. Really, any one of his tracks would have made the cut. I love the delay effects and the synth from the beginning here. Such powerful sound.

tracks.name: Night Rain
tracks.desc: Kepz is a huge inspiration for all! Especially love the depth of this song and the very interesting percs. His sound design is tight as ever.

tracks.name: Cluster III feat. NXTXRE
tracks.desc: GraphBoii more commonly known as Zone is a top notch producer from his attention to detail for mastering, to the carefully crafted sounds. Always willing to change genre, I've been able to like whatever he makes. This track is specifically beautiful and unique.

tracks.name: Twilatoo - Je rave (the schedule remix)
tracks.desc: ... by The Schedule also known as Sandburgen (now Nock Nilte). I remember where I was when I listened to this track for the first time. An amazing track that blew me away from start to finish.

tracks.name: 3- EVERYDAY ANYONE [2013 mix]
tracks.desc: An AT classic. This track has constantly haunted me throughout the years the way the Legendary Pink Dot's Alchemical Playschool has haunted me. An amazing track with beautiful bass and contagious dark groove.

tracks.name: Beyond the Screen
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. ne.time, formerly Night Eyes and the8bitexperiment has been a constant innovator. Each track is cleverly crafted and there is always a noteworthy progression and statement in each piece. ne.time is a talented artist that truly writes for the love of his music.

tracks.name: Exit Stance
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. The original draft I had for this was a stupid-happy, dead-end, 4 chord turd of a track until I was experimenting with reverb and a percussion technique (overhead drum sounds). I created that pad and all the sudden, I knew I had to delete everything and go with the vision I saw in it.

intro: Mute's output rate is not the highest but everything he publishes has a special quality. In everyone of his cool Breakbeats and Dub tracks you can hear the experience he has collected over the years in this field of electronic music both as a producer and a consumer. Look by on him it's definitely worth it.
about:
Paul Linwood
Place: I have English roots but grew up in Switzerland. Living in a small city by the lake of Zurich.
Age: 28
Job/School: Environmental Engineering Student

interview.a: I don't think I keep to a specific style, but if I have to break it down to some keywords I'd say I make rhythmic Bass Music with an influence of Reggae/Dub and Breakbeat.

I took guitar lessons for 3 years when I was young but I never really was too enthusiastic about it. I still like to pick up the guitar now and then but don't have the ambition or skills to take it to a next level. When I was about 14 years old some mates and I got into Hip Hop. They used to freestyle Rap to my beatboxing. It was around then when I got interested in software music production. I tried to get the hang of different DAWs (FL, Logic) but never was very good and often lost motivation. At 17 I met a mate who was collecting old school Drum and Bass and Jungle vinyls. He had a music room we used to hang out in and jam all night long. About 4 years ago I discovered Dubstep when it was at the beginning of its hype. I quickly fell in love with the deeper and dubbier side of it and started to collect vinyl too. A couple of weeks ago I only just had my first DJ club performance, which was quite exciting.

About two to three years ago a mate, who was also hanging out in the music room told me to check out Audiotool. I really liked the fact that it isn't as complex as professional DAWs but still offers a huge amount of options to create sounds and effects. The possibility to open other users tracks and study them is still really inspirational to me today.

First of all, I'd like to thank the Audiotool development team for investing countless hours in building this great platform. As the suggestions page already says: continue being awesome! :)

I'd like to see people digging a little deeper in the depths of Audiotool, instead of only checking the front page and the most rated pieces. There's a lot of people who are rather reserved and don't get the attention their music deserves.

Otherwise keep the vibes positive and keep on doing what inspires you and you love to do. A path will open up finally.

tracks.name: sideline
tracks.desc: Tornsage was one of the first producers on Audiotool I stumbled upon. His deep and melancholic style instantly caught me. On this track I especially enjoy the dubstepish deep bass and percussions.

tracks.name: Ex-Data
tracks.desc: Intracktion is another producer whose music comes from the depths of the heart. In particular, the tracks he sings over give me the shivers. This particular track shines because of its unique groove, the nice melodies and the perfect drum work.

tracks.name: Cembalo undead [Orchester der Struktur Remix]
tracks.desc: Structure/Sandburgens distinctive sound never gets boring. He manages to capture many different moods and still keep his unique style. This track is a weird mixture of melancholic strings with chirpy melodies. A truly enjoyable trip.

tracks.name: LION VERSUS SNAKE - ROCKSTEADY DUB REGGAE
tracks.desc: May I introduce Audiotools king of Dub music: docremix. I'm a sucker for reggae and dub music and this guy just kills it. Would love to hear his stuff on a proper sound system.

tracks.name: Slapstick Speakeasy
tracks.desc: Sota creates wonderfully grooving Bass Music. His percussion work is some of the most outstanding I've heard on Audiotool.

tracks.name: Reincarnation - Yerevan (Zend Dubstep Remix)
tracks.desc: This track by Triquend isn't the style of dubstep I'm usually into. The beautiful original track and really well designed synths and the overall progression of this piece made it stick in my head. One I've been coming back to frequently.

tracks.name: Friday afternoon jam feat Christian Chrom
tracks.desc: Two magnificent producers packed into one track here. Christian Chrom who masters experimental Audiotool-only production and SharkYyo with his psychedelic and progressive style. I really enjoy the euphoric melody and the raw bass on this one.

tracks.name: Rägeboge!
tracks.desc: Pathfinder packs tons of energy in his tracks. On top of that he manages to produce a really professional sound. This is one of my faves of his because it's the only track on Audiotool I know of with Swiss German vocals ;)

tracks.name: Chalk
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. Ed Knowles is a very versatile producer. I especially like his breakbeat pieces like this one. He's way too unknown considering the quality of his compositions so go and check him out!

tracks.name: Dubby Shizzle (feat. KernelPanic)
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. Digging up an old one here. This is a special one to me because I worked on it with my mate who showed me Audiotool. It's rather slow progressing. I like the spacey sound, the piano like chords and the drum beat on this one.

intro: arche3.0 must be one of the most unconventional Artists in the Audiotool community. His eruptive fusion of Retro Electro, Jazz and also elements of contemporary music like Drum and Bass and Electro Pop is without competition on Audiotool and can be attributed to him within a fraction of a second. At the moment things have quietened down around him a bit but that's no excuse for you not to give this explosive mixture a try.
about:
Joshua Kasper
Place: Rhode Island, USA
Age: 42
Job: Manager, Clinical Research & Decision Support

interview.a: My main style is retro electronic music. I look to find nostalgia in my music and touch on earlier styles of electronic music of the 80's and 90's. I also often dabble in jazzy harmonies.

I started playing piano in high school at around 16. I took lessons for about a year and a half from a jazz musician. He taught me how to read and understand music theory, so that when I was no longer taking lessons I could read and teach myself a lot about how music worked. This is where I got my understanding of jazz theory. To make the most of my knowledge I needed a site like Audiotool where my physical performance limitations were not a problem and I could concentrate on composition, voicing and sound design. I do miss playing my old Czerny exercises though.

I started making compositions of music when I started on Audiotool. Prior to that I mostly just played around with sounds and played other people's music. Audiotool gave me the ability to think beyond one instrument and sound and allowed me to learn more about composition and flow of a track. I learned a lot from listening to fellow Audiotool artists as well as listening to music of the 80's and other Nu Disco and retro artists.

I discovered Audiotool around the end of the summer in 2010. I was looking for a place that would allow me to make music electronically because I wasn't able to really play on my keyboard much. I did a search online for places to make music and Audiotool came up. After listening to the artists for a while, I gained enough confidence to try the instruments for myself. It took some time to get used to synthesizers for me, but with perseverance I found a sound that resonated with me and I think others in the community.

Audiotool is a blessing for me as it allowed me to do what I've always wanted to do as a kid. I've even put out albums, which was a dream of mine. I don't care about popularity, but want to find like minded listeners. So for others I say, don't make music for everyone else to like, but make music for yourself. Others with like minds will find you and support you as you find your own sound. Audiotool has a community that is encouraging and helpful and a great place to find yourself.

tracks.name: Chill n' Bass [Full Track]
tracks.desc: Infyuthsion has always had a high quality of tracks and this one was one of my first listens to his music.

tracks.name: Count Floyd - U.F.O.
tracks.desc: An old track, but good and used all samples.

tracks.name: My Radio Is Jammin' On
tracks.desc: One of my favorites on the site. Just a jammin' track!

tracks.name: Illuminated Darkness
tracks.desc: 808Chunk has been a huge influence to me on the site and I try to learn as much as I can from his music.

tracks.name: Techno-Ravelation
tracks.desc: A track from 808Chunk's alternate identity :)

tracks.name: 7/4 DnB
tracks.desc: Another oldie, but well done track.

tracks.name: allday long dub
tracks.desc: This is the classic Noraus track that helped teach a lot of us about effects and sound quality.

tracks.name: Parlez-vous Francais
tracks.desc: A great filtered track by Joa. A classic!

tracks.name: Massive Progressive (E-trim Remix)
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. E-trim is an artist I like and think should be exploding on the scene now. He puts out quality and makes some massive tracks.

tracks.name: Demonstration
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. This is a track I'm proud of for working with multiple 303 patterns in a fun way. I spent hours lining up those patterns to the point where it became a labor of love. I love the 303's timeless sound.

intro: ABADDON aka Lost Protocol is one of the few advocates of the harder, classic Dutch School – if not the only one. With his pounding Hardcore monsters he soon stood out among the tame mass of Audiotool users, who – at least where the music is concerned – tend to be addicted to harmony. That doesn't mean there are no sensitive tracks among his work. We are glad to see that he has survived his baby break and is back to provide us with the requisite dose of otitis.
about:
Niels Bos
Place: Almere, Netherlands
Age: 35
Job: Head of the Spare Parts department at Mitsubishi. We deliver engine parts for the shipping industry.

interview.a: I don't think this needs a lot of explanation. My style is the harder style of electronic music, mainly Hardstyle and Hardcore but I don't really like the new Hardstyle so what I produce is best described as early Hardcore. I like dark and deep sounds.

When I was a young boy a friend of mine bought some turntables and this is where it all started. Soon I bought my own and was playing the decks the whole time. I played at some illegal raves and also in a club every Friday night. Producing music is something I have learned by just doing stuff and seeing what happens. I'm a vinyl junkie and love mixing.
When I was 15 years old I started mixing and playing the turntables. I realised that I was pretty good at it and slowly started to make some music with a friend.

When my wife got pregnant I took a long holiday and I found Audiotool (best website in the world). That's about 6 years ago. I have been away for almost a year but now the kids are a bit bigger and I have more time again to do what I love.

When I started on Audiotool the community was not as huge as it is now but it was a place of respect and helping each other with all kind of things.

Don't let anyone tell you what to do. Making music is letting the emotional energy out. From the heart and mind, so really make what you like and don't try to please others who are trying to get you down.

Keep real and stay yourself.

tracks.name: farewell tribute
tracks.desc: This is where I first encountered the big Tornsage. He has such a great gift. The emotions he can put into music are just awesome.

tracks.name: Farewell, Friends.
tracks.desc: This track really makes me smile. I love the sounds in it.

tracks.name: Footprints in technicolor
tracks.desc: Sbronz is so epic, he is one of the old users and I just love his enery. This track has everything.

tracks.name: Industrial Sabotage
tracks.desc: Cripta's music really gets me. I love the deep and dark feel he puts into his tracks.

tracks.name: Stanze
tracks.desc: This heavy work by CGMAN is so damned good, really like the big industrial sounds and the pounding kick.

tracks.name: Night Run
tracks.desc: I think if this were the wake up track for everyone in the world poeple would be a lot more relaxed. I would have a smile all day long.

tracks.name: Dirty Dan Pt .2 (Party Daze) FT. Oedipax & DJ Simplex
tracks.desc: I don't like Dubstep that much, but this track is so big. Oedipax joining this only made it bigger. Everyone on Audiotool should know this work by the master Uprising.

tracks.name: REtaliation
tracks.desc: Really love the sounds in this. Feels like flying in space with a broken space ship. This is truly an awesome piece of music.

tracks.name: Disinformation Of Overload
tracks.desc: Some Oldskool Hardcore from the chunkster, this so so well put together.

tracks.name: pure cocaine
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. This track worked out so nice, it's a simple but hard track. Hope you like it. Artist recommendation: vhh - I think VHH could be one of the great ones, but he has left Audiotool.

intro: If you ever wondered who's behind that cat with the noble physiognomy, here you have it. Almate's roots are unmistakably to be found in Classic and Jazz, but these and other elements are skillfully mixed with contemporary sounds, mainly Drum and Bass. It's another of those cases where it is a real joy to watch a dedicated young artist grow.
about:
André Matějka
Place: Gig Harbor, Washington, United States
Age: 18
Job/School: I am currently a senior in high school. I'm planning on going to a community college after I graduate this year. I hope to later become a music composer or a music teacher for a living, something to do with music in general.

interview.a: I don't belong to a specific style. I really enjoy making genres of all kinds, although I am most known for Drum and Bass. I just like to go with the flow or how I feel. I am a jazz, classical, and contemporary musician when I'm not working on electronic music. I'm addicted to making music with harmonies and complex chord progressions.

It's hard to say when my musical background started because music has always been part of my life. When I was little, I used to play with my parent's keyboards for fun. I didn't really get too involved with music until I started teaching myself how to play piano at the age of 10 or 11. I took my recorder music from my 5th grade music class and used the screen from one of the keyboards at my house to help me learn what each note of the music was; I also learned music from The Legend of Zelda series as a way to help me learn more about music. Learning piano sparked curiosity in me so I decided to start playing trumpet at school in the beginning of middle school. This led up to my obsession with music, which caused me to develop perfect pitch and to learn a total of 10 instruments.
I first started making music during my 8th grade year when I composed a piece for a band. It didn't go well.

One August afternoon about 4 years ago, I was bored so I searched in the Google Chrome web store 'free music studio' and discovered Audiotool. I fell in love with Audiotool once I started to get the hang of how it worked.

You guys are a great community and have shown me so many things I could never even imagine. I am so thankful to have known everyone that I have met up to this point. I am also thankful to become a better musician because of you guys.

Keep doing what you love whether it's making music or anything else you love doing, even if it's just a hobby. Your life will be so much better if you keep the things you love in life.

One last thing to remember: Music is communication in its most pure form.

tracks.name: nightlight (Notoz Remix)
tracks.desc: Not many people can produce at a caliber of this level. It feels good just to listen to this. I wish to be able to produce Drum and Bass like this someday.

tracks.name: Til you
tracks.desc: A collaboration of two people that I look up to and respect so much. These two are intelligent producers who can work well together. I want to have a career where I can work well with people like these guys.

tracks.name: remnant
tracks.desc: There is so much in this track that makes it unique. The chord progression and melody complement each other in ways that I can't describe. The percussion is laid back and simple, yet has a drive to it that makes you want to keep listening.

tracks.name: Distorted Memories
tracks.desc: From a longtime Audiotool friend of mine. This track demonstrates part of how far along he's come and how talented he is. The sound design is diverse as well as each part of the track being important.

tracks.name: Synthesizer Symphony 1st Mov.
tracks.desc: A track I discovered when I first joined Audiotool. It reflects a little bit of me in it since I have a classical background as well as having electronic influences.

tracks.name: Lonesome
tracks.desc: The reason why this track inspires me so much is that the source of every sound comes from one 303, which makes me believe that anything is possible when it comes to producing music. It feels like I'm listening to a story when listening it.

tracks.name: Life
tracks.desc: An amazing track from a favorite Audiotool artist of mine. Neurofunk is one of my favorite genres because of CGMan exposing me to it. This is a great example of Audiotool's potential in Neurofunk.

tracks.name: Panopticon
tracks.desc: One of the tracks that caused me to develop my love for Drum and Bass. It reflects a lot about the DnB scene in Seattle, Washington.

tracks.name: Black
tracks.desc: A simple, yet complex track. This track reminds me that less is more in a lot of cases of music.

tracks.name: [Title]
tracks.desc: From a good friend of mine who inspires me to keep producing music and to do my best to become a better producer. I get reminded of all of the friends I've made through Audiotool each time I listen to this track.

tracks.name: I want you
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. Crazy about you has a style unlike most Audiotoolers. He can find ways to rework music clips and add them into his own way of music as well as composing music together well. There is no clear way to define his music besides saying it is thoughtful.

tracks.name: Liquid Emotion
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. I made this track during one of the happiest times of my life. I felt so free when creating this track. I still feel free every time I listen to it.

intro: The Name Regret stands for contemplative, atmospheric Downbeat and Drum and Bass tracks. With his previous alter Egos … and Mourn he had already gained the reputation of an insider tip that was to be taken seriously. And it's probably due to the changing identities that his work has remained more or less in the underground, lending an enigmatic touch to the whole matter. It's about time to check out this talented young artist called Regret.
about:
David
Place: Baltimore
Age: 20
Profession: College-enrolled slacker at University of Maryland

interview.a: Tracks with big feelings and tracks to chill out to. Also tracks to do psychedelics to (can I say this?) [Editor's note: If you mean lava lamps, yes]

Love Funk music and Jazz.... Favorite is Herbie Hancock. As far as modern music goes: Boards of Canada are gods; Bon Iver is very nice to listen to; Oneohtrix Point Never made an album recently I like a lot. Also Death Grips.
I took to piano as a kid and used it as a place to express my emotions throughout high school.

I found the old Hobnox site on my parent's PC 5+ years ago and messed around with the 303 for a while.... Stumbled across the site again a few years later when I was 17 and have been using it ever since.

Make whatever you want and don't try for favs. You need to make the music you want to hear the most... There's a genre or sound or idea out there that only you know about and it's your job to show the world. Everything else is just secondary.

tracks.name: woes
tracks.desc: This track was already featured in amoeba's album but I had to put it here because a few years ago my dad was in the hospital with clots in his leg, and seeing someone I'd always thought was so strong weakened like this made me feel a certain way. It was raining and my mom and I were waiting for news from the hospital and I listened to this track, and it encapsulated how I felt.

tracks.name: 火花
tracks.desc: This track reminds me of the song playing on the welcome screen on TV at a hotel room, and I really like the way the guitar is chopped in it.

tracks.name: unrest

tracks.name: neotokyodub

tracks.desc: Where to start with future sound collabs.... These are the artists I've looked up to since I found the site and they've all had profound effects on the music I make. Those vocals with that beat and piano, it's so simple and so nice. Too chill.

tracks.name: Monochroma
tracks.desc: More VLTRA... why not. This has been front and center on my ipod for a while because of that bass and that perc, it's all so good. The quintessential VLTRA sounds. Amazing work.

tracks.name: soar
tracks.desc: My fav track by tornsage, one of the first users on here to notice my profile and one of the friendliest people on the site. Those drums at 45 seconds are amazing and remind me of a lot of music I enjoyed in the early 2000's when I was growing up.

tracks.name: innerlude
tracks.desc: So beautiful and really optimistic in a way I like a lot. Amoeba is an artist who has had a profound effect on me with his sick tracks.

tracks.name: M V M M O N
tracks.desc: This is so heavy and that saw synth is too good. Blew my head off when I first heard it. Nominal is a god; EVERYDAY ANYONE is the best track on the site but everyone already knows that one so I want to spread this oldie.

I discovered Audiotool in late 2011/early 2012 after taking a music class at school that introduced me to using software to make music. After doing some browsing I came upon Audiotool through the Google app store and I've been hooked ever since.
Keep working at it. Keep looking for ways to improve your work and ask for feedback.

intro: With his 300 tracks in less than three years, Bit-Tron is one of our most prolific producers. His passion for Hip-Hop is evident throughout his work, but doesn't restrict its variety. He leaves the design of his track and his Album covers neither to chance nor to others – another good reason for taking a look at this Audiotool Allrounder.

Roughly 2 years ago
I searched the web for 'free music maker' I saw Soundation, used it, hated it. Then I found Audiotool and fell in love haha.

The Audiotool community is absolutely amazing, I've met so many amazing people and made many friends there. Very supportive and friendly. There is such diversity with artists making all sorts of genres, a lot of really good music! :)

intro: To nail SuperN.O.V.A. down to one style would be sacrilege. Around spanking rhythmic constructions he weaves atmospheric melodies and sound collages as if - at the tender age of seventeen- he had never done anything else. Currently he is apparently concentrating on products outside Audiotool but looks in now and then, leaving behind audible proof of his existence.
about:
Tyler D. Clark
Age: 17 but will be 18 this November.
Job/School: I am currently a senior at Pinkerton Academy but my main goal is to go to college for audio engineering and recording. Some schools that I am looking at is my first pick Full Sail University in Winterpark Florida, Emerson college, Berklee, Umass Lowell, and some community colleges.

interview.a: Unique is the best way to put it. However I think space and futuristic is the best way to describe my music.

I started making music about 6 years ago. I started on a website called soundation.com and joined on November 13th, 2010. I also self taught myself how to master, mix, and create music before I learned any instruments or theory. The first instrument I learned to play was percussion and drums and also I was part of chorus. Then I learned piano and have taken two Music Theory classes at school to further enhance my ability to create music.

I discovered Audiotool about 4 years ago at the beginning of my 8th grade year. I discovered Audiotool after I was bored with soundation and when I realized how bad my music sounded. I found audiotool by googling websites like Soundation and it took a while but I fell in love with Audiotool. Now I use programs like Fruity Loops and Audacity and have started to move more away from audiotool.com. If anyone wants to listen to any of my new songs follow me on soundcloud!

First I would love to thank the Audiotool staff for featuring me after all these years. I would also like to thank all my fans or even just casual listeners to my music. Lastly audiotool is a large community full of passion and sometimes it will be hard to find motivation or sometimes you are not getting the support from others like you used too. Don't give up, become unique and be willing to take risks like new genres or maybe trying to create a new preset. Be open too learning and criticism. More importantly be inspired by everyone and let the people come to you not the other way around. Again, thank you everyone.

tracks.name: lost found lost again
tracks.desc: Also known as Jace or top hat he has taught me so many things and I still look up to him and his music.

tracks.name: Snowfall (w/ Potasmic)

tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. My artist recommendation is everyone and what I mean is it won't hurt to look through the community and give songs a listen that might have just been uploaded. I went through the community, spent minutes and found this gem Chrono Realm.
