tracks.desc: Favorite own track.

intro: archives is not only a talented up and coming young artist with an inclination for obscure and serious melodies and settings. With his weekly compilation series on Audiotool Hits he makes it possible for interested users to get an overview of the wide scope of Audiotool artists and music in the past, present and future. It can't be taken for granted that a young person like him shows such a constant interest in the work of others. The passion for things like this brings us full circle to his artist name: Our special thanks go to archives.
about:
Benjamin Verti (Ben)
Place: Los Angeles, California, USA, but I move around a lot because my dad is in the Navy. Currently in New London, Connecticut.
Age: 14
School: Fitch High School

interview.a: I don't know how I would describe my style. I just make what I like. Sometimes it's Future Bass, sometimes it's Trap, sometimes it's Witch House.
I don't have much of a musical background. I can play piano and trombone.

I joined my school band in 4th grade - playing trombone. I started making music on Audiotool in November 2014. It was on an account called BCP (it stands for Ben Christopher Productions). I would make only sample tracks for a while though, since I didn't understand how the synths worked and I didn't care that much. During the summer of 2015, I started to want my music to be better. I would join rabb.it groups with other people in AT and then I started making my own original tracks. That account is deleted now.
I discovered Audiotool in November 2014 when a friend was promoting his Audiotool account on Instagram.

I was curious what the charts were like 1 year ago, so I thought it would be a good idea to make albums with the charts and in the future, I could look back and see what songs were popular at the time. I made an account for it on September 19, 2015 (Editors Note: Happy first anniversary) and I also made 3 other albums along with the charts. They were Underrated, Chill, and Dance. Since then Chill and Dance have been ditched and replaced with New School and Throwback and Genre Edition has been added and replaced by Monthly Editions. I am very proud of what this account has grown to be.

interview.q: How do you find the songs
interview.a: To find the songs I put in the albums, I have people suggest artists, but mostly pressing 'music' in the top left-hand corner and looking at tracks from the last week.

interview.q: What has changed over the years
interview.a: There are a lot of things that have changed when I look back at the albums. One thing is popular artists, I see a lot of accounts that have been deleted, but mostly, I see what genres had been popular at the time. It's been Future Bass, then Melodic Dubstep, and right now, I would say it is Trap.
Make what you like. Have fun making tracks that get 5 likes instead of making tracks you don't like that get 50 likes.

tracks.name: quiet life
tracks.desc: Clu is probably my biggest musical inspiration, ever. When I joined Audiotool, I remember his 200th track was one of the popular songs at the time and I loved it. Quiet Life is my favorite. A great track to unwind to.

tracks.name: Last Minut35 - Down the street
tracks.desc: My favorite track from one of the nicest people I've met on here. I love the melody of the track at 1:43 and something about the song feels motivational. Overall it's just a great track. I definitely recommend it.

tracks.name: technicolor//mountains (S p o t s & oceanic)
tracks.desc: One of the two super underrated artists I featured, Oceanic is crazy talented and people really need to check him out. I love the simplicity of this track, but it still is so intriguing and beautiful.

tracks.name: Delta Phi
tracks.desc: Luxior is the gold of the Audiotool community. My favorite from him is Delta Phi. This song is so diverse and detailed. I love how the song changes directions (specifically at 1:58).

tracks.name: I still think about you all the time. (and it kills me)
tracks.desc: This song is so beautiful and expressive. It's one of the most emotional songs and you can feel the pain Lewi was going through when you listen to this. I love every five minutes and thirty seconds of this beautiful song.

tracks.name: SUMMER NIGHTS
tracks.desc: My summer jam! It can bring a smile to anyone's face. Tim has mastered Tropical House, and in my opinion this is his best. The vocal chops and piano are perfect. A great song to get up and dance!

tracks.name: Sexy Time with Lux and Kav
tracks.desc: Luxior and Kavil get sexy with this jazzy track! But, being serious this song is nice. The chords are so juicy and the drum patterns are amazing.

tracks.name: Evangeline the Oyster Girl
tracks.desc: This is not what you normally hear from The Seahorse. I love how it is not unhappy. My favorite part of this track is the percussion. You can tell how much work was put into this song.

tracks.name: You're No Fun
tracks.desc: A classic. I am not a fan of Dubstep, unless it's this song. SadfaCe hasn't been active recently, but I really wish he was. Anyways, the bass on this is so crazy, and it has a real catchy melody.

tracks.name: conveyor
tracks.desc: It's hard to describe Opaqity's music. All of it is so unique and beautiful! Conveyer stands out because it's more upbeat. I have this song downloaded and I listen to it every day.

tracks.name: Rough Waters
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. Radioactive Octopus is so underrated. He has taught me so much about Audiotool and is one of the reasons why I still produce. Rough Waters such a good song that people don't appreciate.

tracks.name: A C C I D E N T
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. This was my returning track after leaving Audiotool for a month. I like how it turned out. This is probably the track I've put the most time and work into.

intro: Clu - formerly known as clueless - is one of our high quality EDM producers. His compositions are always straight to the point and varied, falling into line with modern elektronic Music without ever becoming so mainstream that Oldschool purists couldn't enjoy it, too. Not without reason one of the Audiotool chartbreakers.
about:
Charlie Liou
Place: San Jose, California
Age: 19
School: California Polytechnic State University, San Luis Obispo

interview.a: Super fruity tracks that are chill or go hard
Piano until 5th grade, violin for 8 years, produced on Sunvox, Audiotool, FL and Ableton

I think I started junior year summer. I discovered an app called Sunvox and I tried remaking Follow You Down by Zedd on it. When I discovered AT (I actually stumbled on it 2 years before I started on Sunvox) I realized that AT was much better than Sunvox and I switched. I began to use FL at least one year ago, I don't remember.
I found it on Chrome Web Store like 4-5 years ago. Had no idea how to use it and quit. Rediscovered less than two years ago.

Yo shoutout to all the people on Audiotool for supporting me these past few years, I really appreciate it. I promise I will only post only AT related tracks from now. If you really want to listen to my music, just hit me up on soundcloud. Thanks for your time :)

tracks.name: the best day (opaque)
tracks.desc: This was the very first song I heard when I got on Audiotool. I was completely blown away by how clean the production is, especially because Audiotool was just a website. Little did I know.

tracks.name: Firefly (Ft. Luxior)
tracks.desc: This was the second song I heard after getting on Audiotool and I decided I had to start using the app at this point. At this point in time my music taste was basically Firefly styled tracks so yeah, shoutout Xavi for convincing me to use AT.

tracks.name: Already Here
tracks.desc: I love this song so much. It makes me very happy (especially because this is a one-of-a-kind track). Check out his other songs for some great Lo-Fi Hip-Hop.

tracks.name: [VHs.RiP.]
tracks.desc: The melty goodness of the static keeps me coming back for more of Dzool's music.

tracks.name: Jingle Bells - Synth Barbershop
tracks.desc: I can't believe someone took the time to find the right formants for a human voice. Truly the mad genius of Audiotool.

tracks.name: Taste of Night
tracks.desc: One of my favorite songs on AT. The vocals paired with the various beautiful synths bring together an image that you can only see when you experience it for yourself.

tracks.name: MUGEN
tracks.desc: The synths in this song are so beautiful. cuddlex's songs are and have been super unique and this is no exception.

tracks.name: Azure. (1K Remix Entry)
tracks.desc: I listened to this song for 1.5 hours straight today while I was working out. The trance vibe Synthonix managed to create is insanely hypnotic and the plucks are so yummy.

tracks.name: Still.
tracks.desc: Beautiful production. Kavil's songs really challenged me to learn good music theory and I really appreciate him for that.

tracks.name: mii & u
tracks.desc: Even though this is the last song, I have many more I wish I could share. Nav – oh wait, dove created an insanely vibey track with a great name to top it off. Can't you hear Chance the Rapper on it?

tracks.name: Pellter
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. Carlyon is so underrated. He/she/they has a very unique sound I haven't heard elsewhere. It's dark but it also has this moving energy that keeps me listening until the end of the song.

tracks.name: four hundred (remix competition)
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. That's because I was able to make it even when my computer couldn't handle it. Even though the production isn't that good, I learned a ton of skills through creating this song.

intro: Herusew is one of our new generation Audiotool hipsters. Contemporary EDM music is his metier as he demonstrates unmistakably with every tracks he publishes. We are in for something here, we'd better be prepared for it.
about:
Herluf Sewerin
Place: Denmark
Age: 14, I consider myself a very young artist.
School: I currently go to school at "Sølvgade Skole" In Copenhagen.

interview.a: Electronic, but sometimes I try my hand at Trap and other genres.
It started way back in 2011 when one of the teachers at our school introduced us to a program called Soundation, I had already been taking drumming lessons so I found that interesting.
The first time I really started making music - not only for my friends - was on Audiotool. That'd probably be around 2014-2015.
One day I couldn't find inspiration on Soundation, then I began googling for something new and more powerful. I found Audiotool and oh boy I have been on it almost every day since.
There are loads of artists that need recognition. If you see someone with a huge following it normally means they are good. If you see someone with a smaller following it doesn't mean their bad.

tracks.name: Flashbang
tracks.desc: Bluedude. I'm surprised myself why I chose this as number one. But the more I think about it, the more it seems like to be the right choice. This track is a (flash)banger. Keep in mind that the song was made 3 whole years ago.

tracks.name: Destiny
tracks.desc: Vulkron was pretty much one of the guys who got me into Audiotool. He became an inspiration and I learned a lot from him. I remember when this track came out and I shit bricks over how I had talked to the guy who made this kind of stuff. <3 Vulkron

tracks.name: Efflorescence [Melodic Dubstep]
tracks.desc: Zerod, basically just god if he came down to earth trying to make melodic Dubstep on Audiotool. He has successfully mastered sampling and flowing drums, combined with some of the best track compositions ever seen on Audiotool.

tracks.name: lose control
tracks.desc: Teq-oh wait no. He changed his name to ViNL and that name is awesome, but no matter the name. He will always be one of the best and most skillful artists on this website. He has some of the best drum skills on this website in my opinion.

tracks.name: ion. [electro]
tracks.desc: Synthonix. This guy basically made Complextro a thing on Audiotool. His chord progressions are really good, I have studied countless of his tracks to see if I could learn a thing or two.

tracks.name: Sol3r x StageFright - Storm Chasers
tracks.desc: Stagefright is really talented, even though this is one of his earlier works, it instantly gained a lot of love from me and the Audiotool community. The drop is so smooth.

tracks.name: Analog Tears
tracks.desc: Now its Xav's time to show us what he's got. The chords in Analog Tears are so filling and every time I hear the voice in the middle of the track I get goose bumps on the back neck. Simply amazing.

tracks.name: EARTHBOUND (Tim Derry)
tracks.desc: Tim Derry! Musical god. Or maybe even one of the most friendly guys on Audiotool. The guitars in this track are really warm and comfy.

tracks.name: sinister
tracks.desc: Of course Luxior had to be on here. Sinister is so amazingly produced it's actually scary. To this day I cannot find much that he did wrong on this.

tracks.name: Werbs - The Final Battle (ExPe Entry)
tracks.desc: I love this track! And I love the artist as well. Expe/Ethan has been a huge inspiration for me on Audiotool. #ExpeIsLove

tracks.name: Dither
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. This guy is so nice and so talented it's unbelievable. His chords are so filling and wonderful. I wish for this guy to surpass my following by a lot.

tracks.name: Straightaway (acloudyskye x Herlufsew)
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. The song got so many views and favorites it's unbelievable. It was my first time charting and I couldn't have chosen a better person to make the track with!

intro:
Tyburn - still known to just a few of us by the name of virtuousduck - is one of the formative minds of that aera. Hardly any of his tracks came to the public eye without showing anew what is possible with Audiotool. After the change of name and image some four years ago he devoted himself more to atmospheric Bass Music and then a short time later got together with an illustrious group of prominent colleagues to form Audiotool's first supergroup: "Future Sound Collabs".

Here again he was responsible for the deep dusty sound of that period, taking it to perfection with among others Vltra and Nominal. His track list is a piece of Audiotool history, but one that with all its musical pleasures will enthuse not only historians.
about:
Tom
Place: UK
Age: 23
Job: Astrophysics PhD student

interview.a: Less music and more noise.
Played guitar for a bit while younger though I wasn't very good.

I played guitar in some awful bands with my friends from the age of 11 but I started using audiotool a few years on and that's when I started to actually try to make music rather than just badly playing covers of songs in bands.

A friend first showed me Audiotool between 6 and 7 years ago when he found it using the stumbleupon site. I can't remember exactly when that was, but it was impossible to publish tracks and the Pulverisateur didn't exist yet. I forgot about it for a few months (maybe a year) then found it again once the Pulverisateur had been added and I've been using it ever since.

tracks.name: wait
tracks.desc: Just sounds beautiful.

tracks.name: Nous
tracks.desc: Incredibly atmospheric, sounds like it could be the title theme to a 90's tv crime series. Enjoyyourshoes' music is definitely worth listening to if you haven't already.

tracks.name: Rue Jue - Into The Sky Blue (Kepz Remix)
tracks.desc: Incredible track, one of the best pieces of music I've heard this year, even outside of Audiotool. Plus I've listened to Kepz's music for the last 5-6 years and the progression from his earliest stuff is crazy.

tracks.name: not so badman
tracks.desc: I think VLTRA makes the trendiest music on Audiotool, this is my favorite by him.

tracks.name: The Insight (remasters edit)
tracks.desc: Made by ascent, this track is incredible. I picked the version from the remasters account though for the better mixdown.

tracks.name: die form
tracks.desc: ascona400 hasn't made anything in 4 years but this track by him(/her?) is still relevant now. No percussion needed, just a kick and a snare with a weighty bassline.

tracks.name: Lenore (dabrig simple steal mix)
tracks.desc: Dabrig is one of my favorite AT users, his music always sounds so unique to him. Sndbrgn's original is brilliant and definitely worth a listen but Dabrig builds on it and makes it even better.

tracks.name: Nec Plus Ultra
tracks.desc: This track is 5 years old and way ahead of its time in terms of sound quality and production quality compared to what myself and most others on audiotool could do at that point. This was a big inspiration for me.

tracks.name: Reflection
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. Won Ton's music always had excellent sound quality and great melodies, I think he's probably stopped using AT now though. There are some excellent tracks on his page.

tracks.name: Vex
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. With this track I put no thought into what genre or style it might be but played around with it. Plus, all the sounds I used were really simple to make or record or sample and it comes together really nicely in my opinion.

intro: Carlyon's outstanding skills are another proof of Audiotool as a talent factory. His tracks aren't only spot on the zeitgeist they are also original, incredibly clean and full of pressure. Initially dry and pithy Four to the floor tracks - after five years on Audiotool - turned into complex but danceable Neo Pop bangers which don't need to baulk comparisons with the new sound of Astrum. It's about time to upgrade his status as an insider tip.
about:
Ian Switzer-Lambrick
Place: Colorado, U.S.
Age: 22
Job: I'm currently a full time student at University of Denver

interview.a: I would say my style mostly resides in the moody electronica region.
I am not classically trained but have a pretty good ear for what sounds good.
I started making music when I was a kid but not semi-legitimately until about 5 years ago.
I think I found Audiotool through the chrome app store
I would say stay true to your creative instincts, don't think about things too hard, and of course, keep on making music and love doing it.

tracks.name: doom station
tracks.desc: One of the first and most influential tracks I listened to on Audiotool. femme_robot did a great job of creating dynamic, driving and dark rhythms that were simple but effective. A lot of my early pieces were inspired by this track.

tracks.name: Tides
tracks.desc: When I first heard an Olaf track I realized just how diverse the music in this community could be. This track in particular really gripped me with it's dark atmosphere and style. Olaf's work was also huge in helping me define my own style early on.

tracks.name: anyone there
tracks.desc: Truly an amazing piece of music. Opaq is a real pro in my book; all the sounds in this track are crisp and highly polished. The attention to detail is a bit ridiculous at points! The driving dark basses and undulating pads turn me into jelly man.

tracks.name: Everything
tracks.desc: Another highly polished work by ΛΛRON and a great example of combining the workflow of AT with external inputs. The guitar in this piece immediately grabbed my attention as something different, and the chopped vocals at the end always turn me into a sloppy mess.

tracks.name: Lost
tracks.desc: Lost is a tour de force. This track pushed me to realize that even the simplest tools when used the right way can yield incredible results. The far off synths and melodies that Kepz created are mind-blowing in their execution and complexity, and all made in AT no less.

tracks.name: sol solo
tracks.desc: A great track from the boss of synth patches. Noraus pulls off a similar hat trick to Kepz in crafting a piece that is compelling using the tools that AT provides. The layered synth stabs are super high quality and the bass and beat pull it all together. Also the arps get me in a certain kind of way.

tracks.name: Minced meat
tracks.desc: I love this track for so many reasons; low freq. melodies, retro percs and ARPS. While I was still making house-ish stuff, Sndbrgn was a consistently professional source for me to turn to for inspiration. Talk about consistent to his craft as well, his style is well known to many and has been that way since I started making music here.

tracks.name: tightrope
tracks.desc: Torn is my hero here on AT, no doubt. This track is filled to the brim with emotion and stands out like many of Torn's tracks as truly unique. Like Sndbrgn, Torn has always stayed true to form with his tracks and consistently stands out in a sometimes mono-saturated music scene here. Torn found me really early on and shared my (at that point) pretty shitty tracks around until I got my feet under me. Keep it real Tornsage!

tracks.name: Samantha
tracks.desc: When it comes to the contemporary stuff, dove is my go to. His tracks are clean, to-the-point, and relentlessly future. I really like this piece and many of dove's because they show that AT is dynamic in representing a wide variety of new and emerging genres.

tracks.name: sinister
tracks.desc: It's been really interesting to see some of the longer running artists here evolve over the years and lucs is a perfect example of that. His content is constantly changing while still maintaining a nugget of his own style. This song is super creative and the perfect track for me to be all hyphy and emo too haha. I do wanna be hardcore (sometimes).

tracks.name: Eloquence
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. Tocka published this track a month ago and its already one of my favorites. It has that undeniably future sound that I love and a level of polish I haven't seen in awhile. I'm really curious to see where their next track goes and how I can potentially learn from it! Keep it up!

tracks.name: lucid
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. I'm rarely completely happy with the tracks I make but lucid was a really fun experiment that went all the way to publication that I am happy with. The last 45 seconds of the track are my favorite haha.

intro: Mike is one of the few „oldtimers" who are still constantly active. In the early days he made himself a name with driving French House bangers only to turn under the new alias hvd. to the deep crackeling sound of the VLTRA context which he soon could put his own mark on. Our thanks for 250 largely awesome tracks at least as many constructive discussions. Respect Brah!
about:
Michael Lewis
Place: Dayton Ohio
Age: 22
Job: I am currently working, and my job takes up half my time, but I took some courses at Hinds community college.

interview.a: I have no real musical background beside being a self taught Audiotooler, but other than that none.
I started making music back in 2011(?), while I was in highschool, and I had looked up "free music work station" - and it directed me straight to Audiotool.com, and that was about 5 years ago.
Be an individual, and don't be cringy while doing it. :) - hvd 2016

tracks.name: overwrk
tracks.desc: This track always gets me back into juke, his style is whimsical and fun, the last part just takes me there all the time.

tracks.name: Cakedog - HD (Fairfaxxxxxxxx RMX)
tracks.desc: More FAIRFAAAXXXX, just cause he's the man, you know I've always had a thing for Cakedog's music, and seeing how Fairfax did this track EVEN more justice, makes me cream dude.

tracks.name: idk
tracks.desc: Even though this is a short mix of Lux's birthday track for Zerod I believe, I just like this one a bit better cause it's un-even and very lofi ish and I heard it first before the Original.

tracks.name: paper town
tracks.desc: Vltra been that nigga since Pampers I believe, and he's one of those artists that just put the oompfh in any thing, so much organic vibes and just takes you away with every track fam, not to mention the fact that he said I inspired the track.

tracks.name: basement$ (dirty cassette mix)
tracks.desc: This track from Vltra is a remix of my second account's track, but he just did it so much justice that it had to be on the list, that kick gets my blood flowing.

tracks.name: what is it
tracks.desc: Man this track from dove is just a masterpiece imo, I think it captivates how being alone on valentines day can really boost your creativity, who needs women when you got music like this eh?

tracks.name: death note
tracks.desc: dwn is just a boss at whatever he's making man, he can pretty much make whatever he wants imo, shout out to him for being extremely dope.

tracks.name: [No.Filter][SP404SX]
tracks.desc: Ahh, what can I say about Hybrid's music that hasn't been said about great sex yo, I feel like when he got that sp 404 his abilities went sky high, hybrid is the truth.

tracks.name: [as] FindTheGroove
tracks.desc: THIS IS FUCKIN' GODLY, and it should be longer because my homie OBVG IS THE GOAT. Note: hvd's original pick was an OBN track that was later deleted, so this was placed as replacement.

tracks.name: You'r Soul (a.b.remastered)
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation: VLTRA. Alright this is a track that I found very nice back when I first started some 4-5 years ago, and till this day I wanna replicate this, but can't. I dont know what ever happen to him, but he needs to come back.

tracks.name: piray.
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. Seeing as how I made this a long time ago, and I've used this sample by dzool many times. (shout out to him) I used to bump this all the time on my way to and back from work, and I just love how hazy it makes me feel.

intro:
What better time could there be than Christmas to celebrate an international group of musicians and their ability to cross borders to create something special together. With their fifteen tracks Untrust 三 is one of the most productive supergroups of the website. Their sound design and meanwhile their strong melodic compositions have been setting new standards for more than two years now. We can hardly wait to get to hear the results of the first Untrust 三 live collaboration in Audiotool Next.

Enjoy the Untrust 三 feature and of course the festive season
about:
Aron / Aaron / Nicklas / Liam
Names: Aron - Aron / ΛΛRON - Aaron / Zone - Nicklas / opaqity - Liam
Places: NL / GER / DK / GB

interview.a: Chillout, melodic, atmospheric

Originally Aron made the account '/djflashbang/' as a secret account to mess around with, but he decided to invite Aaron for a collaboration project along the way. Then on Steam we were like: why don't we add someone else to spice it up? After thinking about it, Nicklas (Zone) was the perfect one to add, and our styles mixed together really nicely. After our first EP, we decided we could use another spark, and since our melodic game wasn't too strong, Liam (opaqity), whose melodies are really good, was the ideal choice.

The creation of a track can take place in a number of steps:

Most of the time someone just creates/initializes a draft with a few basic things added.

The draft normally stays there for a good while, but at some point someone picks it up and the others join in on the fun.

We try to discuss what we like and dislike about the track and when everyone, somehow, has agreed on the concept and sound we do some final touch-ups (remove excess synths, samples, patterns etc.) and publish the track. We only have things in our songs that we all like, if one of us doesn't like something he can just delete it.
At the moment, since everyone can't work on the same track at the same time we simply keep each other updated on changes to drafts/sharing music etc. via a Facebook group chat.

interview.q: What makes your collab special (the chemistry between you)
interview.a: Each of us has his own unique style and signature features when it comes to music. Some of us are better at some things than others - but when we are all working together on something, then it doesn't really matter, since all of the things that make our own tracks unique, merge together into something completely new and interesting.

interview.q: What will change for your work in next (specially with the live collaboration)
interview.a: Hopefully the prospect of live collaboration will inspire us to work together and create a lot of new and awesome music. In our current situation, we very rarely have the motivation to start (never mind to complete) a track. Hopefully Next will provide new ways for us to experiment and create things together and start making music regularly.

It is important to try new things and by working in a team it is possible to discover new perspectives on creating music. Get out there and collaborate with other artists, share ideas and blend your styles. Who knows, you might also end up making something better than you expected and in the process make some new friends.

Most important of all, have fun!

tracks.name: Lucid
tracks.desc: Future Sound's incredibly deep brooding atmosphere is unbeatable.

tracks.name: Find a place right here
tracks.desc: The composition and sound of this track are so well-tailored. The soothing atmosphere invites deep thoughts.

tracks.name: D.M.
tracks.desc: This is what you call a banger. The pumping bass and hi-hats are amazing.

tracks.name: Space Clouds
tracks.desc: Perfect! This track is so lush and the atmosphere is on point. The name and cover art fit perfectly to the track.

tracks.name: abs x lux - peripherals
tracks.desc: This one is crazy. The two styles blend together so nicely - definitely an unforgettable track.

tracks.name: something will change
tracks.desc: A very peaceful and mesmerising little track.

tracks.name: glimpse [dove x abstract]
tracks.desc: Collab between dove and abstract.

tracks.name: Family
tracks.desc: Put simply: this is an Audiotool gem. The style is so refined and the work is intrinsic.

tracks.name: Paradise, Honest
tracks.track_artist: Future Sound Collabs, trrrrillionare, Tyburn, Nominal, Post Myth
tracks.desc: FSM never disappoint. The atmosphere and sound is on point.

tracks.name: Dad's tapes
tracks.track_artist: tophat, Limitless, OzWu, OXBLUD, [Khář¡]', Lewi Astro, swrlly
tracks.desc: A nice collection of chill beats. A good composition featuring well tailored sounds.

tracks.name: Nor
tracks.desc: Aaron's favorite. I love it, because it is so warm and contains so many different elements. The percs, the water and the key sound.... They work perfectly together. Perfect teamwork on this one.

tracks.name: Plane
tracks.desc: Aron's favorite. I love the flow and how everything work together in this track. Every sound has a purpose, and it felt really good when I came in at the end and added one small detail and the track suddenly felt finished to all of us!

tracks.name: A TURN
tracks.desc: opaqity's favorite. This was the first Untrust track I got to help out with, and it came out sounding really nice. Everyone did a great job here.

tracks.name: Visage
tracks.desc: Zone's favorite. All of our styles/sounds and different ideas really came together in this one. Personally I find the sound design, composition and mixing flawless.

intro:
In the three years of his presence on Audiotool, Roedrigo Del Canto has become a respected musician and popular member of the community. His tracks – most of them Deep House and Techno – are always produced on point and possess an authenticity which should make them a highlight in any Ibiza DJ's record case. We are glad to have on board such a friendly and talented representative of South America and say welcome to member number 5 of the legenday club 72.

Congratulations on being voted a winner in our Featured Artist election.
about:
Rodrigo Fernando Del Canto Chacana
Place: Valparaíso, Chile
Age: 44
Job: I am an electrical engineer and on the one hand I teach at Inacap University and on the other hand I am a consultant at Powelec.

interview.a: I do not have a style yet. I find myself at a stage of exploration and learning, but I would like to produce EDM, Deep-House, Electro, Psytrance.

When I was 15 I played guitar for the choir of the school church and at the same time I played bass in a Thrash Metal band and we covered Slayer and Metallica. But at the same time I listened to Devo, Yazzo, Bronsky Beat. I like Bach, Vivaldi, Depeche Mode, The Cure, Thievery Corporation, Gustavo Cerati and electronic music in general ... I really like everything.
I started making electronic music when I discovered Audiotool, I'm not sure but I think it was 2014.
I think it was 2014. I always wondered how they created those electronic sounds so captivating, and this uneasiness led me to investigate and search google. Then I came across Audiotool.
I want to send a message of encouragement to all those who are at an early stage of learning and urge them not to give up. The reward sometimes takes time, but finally it comes.

tracks.name: Love Away
tracks.desc: I love this track because it shows how good Audiotool works for producing EDM. The sound is very balanced and the melody very sticky.

tracks.name: N2009EDT
tracks.desc: I like this track because it has some elements of the classic Trance genre but goes so well with the modern EDM style. The sound is powerful and clean all in one.

tracks.name: Don't throw rainbows when you live in a glass house
tracks.desc: This is one of my all time favorites. The fluidity of progression is absolutely masterful and the patterns are difficult to decipher for the brain so that it keeps your attention through the whole track. Moreover the melodies are really beautiful.

tracks.name: Duty is as Heavy as a Mountain, Death is as Light as a Feather
tracks.desc: This is one of the most epic works I have heard on AT. It is like the soundtrack of a movie, it makes you imagine scenes, and it is produced in a fantastic way. And in addition it was created by one of the nicest people I've met on AT.

tracks.name: Infinity [sonic3ze Remix]
tracks.desc: Here the artist takes the original melody and transforms it in a very creative way, handling very well the interplay of nuances and intensities. And the rap part: I love it.

tracks.name: Frostbite.
tracks.desc: This author is one of my favorites and for me he is an authority of the Electro - Complextro genre. I love the sounds of this track. I like how the harmonies have been created. Synthy currently has tracks which are much better produced - but I particularly love this one.

tracks.name: Sonic Boom
tracks.desc: The ability of this author is to decipher sounds and melodies is impressive. This track I love in particular because it has such a clear and powerful sound in the melodies which reminds me of the times when I played Street Fighter.

tracks.name: Audiotool Day 2015 -Pathfinder
tracks.desc: This track is an superb display of synthesizer mastery, and musical creativity. This author brings together the qualities of a virtuoso musician and a very good sound engineer. This track is just an example, but I am sure he can create it in any genre.

tracks.name: See You
tracks.desc: In general I love the aesthetics of the creations of Aura and in particular this track has enchanted me because it has a funky style which is very fresh and attractive.

tracks.name: Fest
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. This artist really impresses me more and more. He has a natural talent for understanding the patterns of different EDM styles perfectly. He is able to produce on a very high level, is very creative and the most impressive thing is, that he is only 13 years old. Wow man! Everyone who likes EDM will love it.

tracks.name: Zingara
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. This track is well accepted and somehow points out in which direction I want to go.

intro: The other winner of "the most important election of the year" and first Featured Artist in 2017 is a guy called Lewi Astro. His exciting mixture of Hip-Hop- or Trap beats and atmospheric melody constructions is realized masterfully and has earned him a great respect among the community, as proven by the rapidly increasing number of followers and fans. Go and check his music out - you definitely won't regret it.
about:
Mark-Lewis Ndikintum
Place: Born in Santa, Cameroon in the year 2000.
Age: 16

interview.a: It's really hard for me to define my style but I'll simply say: smooth sounds with nice percussions, that is usually. A more Hip Hop like R&B. My synths and chords are like borderline Ambient most of the time too. I love smooth flowing chord progressions.

I don't really have a musical background, I just had a love for music. I was never good at playing instruments as a kid, and I really haven't tried in my recent years, except for piano (which I'm not too good at, either)

I started making music when I was 12, on a website called "Club-Create" or something like that (currently shut down, I don't know why). I didn't know what I was doing, I would just compile samples onto one another. At the time, I already knew about Audiotool from my older brother. He used to make music here, and he's the one that showed me the website. I didn't get into it at first because it seemed too difficult. However, a couple of months later I was all in because I learned how to drag and drop samples onto the timeline lol. And they had WAY more samples than the site I was using prior. Give it another year and I was making my own drums and synths, and truly started composing my tracks.

My message to the very talented Audiotool community is to enjoy the music and the process of creation. Always stay open-minded no matter the genre you produce. Lastly produce what you feel. Don't conform to genre standards, if your mind says otherwise.

- Lewi Astro, AT Veteran | AT & FL Studio Producer.

tracks.desc: I love diversity! With this track, fwk put the perfect mix of Jazz elements with electronic synths. I just love the composition! The overall track makes me feel like I'm floating off somewhere really gloomy.

tracks.name: G u a v a
tracks.desc: This track is like being submerged in water and finding an underwater kingdom. I love the crisp percs, the dreamy pads, and the pretty arps that flow throughout the track! It really sets the tone, and gives it real personality. Shleepytime's production is A+ on this track, and while listening you can just tell he put so much thought, and effort into it.

tracks.name: MUGEN
tracks.desc: Sadly, at the time I heard this track, Cuddlex had already gone. Ignorant of who he even was, I was really moved by the track, and greatly inspired. As the track progresses I just get the feeling of a journey. I love the bass as it feels like it communicating and the way the lead flows through the track is so mesmerizing.

tracks.name: saturday
tracks.desc: Honestly, this is my favorite bump on Audiotool. Hands down like... Ultrasann has gems, man. However this one stood because of the way he chopped the sample to flow so smoothly, the way he incorporated his own bassline and even synths. I'm also a big fan of Lo-Fi and I just adore that filtered snare.

tracks.name: tropic of capricorn
tracks.desc: You may wonder why I chose this song out of every other celery/super deformed songs. It's simply because it stuck with me the most. The track though... at the time I heard it, things were slow. Time was just still. This song captured an emotional moment in my life, and for that I commend it. I still can't find words to describe it though,,, it's one of those tracks.

tracks.name: Audiotool Day 2015 - Luxior Entry
tracks.desc: Simply beautiful. That's all I can say. Luxior is a true AT master. The track is just so A-class. Out of every lux song, this one stuck with me because it's really the type of sound I aspire to capture in a lot of my songs, but Lux nailed it perfectly... Amazing once again man.

tracks.name: SURF DUDE
tracks.desc: I love everything about VLTRA. He is probably the most characterized user on the site, in my opinion. I chose this track over several of my favorites from him because of the feeling it gives off. It makes me feel really feel 'in space'. I also really adore the rhythm of the drums, the subtlety of the bass, and the consistency of the sample. it's not repetitive because of the variation in sound and percussive rhythm throughout the track.

tracks.name: Finding the words to say to you
tracks.desc: Lastly, this gem. I really wish I could find the words to say to you about your music, Joel (pun intended :^) ). Unfortunately, 'amazing' is a huge understatement.

tracks.name: V̶E̶N̶O̶M̶
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. If you enjoyed this list, I recommend checking out |fwk|. One of the first people I followed on here (if not the first.) Super talented individual(s). You should also listen to a lot of their works from 2 and 3 years ago.

tracks.name: マイティー
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. The words translate to 'mighty' in English. I named it that, because the main pad you hear throughout the song just brings a nostalgia feeling to me. I remember as a kid I'd always go to my basement and watch mighty morphin' power rangers on sunny Saturday mornings. I think the way the chords progress makes me think of the intro to the show.

intro: Flower, also known as Aura, is a rising star on the Audiotool EDM firmament. Without exception his tracks are produced on point, clean sounding but still energetic. His more chilled tracks are no less focused but they gain their power from their engagingly calm melodies and easygoing beats. We recommend this flower for the cold season.
about:
Shawn Baker
Age: 17

interview.a: I consider my style to be atmospheric or experimental. I am always looking to try out new genres and different forms of production while keeping my main focus on the aura the track gives off.
I am very acquainted with the piano and the saxophone. I play the piano regularly in my free time, and I enjoy sitting down on a warm evening to enjoy the sounds of my saxophone.
I began playing the piano when I was about 8 years old, and the saxophone when I was 13 years of age.
I discovered Audiotool around 2013, but was never really into it until late in 2014. A friend of mine showed me it at the time, and I haven't looked back since.

Keep progressing. I know in this day of Audiotool many artists are now beginning to focus on getting a huge amount of likes or plays, and are starting to lose focus on what producing music is all about. Producing is a way to express yourself, and it should be a way for you to set goals and standards for yourself. I cannot thank the artists who taught me that lesson enough because I have never been happier in my life than when I am sitting with my laptop looking at the Audiotool app.

tracks.name: Distant.
tracks.desc: This track has been the driving force behind many of my tracks. This masterpiece gives me incredible inspiration and energy to complete many of my own works. I have no idea where I would be as a producer if Kryptic wasn't so kind as to share this with the world.

tracks.name: Je T'aime
tracks.desc: The top three tracks were very hard to place on this list, as they all are magnificent. This track in particular is such an incredible work of art, provides an incredible amount of energy to the listener, and is by far one of the most professional tracks on Audiotool.

tracks.name: Focus
tracks.desc: I sometimes find atmospheres in tracks that take me on an adventure in my imagination that create a vivid and amazing experience that make me never want to open my eyes again. This track does just that every single time I listen to it. It is so powerful and uplifting. Mikke did such an amazing job with this one.

tracks.name: bike ride
tracks.desc: There are not many words that can be used to describe this track. This is a perfect example of true emotion transpired through musical capabilities, and no matter how many times this track is played you can find something new within it.

tracks.name: Liquid Emotion
tracks.desc: One of the first Drum and Bass tracks I heard when I first started on Audiotool. I was immediately mesmerized by the delectable textures within this track. This guy has become one of my biggest inspirations as a friend to continue progressing in music.

tracks.name: skydoki x dove
tracks.desc: I listen to this every single day on my morning commune. This track is stunning in every way. I really hope to see these two work together again soon.

tracks.name: Rhodes Moore
tracks.desc: Made by a great friend of mine, who has really helped me in more than just musical ways. This guy is not only an incredible person, but a wonderful producer as well. This track in particular combines simplicity with powerful melodies and beautiful chords.

tracks.name: Janus Wallflower
tracks.desc: This guy is absolutely brilliant! He has such an amazing talent with production. This track in particular completely floored me on how someone can combine so many elements and yet retain a beautiful simplicity to an atmospheric experience.

tracks.name: Globular Cluster
tracks.desc: One of the very first tracks I listened to when I arrived on Audiotool. To this day it still blows me away. I am always shocked by this guy's talent but I have no idea why, as this track speaks wonders of how far this guy will go.

tracks.name: Inner demons
tracks.desc: Artist recommendation. This guy is one of the best DnB producers I have listened to. I can easily compare him to many huge artists because of the way he can floor you with his amazing basses, clean cut mixing, and dark sounds. He has completely gained my respect as an artist, and will always be someone I can count on to put out brilliant tracks.

tracks.name: 100 (Flower Entry)
tracks.desc: Favorite own track. This track was the first time I pushed to set new standards for myself. I am honestly impressed on the way it turned out, as I never thought I'd be able to create something like it.
